structure.zh: 750年的阿斯图里亚斯政权规模较小，中央机构尚不分化，国王通过宫廷官员、亲族、伯爵和主教实施统治。许多行政与军事职责重叠，地方治理依托旧罗马—西哥特传统与山区习惯法并行。
structure.en: In 750, the Asturian polity was small and only lightly centralized. The king ruled through household officers, kinsmen, counts, and bishops. Many administrative and military responsibilities overlapped, and local governance combined late Roman-Visigothic traditions with mountain customary law.

function.zh: 管理国王近侍、文书、赏赐、使节接待与王室地产
function.en: Managed the king’s attendants, charters, patronage, diplomatic reception, and royal estates

function.zh: 组织王家亲军、召集贵族追随者、守备山口并主持对穆斯林边境地区的袭扰与撤民
function.en: Organized the royal warband, summoned noble followings, guarded mountain passes, and directed raids and population transfers along the Muslim frontier

function.zh: 协调主教区与修道院，见证契据，调解争端，并维持西哥特法传统的有限延续
function.en: Coordinated bishoprics and monasteries, witnessed charters, mediated disputes, and maintained a limited continuation of Visigothic legal traditions

function.zh: 征收实物贡赋、管理谷仓、牲畜与军需，并监督地方劳役
function.en: Collected payments in kind, managed granaries, livestock, and military supplies, and supervised local labor obligations

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依赖伯爵、地方贵族、村社首领和教会机构。山区聚落自治程度较高，王权在核心地带较强，在新近控制的加利西亚和杜罗河以北边区则更多通过军事首领与教会据点维持。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on counts, local nobles, village leaders, and ecclesiastical institutions. Mountain communities retained substantial autonomy. Royal authority was strongest in the core territories and more dependent on military chiefs and church foundations in recently controlled Galicia and the frontier north of the Duero.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以西哥特法传统、王室裁断、教会法和地方习惯法并用。书面法存在，但在广大乡村地区，纠纷更多通过贵族与神职人员调解解决。
legalSystem.en: Law combined surviving Visigothic legal tradition, royal judgment, canon law, and local custom. Written law existed, but in much of the countryside disputes were more often settled through mediation by nobles and clergy.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以实物征收为主，包括谷物、牲畜、蜂蜜、酒、布匹和劳役。王室还依靠战利品、罚金、教会支持和王室土地收入，货币税的重要性很低。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was primarily in kind, including grain, livestock, honey, wine, cloth, and labor services. The crown also relied on booty, fines, church support, and income from royal lands; monetized taxation remained very limited.

religion.zh: 尼西亚派基督教为国教与统治合法性的核心，主教与修道院在社会整合中作用显著；乡间仍残留部分前基督教习俗与地方圣徒崇拜。
religion.en: Nicene Christianity formed the core of state legitimacy, with bishops and monasteries playing a major role in social integration; some pre-Christian customs and local saint cults persisted in the countryside.

philosophy.zh: 思想以拉丁基督教世界观为主，强调王权护教、罪与救赎、圣徒崇拜以及对西哥特正统传统的继承。
philosophy.en: Thought was dominated by a Latin Christian worldview, emphasizing royal defense of the faith, sin and salvation, the cult of saints, and continuity with orthodox Visigothic tradition.

writingSystem.zh: 使用拉丁字母书写晚期拉丁文，文书与宗教文本由教士和修道院抄写保存，口语则逐渐向伊比利亚罗曼语和阿斯图尔-莱昂方言演变。
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used to write Late Latin, with charters and religious texts preserved by clergy and monasteries; spoken language was evolving toward Ibero-Romance and Astur-Leonese vernaculars.

culturalAchievements.zh: 750年前后的阿斯图里亚斯文化成就主要体现在维持西哥特—拉丁基督教传统、保存教会文书、建立边地教会网络，以及在战乱后重组北方基督徒社会，而非大型建筑或成熟的宫廷艺术。
culturalAchievements.en: Asturian cultural achievement around 750 lay chiefly in preserving the Visigothic-Latin Christian tradition, maintaining ecclesiastical literacy, building frontier church networks, and reorganizing northern Christian society after upheaval, rather than in large monuments or a mature court art.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系：通行晚期通俗拉丁语向伊比利亚罗曼语过渡，部分山区保留前罗曼语言底层影响
languageFamily.en: Indo-European: Late Vulgar Latin transitioning toward Ibero-Romance, with substrate influence from pre-Roman mountain languages in some regions

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和畜牧业为主，货币流通稀少，交换大量依赖实物。由于疆域狭小、人口有限且处于战争边地，其总量远逊于伊斯兰安达卢斯与法兰克地区，但在坎塔布连山区已具备稳定生存与有限扩张能力。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was dominated by subsistence farming and herding, with little coin circulation and much exchange conducted in kind. Because the realm was small, sparsely populated, and located on a war frontier, its total output was far below that of al-Andalus or the Frankish lands, yet it had enough resilience for survival and limited expansion in the Cantabrian zone.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、牧牛牧羊、养马、林业、蜂蜜与蜡、铁器锻造、小规模采矿、皮革和羊毛织造
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, cattle and sheep herding, horse breeding, forestry, honey and wax production, ironworking, small-scale mining, leatherwork, and wool weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、皮革、羊毛、蜂蜡、蜂蜜、木材、铁器、盐以及少量奴隶；输入品包括奢侈布料、酒、宗教器物和少量银币
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, hides, wool, beeswax, honey, timber, iron goods, salt, and a small number of slaves; imports included fine cloth, wine, liturgical objects, and limited quantities of silver coin

name.zh: 索利都斯与第纳里乌斯记账体系

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富体现在土地使用权、牲畜、农具、粮食储备和亲族关系上，而非贵金属。山区自由农民拥有一定独立性，但易受歉收与战争影响。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was held in land-use rights, livestock, tools, food stores, and kin ties rather than precious metal. Mountain free peasants retained some independence but were vulnerable to bad harvests and warfare.

economicSystem.zh: 以农村自给、领主支配和贡赋交换为基础的早期封建化经济，市场规模有限，王室收入依赖土地、实物贡赋和战争掠获。
economicSystem.en: An early feudalizing economy based on rural self-sufficiency, lordly control, and tribute-in-kind exchange, with limited markets and royal income drawn from land, produce, and war booty.

source.en: Tribute in kind and local collections

category.zh: 教会、修道院与宗教建筑

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模不大，更多由贵金属器物、教会保管资产、牲畜储备、谷仓存粮和可动员贡赋构成，而非大量铸币现金。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was modest and consisted more of precious metal objects, church-held reserves, livestock, stored grain, and mobilizable dues than large quantities of coined cash.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重在维持边防、奖赏贵族追随者和支持教会。王权尽量积累实物储备，以便应对战事、饥荒和迁民安置。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, aimed at sustaining frontier defense, rewarding noble followers, and supporting the church. The monarchy sought to maintain reserves in kind to meet war, famine, and resettlement needs.

description.zh: 由国王直属战士、贵族扈从与精选步兵组成，负责宫廷保卫、快速突击和山地作战。
description.en: Composed of the king’s direct retainers, noble attendants, and selected infantry for court protection, rapid strikes, and mountain warfare.

description.zh: 由自由农民和地方追随者组成，战时集结，适于守山口、伏击和短期远征。
description.en: Raised from free peasants and local retainers, assembled in wartime and suited for pass defense, ambushes, and short campaigns.

description.zh: 数量有限，主要是贵族及其侍从骑马作战，用于侦察、追击和指挥联络，而非大规模重骑兵冲锋。
description.en: Limited in number, consisting of nobles and their attendants fighting on horseback for scouting, pursuit, and command liaison rather than large heavy cavalry charges.

description.zh: 驻守山地要塞、河谷据点和补给节点，监视穆斯林边境动向。
description.en: Stationed in mountain strongholds, valley posts, and supply nodes to watch movements on the Muslim frontier.

commanderInChief.zh: 阿方索一世国王
commanderInChief.en: King Alfonso I

technology.zh: 装备以长矛、短剑、圆盾、投枪、弓箭和锁子甲或皮甲为主，沿袭晚期西哥特与地方山地战传统。攻城能力有限，更擅长突袭、焚掠和复杂地形中的防御作战。
technology.en: Equipment centered on spears, short swords, round shields, javelins, bows, and mail or leather armor, reflecting late Visigothic and local mountain warfare traditions. Siege capacity was limited; the army was more effective in raiding, devastation, and defensive fighting in difficult terrain.

threats.zh: 首要威胁来自南方穆斯林伊比利亚的远征军与边境据点；其次是内部贵族离心、补给不足以及王国扩张过快造成的控制力稀薄。
threats.en: The main threat came from Muslim Iberian expeditions and frontier strongholds to the south; secondary dangers included centrifugal nobles, limited supplies, and overstretched control from rapid expansion.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在阿斯图里亚斯、坎塔布里亚与加利西亚部分地区的山谷、沿海带和可耕盆地。居民包括本地阿斯图尔与坎塔布连人口、加利西亚人，以及逃离穆斯林统治区的西哥特—伊比利亚基督徒。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in valleys, coastal strips, and cultivable basins of Asturias, Cantabria, and parts of Galicia. Inhabitants included native Astur and Cantabrian populations, Galicians, and Visigothic-Iberian Christians who had fled Muslim-ruled territories.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与大贵族、地方贵族和军事扈从、主教与修士、自由农民、小佃户、依附民、手工业者与少量奴隶构成主要社会层次。自由农民在北方山区的重要性高于许多同时代国家。
socialClasses.en: The main social layers were the royal family and great nobles, local nobles and military retainers, bishops and monks, free peasants, small tenants, dependents, artisans, and a small number of slaves. Free peasants were more important in the northern mountains than in many contemporary states.

allies.zh: 无正式大国盟友，但与法兰克世界、教宗势力及伊比利亚北部基督教社群存在潜在宗教与政治联系。
allies.en: It had no formal great-power ally, but maintained potential religious and political ties with the Frankish world, the papacy, and Christian communities across northern Iberia.

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为穆斯林伊比利亚的倭马亚统治者及其地方驻军。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were the Umayyad rulers of Muslim Iberia and their local garrisons.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以生存和边境机会主义为主：避免与更强邻国正面决战，通过山地防御、边境袭扰、迁移基督徒居民和争取教会合法性来巩固自身地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was focused on survival and frontier opportunism: avoiding direct confrontation with stronger powers while using mountain defense, frontier raiding, relocation of Christian populations, and ecclesiastical legitimacy to consolidate the kingdom.

era.zh: 早期中世纪西欧山地边疆时代
era.en: Early Medieval Western European mountain frontier age

keyInnovations.zh: 技术并无显著原创突破，关键在于延续罗马—西哥特农业与铁器工艺、山地防御工事、修道院抄写文化，以及适应边境战争的快速动员方式。
keyInnovations.en: There were no major original technological breakthroughs; the kingdom’s strengths lay in preserving Romano-Visigothic agriculture and ironworking, mountain fortification, monastic literacy, and rapid mobilization suited to frontier war.

strengths.zh: 地形险要、社会凝聚力较强、基督教合法性明确、自由农民与贵族战团具备韧性，且能吸纳南方流亡人口与西哥特政治遗产。
strengths.en: Its strengths were defensible terrain, relatively strong social cohesion, clear Christian legitimacy, resilient free peasant and noble warbands, and the ability to absorb southern refugees and Visigothic political heritage.

weaknesses.zh: 人口和财政规模很小，城市与贸易薄弱，中央集权有限，难以长期进行大规模攻城或平原会战，且内部贵族关系仍可能动摇王权。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were its very small demographic and fiscal base, weak urban and commercial development, limited centralization, inability to sustain major sieges or open-field warfare for long, and the continuing potential for noble instability.

outlook.zh: 在750年前后，阿斯图里亚斯前景谨慎乐观：只要穆斯林伊比利亚内部持续出现政治紧张，王国便有机会稳固加利西亚与杜罗河以北边地，并逐步塑造自己为伊比利亚基督教复兴的核心之一。
outlook.en: Around 750, Asturias had a cautiously favorable outlook: so long as political tensions continued within Muslim Iberia, the kingdom had a chance to secure Galicia and the lands north of the Duero, gradually presenting itself as one of the cores of Christian renewal in Iberia.

description.zh: 伊比利亚北部基督教王国，是后期收复失地运动的核心之一。
description.en: A northern Iberian Christian kingdom, one of the nuclei of the later Reconquista.

dynasty.zh: 与杜罗氏相关的统治支系

governmentForm.zh: 由草原军事贵族领导、统辖保加尔人与斯拉夫部族联盟的君主制汗国
governmentForm.en: A monarchical khanate led by a steppe military aristocracy ruling over Bulgars and a federation of Slavic tribes

socialStructure.zh: 社会由统治性的原始保加尔贵族、附属与盟属的斯拉夫部族首领、自由农牧民、战士随从、手工业者与少量俘虏奴役人口构成；国家整合仍在推进，族群与部落身份仍强于统一臣民身份。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of a ruling Proto-Bulgar aristocracy, subordinate and allied Slavic tribal chiefs, free farmers and herders, warrior retainers, artisans, and a smaller population of captives and dependents; state integration was still advancing, and ethnic and tribal identities remained stronger than a fully unified subject identity.

rulingClass.zh: 保加尔波伊拉贵族、可汗家族及其军事随从集团
rulingClass.en: The Bulgar boila aristocracy, the khan's family, and its military retinue

succession.zh: 名义上为王族世袭，实际上需获得主要贵族与军政首领承认，宫廷政变与贵族干预并不少见。
succession.en: Nominally hereditary within the ruling line, but in practice succession required recognition by leading nobles and military-political chiefs, with coups and aristocratic intervention being far from rare.

structure.zh: 中央政权围绕可汗宫廷运作，结合草原式君主权威、贵族议决传统与对斯拉夫部族的间接统治。普利斯卡既是王廷驻地，也是军事与贡赋集散中心。
structure.en: The central government operated around the khan's court, combining steppe-style monarchical authority, aristocratic deliberative traditions, and indirect rule over Slavic tribes. Pliska served as the royal seat as well as the main military and tribute-collection center.

function.zh: 负责宫廷事务、诏令传达、使节接待、盟约记录与核心政治协调。
function.en: Handled court affairs, transmission of decrees, reception of envoys, recording of treaties, and core political coordination.

function.zh: 统筹禁卫、边防、征召、骑兵调动、营垒维护与对拜占庭方向的战争准备。
function.en: Coordinated the guard, frontier defense, levies, cavalry deployment, camp-fortress maintenance, and war preparation against Byzantium.

function.zh: 征收粮食、牲畜、手工业贡赋与战利品分配，并管理王室储备。
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, craft tribute, and war booty distributions, and managed royal reserves.

name.zh: 部族自治与边地事务署

function.zh: 处理斯拉夫部族义务、边地安置、迁徙管理、附庸关系与地方调停。
function.en: Managed obligations of Slavic tribes, frontier settlement, migration control, vassal relations, and local mediation.

function.zh: 负责土木防御工事、宫廷建筑、武器工坊、车马与储备设施维护。
function.en: Oversaw earthwork fortifications, court construction, weapons workshops, transport animals and carts, and storage facilities.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖保加尔军事总管、贵族家族与受约束的斯拉夫部族首领共同实施。中央对核心地区控制较强，对边缘地区多通过贡赋、征兵与人质制度维系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on Bulgar military governors, noble families, and subordinated Slavic tribal chiefs acting together. Central control was stronger in the core territories, while peripheral areas were held mainly through tribute, military obligations, and hostages.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以习惯法为主，融合草原贵族法、部族仲裁、血亲赔偿与可汗裁断。尚未形成如拜占庭般成文法典，刑罚强调威慑、忠诚与秩序维护。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was primarily customary, combining steppe aristocratic law, tribal arbitration, kin-compensation, and the khan's judgment. It had not developed a codified structure like Byzantium's, and punishments emphasized deterrence, loyalty, and maintenance of order.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自部族贡赋、农业征收、牲畜税、贸易关卡、战利品与对附属群体的劳役义务；货币税收有限，实物征收更常见。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal extraction came chiefly from tribal tribute, agricultural dues, livestock taxes, trade tolls, war booty, and labor obligations from subordinate groups; monetary taxation was limited, and collection in kind was more common.

religion.zh: 统治集团主要信奉原始保加尔的腾格里式天神崇拜与祖先祭祀，斯拉夫居民广泛保持多神信仰，境内亦有少量基督徒，尤其在与拜占庭接触地区。
religion.en: The ruling elite mainly followed a Proto-Bulgar Tengri-like sky cult and ancestor worship, while the Slavic population broadly retained polytheistic beliefs; there were also small Christian communities, especially in zones of contact with Byzantium.

philosophy.zh: 思想传统以部族伦理、军事忠诚、祖先威望与实用政治为核心，并无系统哲学学派；来自拜占庭的基督教观念与行政文化影响正在边缘渗透。
philosophy.en: The intellectual tradition centered on tribal ethics, military loyalty, ancestral prestige, and practical politics rather than formal philosophical schools; Christian ideas and administrative culture from Byzantium were beginning to filter in at the margins.

writingSystem.zh: 国家尚无广泛普及的本土书写行政体系，官方记忆多依赖口传、符号刻记和希腊文或其他外来文字在外交中的有限使用。
writingSystem.en: The state did not yet possess a broadly institutionalized native writing system for administration; official memory relied largely on oral transmission, symbolic marks, and limited use of Greek or other foreign scripts in diplomacy.

culturalAchievements.zh: 这一时期的成就主要体现在普利斯卡早期都城格局、土木与木石结合防御工事、草原与巴尔干元素融合的宫廷文化、金属器制作、骑兵军事传统，以及一个跨多族群国家认同的初步形成。
culturalAchievements.en: The main achievements of the period lay in the early urban layout of Pliska, earthwork and mixed timber-stone fortifications, a court culture blending steppe and Balkan elements, metalworking, cavalry military traditions, and the early formation of a multi-ethnic state identity.

languageFamily.zh: 统治层原始保加尔语可能属突厥语系或与草原语言群相关；境内多数人口使用南斯拉夫语支方言。
languageFamily.en: The ruling elite's Proto-Bulgar language was likely Turkic or related to the steppe linguistic sphere, while the majority population spoke dialects of the South Slavic branch.

gdpDescription.zh: 750年前后的保加利亚经济以农业、畜牧、贡赋与军事掠夺收益为主，市场交换增长但尚不如拜占庭货币化。其经济实力在巴尔干北部属于强国水平，但总体仍是以土地、牲畜与人力动员为核心的早期中世纪经济。
gdpDescription.en: Around 750, Bulgaria's economy was based mainly on agriculture, pastoralism, tribute, and gains from military raiding, with market exchange growing but still less monetized than Byzantium. Its economic strength ranked it among the major powers of the northern Balkans, though it remained an early medieval economy centered on land, livestock, and mobilization of manpower.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧养马、蜂蜜与蜡、铁器锻造、皮革、木工、陶器、武器制作、边境贸易与贡赋运输
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, livestock raising and horse breeding, honey and wax production, ironworking, leatherwork, carpentry, pottery, weapons manufacture, frontier trade, and tribute transport

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牲畜、皮毛、蜂蜜、蜡、盐、奴隶、金属制品、马匹，以及自拜占庭输入的丝织品、酒、奢侈品与铸币
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, furs, honey, wax, salt, slaves, metal goods, and horses, alongside imports from Byzantium such as silk textiles, wine, luxury goods, and coinage

name.zh: 拜占庭索利都与实物交换并用
name.en: Byzantine solidus and barter in parallel

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距很大。核心贵族以土地控制、牲畜群、随从武装和贡赋权力积累财富；普通家庭的主要资产是耕地使用权、牛羊、马匹、谷物储备和简单工具。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply. Core nobles accumulated wealth through control of land, herds, armed retainers, and tribute rights, while ordinary households held value mainly in cultivation rights, cattle, sheep, horses, grain reserves, and basic tools.

economicSystem.zh: 国家经济为部族贡赋、王室再分配与地方自给农业的混合体，辅以对拜占庭边境市场和外交交换的有限参与。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a mix of tribal tribute, royal redistribution, and locally self-sufficient agriculture, supplemented by limited participation in Byzantine frontier markets and diplomatic exchange.

source.en: Livestock dues and collections in kind

category.en: Army, frontier defense, and horse supply

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要由贵金属储备、拜占庭金币、战利品、牲畜、粮食与武器库存构成。对这类早期中世纪国家而言，仓储与可动员资源与金属货币同样重要。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly of precious metal reserves, Byzantine coin, war booty, livestock, grain stocks, and weapon inventories. For an early medieval state of this kind, stored goods and mobilizable resources were as important as metallic money.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守务实，以维持军队忠诚、边防稳固和贵族联盟平衡为核心，避免对斯拉夫部族征收过重而激化离心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and pragmatic, aimed above all at maintaining army loyalty, stable frontier defense, and balance within the noble coalition, while avoiding overburdening Slavic tribes in ways that might intensify centrifugal tendencies.

description.zh: 由保加尔贵族及其随从组成，是国家最精锐力量，负责冲击、护卫王廷与关键战役。
description.en: Composed of Bulgar nobles and their retainers, this was the state's elite force, used for shock action, protection of the court, and decisive engagements.

description.zh: 机动侦察、袭扰、追击与边境巡逻主力，继承草原作战传统。
description.en: The main force for scouting, raiding, pursuit, and frontier patrol, preserving classic steppe methods of war.

description.zh: 由农业人口和部族战士组成，多用于守城、伏击、山地与森林作战以及后勤支援。
description.en: Drawn from the agrarian population and tribal fighters, they were mainly used for fort defense, ambushes, mountain and forest warfare, and logistical support.

description.zh: 驻守普利斯卡及边境防线，维持预警、道路控制和关隘防务。
description.en: Stationed in Pliska and along frontier lines, they maintained warning systems, road control, and defense of passes and strongpoints.

commanderInChief.zh: 可汗科尔米索什
commanderInChief.en: Khan Kormisosh

technology.zh: 军事技术以复合弓骑射、长枪骑兵、木土要塞、皮革与铁甲、盾矛步战和快速机动为核心；攻城技术有限，常依赖包围、袭扰和机会战而非复杂器械。
technology.en: Military technology centered on composite-bow mounted archery, lance-armed cavalry, timber-earth fortifications, leather and iron armor, spear-and-shield infantry fighting, and rapid mobility; siege capability was limited and often relied more on blockade, harassment, and opportunism than on advanced engines.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自拜占庭帝国的北上反击、与周边斯拉夫部族关系不稳、黑海草原方向势力变动，以及内部贵族政争可能削弱战时指挥。
threats.en: The main threats came from Byzantine counteroffensives to the north, unstable relations with neighboring Slavic groups, shifts among powers on the Black Sea steppe, and internal noble factionalism that could weaken wartime command.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约120万，其中多数为定居的斯拉夫农业人口，原始保加尔人作为统治与军事核心人数较少但政治地位突出。人口密度在多瑙河以南平原与东北巴尔干核心区较高。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 1.2 million, with the majority consisting of settled Slavic agricultural communities, while the Proto-Bulgars formed a smaller but politically dominant ruling and military core. Population density was higher in the core plains south of the Danube and in the northeastern Balkans.

name.zh: 马尔切利周边边境聚落

socialClasses.zh: 上层为可汗家族、波伊拉贵族和高级军事头领；中层为部族首领、自由战士、富裕牧主与地方长者；下层为普通农民、牧民、工匠、附属民与战俘依附人口。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the khan's family, the boila nobility, and senior military leaders; the middle stratum included tribal chiefs, free warriors, prosperous herders, and local elders; the lower stratum consisted of ordinary peasants, herders, artisans, dependents, and captive laborers.

allies.zh: 无稳固的长期大国盟友，但可依据局势联合部分斯拉夫部族或与草原势力保持临时协调。
allies.en: It had no stable long-term great-power ally, but could align with certain Slavic tribes or maintain temporary coordination with steppe powers depending on circumstances.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手是东罗马帝国；双方围绕巴尔干边境、贡赋、要塞与战略通道长期对抗。
enemies.en: Its principal enemy was the Eastern Roman Empire, with the two sides locked in long-term rivalry over Balkan frontiers, tribute, fortresses, and strategic corridors.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以牵制拜占庭、巩固巴尔干东北部霸权、控制斯拉夫部族并保持与草原世界的灵活联系为核心，采取战争、威慑、通婚、纳贡与和约并用的现实主义路线。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on containing Byzantium, consolidating hegemony in the northeastern Balkans, controlling Slavic tribes, and maintaining flexible links with the steppe world, following a pragmatic mix of war, deterrence, marriage diplomacy, tribute, and treaty-making.

era.zh: 中世纪早期，处于欧亚唐朝盛世同时代
era.en: Early Middle Ages, contemporary with the Tang Golden Age across Eurasia

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力在于草原骑兵战法、本地化土木与木石要塞建造、跨部族军事动员、马具与复合弓运用，以及将游动政权逐步转化为定都统治结构。
keyInnovations.en: Key capabilities lay in steppe cavalry warfare, localized earthwork and mixed timber-stone fortification building, cross-tribal military mobilization, use of horse gear and composite bows, and the gradual transformation of a mobile polity into a capital-centered ruling structure.

strengths.zh: 保加利亚在750年具有强悍骑兵、能动员大规模斯拉夫附属人口、地处多瑙河与巴尔干战略要冲，并已形成稳定的统治中心普利斯卡。它对拜占庭构成持续军事压力，是东南欧最重要的新兴强权之一。
strengths.en: In 750, Bulgaria possessed formidable cavalry, the ability to mobilize large Slavic subject populations, a strategic position between the Danube and the Balkans, and an established ruling center at Pliska. It posed sustained military pressure on Byzantium and ranked among the most important rising powers in southeastern Europe.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于王位继承不稳、贵族政治斗争频繁、国家整合程度有限、货币化和文书行政薄弱，以及对部族忠诚和军事成功的高度依赖。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in unstable succession, frequent noble political struggles, limited state integration, weak monetization and documentary administration, and a heavy dependence on tribal loyalty and military success.

outlook.zh: 短期内，若可汗能够维持贵族平衡并成功抵御拜占庭，国家将继续稳固扩张并深化对斯拉夫人口的统合；但若宫廷政变加剧，保加利亚也可能在强敌压力下出现统治危机。
outlook.en: In the short term, if the khan could preserve noble balance and successfully resist Byzantium, the state would likely continue consolidating and expanding while deepening its integration of Slavic populations; however, if court coups intensified, Bulgaria could also face a crisis of rule under pressure from powerful enemies.

description.zh: 巴尔干东北部的重要草原-斯拉夫混合国家，与拜占庭长期对峙。
description.en: A major steppe-Slavic state in the northeastern Balkans, long opposed to Byzantium.

ruler.zh: 优素福·伊本·阿卜杜勒·拉赫曼·菲赫里

governmentForm.zh: 名义上隶属于倭马亚哈里发国、实际上日益自主的军事总督制行省政权
governmentForm.en: A military governorship nominally subject to the Umayyad Caliphate but becoming increasingly autonomous in practice

socialStructure.zh: 社会由阿拉伯征服贵族、柏柏尔部族军人、穆瓦利归附者、本地皈依穆斯林、犹太社群以及占多数的基督教与穆扎拉卜人口构成，族群、宗教与征服资历共同决定地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of Arab conquest elites, Berber tribal soldiers, mawali clients, locally converted Muslims, Jewish communities, and a majority Christian and Mozarab population, with rank shaped by ethnicity, religion, and conquest pedigree.

rulingClass.zh: 阿拉伯军政家族与其附属的叙利亚和也门系军团首领
rulingClass.en: Arab military-administrative families and the chiefs of attached Syrian and Yemeni-oriented jund contingents

succession.zh: 并非世袭君主制；总督通常由哈里发任命或由驻军与地方显贵在危机中推举，750年因东方政局崩解而带有强烈事实自治色彩。
succession.en: Not a hereditary monarchy; the governor was normally appointed by the caliph or elevated by military and provincial notables in crisis, and by 750 this had taken on a strongly de facto autonomous character because of upheaval in the east.

structure.zh: 政权核心设于科尔多瓦，由总督统辖军政、税收和司法；地方上依托旧西哥特城市、军事驻屯点和部族网络治理各省区，沿用伊斯兰行省制度并吸收晚期罗马—西哥特行政习惯。
structure.en: The regime was centered in Cordoba, where the governor directed military, fiscal, and judicial affairs; locally it relied on former Visigothic cities, garrison centers, and tribal networks to govern provincial districts, combining Islamic provincial practice with late Roman-Visigothic administrative habits.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保存档案、处理任命、与各城及东方哈里发政权往来文书。
function.en: Drafted decrees, kept archives, handled appointments, and managed correspondence with cities and with the eastern caliphal authorities.

function.zh: 征收土地税、人头税、什一税与市场课税，核定庄园、灌溉地和城市税基。
function.en: Collected land tax, poll tax, tithe, and market dues, and assessed estates, irrigated lands, and urban tax bases.

function.zh: 管理驻军名册、军饷、军马、要塞补给及边疆征讨动员。
function.en: Managed garrison rolls, military pay, horses, fortress supply, and mobilization for frontier expeditions.

function.zh: 协调卡迪任命、监督清真寺与宗教基金，并处理穆斯林社群法务；非穆斯林社群保留部分内部法权。
function.en: Coordinated qadi appointments, supervised mosques and religious endowments, and handled legal affairs for Muslims; non-Muslim communities retained limited internal jurisdiction.

function.zh: 维持驿传、传递军政消息、监视地方豪强和部族派系。
function.en: Maintained courier routes, transmitted military and administrative intelligence, and monitored local magnates and tribal factions.

function.zh: 管理集市秩序、度量衡、桥梁道路维护与主要城市治安。
function.en: Oversaw market order, weights and measures, road and bridge maintenance, and policing in major towns.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以库拉和城镇为基础，由总督任命或认可的地方长官、军镇首领、税吏与法官共同治理；边境地区更依赖军事驻屯和与地方贵族的协商。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on districts and towns, governed by provincial officials, garrison commanders, tax collectors, and judges appointed or recognized by the governor; frontier areas relied more heavily on military settlements and negotiation with local magnates.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系并存：穆斯林社群适用早期伊斯兰法与总督敕令，基督徒和犹太人多按各自宗教法处理婚姻、继承等内部事务；土地、税赋和治安则由行省权力直接裁定。
legalSystem.en: The legal order was plural: Muslims were judged by early Islamic law and gubernatorial decree, while Christians and Jews often handled marriage, inheritance, and communal matters under their own religious law; land, taxation, and security were adjudicated directly by provincial authority.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖对非穆斯林人头税、土地税、穆斯林什一税、市场税、关津税以及战利品与罚没收入；皈依与地方和约会改变具体税负。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended chiefly on poll tax from non-Muslims, land tax, Muslim tithe, market dues, tolls, and income from booty and confiscation; conversion and local treaty arrangements could alter the exact burden.

religion.zh: 统治集团以逊尼派伊斯兰教为主，但750年安达卢斯宗教格局高度多元，绝大多数人口仍为基督徒，犹太社群在许多城市中亦具重要地位。
religion.en: The ruling elite was predominantly Sunni Muslim, but the religious landscape of al-Andalus in 750 was highly diverse: most inhabitants were still Christian, and Jewish communities remained important in many towns.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活尚处形成期，以《古兰经》诠释、阿拉伯部族政治伦理、伊斯兰法学萌芽及晚期古典基督教—犹太学术传统并存为特色。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was still in a formative stage, characterized by Qur'anic interpretation, Arab tribal political ethics, early Islamic legal thought, and the coexistence of late antique Christian and Jewish scholarly traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 官方书写以阿拉伯字母为主；民间与教会文书继续使用拉丁文和后期西哥特书写传统，部分地方仍见罗曼方言口语。
writingSystem.en: Arabic script dominated official writing; meanwhile Latin and late Visigothic scribal traditions persisted in church and local documentation, and Romance vernaculars continued in speech.

culturalAchievements.zh: 这一时期的重要成就在于罗马—西哥特城市网络与伊斯兰行政、建筑和农业技术的结合；科尔多瓦逐渐成为西地中海新的阿拉伯化文化中心，清真寺、灌溉设施和多语环境开始塑造独特的安达卢斯文明。
culturalAchievements.en: The era's chief achievement lay in merging the Roman-Visigothic urban network with Islamic administration, architecture, and agrarian techniques; Cordoba was beginning to emerge as a new Arabized cultural center of the western Mediterranean, while mosques, irrigation works, and a multilingual environment started to shape a distinct Andalusi civilization.

languageFamily.zh: 统治层主要使用闪米特语系的阿拉伯语；多数民众使用罗曼语支地方俗语，宗教文书中亦见拉丁语和希伯来语。
languageFamily.en: The ruling stratum chiefly used Arabic of the Semitic family; most of the population spoke local Romance vernaculars, while Latin and Hebrew also appeared in religious texts.

gdpDescription.zh: 750年的穆斯林伊比利亚是一个以农业为基础、城市商业较为发达的区域经济体。其财富来自大庄园、灌溉农业、畜牧、手工业城市和地中海贸易，但因内战与政权重组，整体产出低于其长期潜力。
gdpDescription.en: In 750 Muslim Iberia was an agrarian economy with a relatively developed urban-commercial sector. Wealth came from estates, irrigated farming, pastoralism, artisanal towns, and Mediterranean trade, but civil conflict and political realignment kept output below its long-term potential.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、橄榄与葡萄生产、畜牧、纺织、皮革、陶器、金属加工、建筑、灌溉农业和区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, olive and grape production, livestock raising, textiles, leatherwork, pottery, metalworking, construction, irrigated agriculture, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 橄榄油、葡萄酒与葡萄干（部分由非穆斯林生产和消费）、谷物、羊毛、皮革、奴隶、马匹、金属器、陶器、蜂蜜、盐和地中海输入的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Olive oil, wine and raisins (partly produced and consumed by non-Muslims), grain, wool, leather, slaves, horses, metal goods, pottery, honey, salt, and luxury imports from the Mediterranean

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距显著。阿拉伯与部分柏柏尔军事定居者掌握较多土地和税收份额，城市商人与地主较富；大多数乡村家庭以自给农业和租佃为生，现金持有有限。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Arab and some Berber military settlers held a larger share of land and tax income, while urban merchants and landlords were comparatively prosperous; most rural households lived from subsistence farming and tenancy with limited cash reserves.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地租和税赋为核心、辅以城市市场交换和跨地中海贸易的前近代混合经济；国家不直接控制全部生产，但通过税收、驻军与土地分配深刻影响资源流向。
economicSystem.en: A premodern mixed economy centered on agrarian rents and taxation, supplemented by urban market exchange and trans-Mediterranean trade; the state did not directly control all production but strongly shaped resource flows through taxation, garrisons, and land allocation.

category.zh: 派系安抚、馈赠与非常支出
category.en: Factional appeasement, gifts, and extraordinary spending

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库尚能维持正常军政开销，但因东方倭马亚崩溃、派系斗争和边境战争而承受压力，流动储备有限，越来越依赖临时征敛与地方妥协。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury could still sustain ordinary military and administrative expenses, but pressure from the Umayyad collapse in the east, factional conflict, and frontier warfare left liquid reserves limited and increased reliance on ad hoc levies and local bargains.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持军队忠诚和行政连续性，优先保证军饷与边防；在危机中常通过临时税、罚没和重新分配税源来弥补赤字。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed above all to preserve troop loyalty and administrative continuity, prioritizing military pay and frontier defense; in crisis it often used extraordinary taxes, confiscations, and reassignment of revenue rights to cover deficits.

name.zh: 阿拉伯与叙利亚驻军骑兵

description.zh: 由阿拉伯征服者后裔和叙利亚军团组成，是政治上最关键的机动核心，擅长快速镇压、边境袭击和野战突击。
description.en: Composed of descendants of the Arab conquerors and Syrian jund contingents, this was the politically decisive mobile core, suited for rapid suppression, frontier raiding, and battlefield shock action.

name.zh: 柏柏尔轻骑与山地部队
name.en: Berber Light Cavalry and Mountain Troops

description.zh: 人数众多，机动性强，适合山地与边疆作战，但忠诚度常受地方利益和族群矛盾影响。
description.en: Numerous and mobile, these troops were effective in mountain and frontier warfare, but their loyalty was often affected by local interests and ethnic tensions.

description.zh: 负责守城、护卫仓储、维持交通线和随军工程，装备以矛、盾、短剑和弓为主。
description.en: Responsible for city defense, storehouse security, maintaining lines of communication, and campaign engineering, they were equipped mainly with spears, shields, sidearms, and bows.

description.zh: 由地方部族、城镇民兵和附属贵族武装组成，多用于季节性防务与后勤支持。
description.en: Raised from local tribes, town militias, and subordinate noble retinues, they were used mainly for seasonal defense and logistical support.

commanderInChief.zh: 优素福·菲赫里总督兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: Governor Yusuf al-Fihri as commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军事技术属8世纪西地中海常见水平，依赖骑兵机动、复合战法、攻城器械、要塞链和罗马旧路网；海军力量有限，不足以与拜占庭或后来的科尔多瓦舰队相比。
technology.en: Military technology matched the normal 8th-century western Mediterranean level, relying on cavalry mobility, combined-arms practice, siege engines, fortress chains, and the old Roman road network; naval power was limited and far below that of Byzantium or the later Cordoban fleets.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自内部阿拉伯—柏柏尔派系冲突、倭马亚崩溃后的合法性危机、北方阿斯图里亚斯山区抵抗，以及可能从北非和东方引发的新一轮权力竞争。
threats.en: The main threats were internal Arab-Berber factional conflict, the legitimacy crisis following the Umayyad collapse, resistance from Asturias in the northern mountains, and potential new struggles for power linked to North Africa and the east.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约四百万，绝大多数分布于瓜达尔基维尔河谷、埃布罗流域、地中海沿岸与旧西哥特城市带。穆斯林仍是统治少数，基督徒为多数，犹太人主要集中于城镇。
populationDescription.en: The population was about four million, concentrated above all in the Guadalquivir valley, the Ebro basin, the Mediterranean littoral, and the former Visigothic urban belt. Muslims were still a ruling minority, Christians remained the majority, and Jews were mainly urban.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为阿拉伯军政贵族与大地主；中层包括柏柏尔军人、城市商人、法学家、书记与宗教人士；下层则是自耕农、佃农、工匠、奴隶和受庇护社群。宗教身份和族裔出身与经济地位交叉重叠。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of Arab military-administrative nobles and major landholders; the middle included Berber soldiers, urban merchants, jurists, scribes, and religious figures; the lower comprised free peasants, tenants, artisans, slaves, and protected communities. Religious identity and ethnic origin overlapped strongly with economic status.

allies.zh: 缺乏稳固的正式盟友；实际合作对象包括部分北非柏柏尔势力、愿与科尔多瓦妥协的伊比利亚地方贵族，以及在具体战事中可被收买或安抚的边境集团。
allies.en: It lacked firm formal allies; practical partners included some North African Berber forces, Iberian local magnates willing to bargain with Cordoba, and frontier groups that could be bought off or pacified in particular conflicts.

enemies.zh: 敌对力量主要是北方阿斯图里亚斯政权、伊比利亚境内叛乱派系，以及随着阿拔斯革命推进而威胁其合法性的东方新政权及相关支持者。
enemies.en: Hostile forces included chiefly the Asturian polity in the north, rebellious factions within Iberia, and the new eastern regime emerging from the Abbasid Revolution together with its sympathizers, which threatened its legitimacy.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交目标以维持安达卢斯内部控制、确保与北非联系、阻止北方基督教边疆扩张为先；对东方则采取观望与事实自治并存的策略，等待哈里发世界权力重组的结果。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy prioritized retaining control within al-Andalus, preserving links with North Africa, and preventing expansion by the northern Christian frontier; toward the east it combined caution with de facto autonomy while awaiting the outcome of the caliphal world's reorganization.

era.zh: 中世纪早期，地中海后古典时代
era.en: Early Medieval, Post-Classical Mediterranean age

keyInnovations.zh: 阿拉伯化财政与档案制度、改进的灌溉和水利技术、骑兵机动作战、城市市场监管、跨地中海货币流通，以及对罗马—西哥特道路桥梁与筑城体系的继续利用。
keyInnovations.en: Arabized fiscal and archival administration, improved irrigation and water management, mobile cavalry warfare, urban market supervision, trans-Mediterranean monetary circulation, and continued use of Roman-Visigothic roads, bridges, and fortification systems

strengths.zh: 控制伊比利亚最富庶的农业区和主要城市，拥有成熟的税基、较强的骑兵力量、连接北非与地中海贸易的地理优势，以及能吸收多元传统的行政弹性。
strengths.en: It controlled Iberia's richest agricultural zones and principal cities, possessed a mature tax base, fielded strong cavalry forces, enjoyed a geographic position linking North Africa and Mediterranean trade, and showed administrative flexibility in absorbing diverse traditions.

weaknesses.zh: 政权合法性脆弱，阿拉伯与柏柏尔矛盾尖锐，东方宗主崩解带来权力真空，地方豪强离心，北部山地难以彻底征服，财政又因持续冲突而承压。
weaknesses.en: Its legitimacy was fragile, Arab-Berber tensions were acute, the collapse of the eastern overlord created a vacuum of authority, local magnates were centrifugal, the northern mountains were difficult to subdue, and the treasury was strained by continuing conflict.

outlook.zh: 短期前景动荡而不稳定。若不能建立新的合法统治核心，安达卢斯可能陷入更深的军阀化与派系战争；但其城市、农业和战略位置极具潜力，一旦出现有号召力的新领袖，便可能迅速重组为更稳固的西方伊斯兰政权。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was turbulent and unstable. Without a new center of legitimate authority, al-Andalus could sink deeper into warlordism and factional strife; yet its cities, agriculture, and strategic position held immense potential, and with the rise of a charismatic new leader it could rapidly reorganize into a more durable western Islamic state.

description.zh: 伊比利亚半岛大部的穆斯林统治区，在倭马亚崩溃中出现权力重组。
description.en: The Muslim-ruled bulk of Iberia, undergoing political realignment amid Umayyad collapse.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部落公国联盟，结合酋邦统治、亲族网络与战士随从体制
governmentForm.en: A decentralized constellation of tribal duchies combining chieftainship, kinship networks, and warrior retinues

socialStructure.zh: 社会以宗族、村社与部族为核心，自由农民占多数，另有战士贵族、祭司或占卜者、工匠、商旅以及少量奴仆与战俘
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on clans, village communities, and tribes, with free peasants forming the majority alongside warrior elites, ritual specialists, craftsmen, traders, and a smaller number of slaves and captives

rulingClass.zh: 地方公爵、部族首领、氏族长老与其战士随从构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: Local dukes, tribal leaders, clan elders, and their warrior retinues formed the ruling class

succession.zh: 继承多依部族习惯法，在家族世袭、长幼资历与贵族会议推举之间调和，并常伴随武力竞争
succession.en: Succession generally followed tribal custom, balancing hereditary claims, seniority, and selection by leading men, often accompanied by armed rivalry

structure.zh: 政治结构高度分散，以若干部族与早期公国为单位。每一政治共同体通常由公爵或酋长统领，在长老会议、战士集会和亲族领袖协助下治理聚落、征收贡赋并组织防御
structure.en: The political structure was highly decentralized, organized around multiple tribes and early duchies. Each polity was typically led by a duke or chieftain who governed settlements, collected tribute, and organized defense with the support of councils of elders, warrior assemblies, and kin leaders

function.zh: 管理首领居所、随从、储藏、宴飨、贡物分配与对外接待
function.en: Managed the ruler's residence, retainers, storage, feasting, tribute distribution, and reception of outsiders

function.zh: 协调部族会议、调解争端、确认继承、见证盟约与重大习惯法裁决
function.en: Coordinated tribal meetings, mediated disputes, confirmed succession, witnessed alliances, and supervised major customary judgments

function.zh: 征集粮食、蜂蜜、皮毛、牲畜与劳役，管理部族仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, honey, furs, livestock, and labor dues, and managed tribal storage and redistribution

name.en: Military Mobilization and Fortified Settlement Defense

function.zh: 组织战士、守卫木土堡寨、动员村社民兵并维持边地警戒
function.en: Organized warriors, guarded timber-earth strongholds, mobilized village levies, and maintained frontier watch

function.zh: 管理河渡、集市、通行费与外来商旅安全，尤其是琥珀、皮毛、盐与奴隶贸易
function.en: Oversaw river crossings, markets, tolls, and the security of visiting merchants, especially in amber, furs, salt, and slave trade

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托村社、堡寨与部族领地。村庄由家长与长老处理日常事务，较大堡寨由酋长代理人或战士首领驻守。行政边界并不固定，常随部族联盟、婚姻关系与军事局势而改变
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on village communities, hillforts, and tribal territories. Villages handled daily matters through household heads and elders, while larger strongholds were held by a chieftain's deputies or warrior captains. Administrative boundaries were fluid and often shifted with tribal alliances, marriage ties, and military conditions

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、赔偿原则、血亲复仇调停和公开集会裁决为基础。重视誓言、见证人与财物赔偿，严重冲突则可能诉诸械斗、驱逐或奴役
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, compensation principles, mediation of blood-feuds, and judgments at public assemblies. Oaths, witnesses, and material compensation were important, while severe conflicts could lead to combat, exile, or enslavement

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一货币税制，主要通过贡赋、劳役、战利品分成、集市抽成与过路费维持首领权力。缴纳形式多为粮食、畜产、蜂蜜、蜡、皮毛、盐、金属器与服役
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system; power was sustained mainly through tribute, labor service, shares of war booty, market dues, and tolls. Payments were commonly made in grain, animal products, honey, wax, furs, salt, metal goods, and service

religion.zh: 以斯拉夫多神信仰为主，崇拜天神、雷神、土地与水域神灵、祖先与地方圣地；部分边缘地区已受到基督教、草原信仰或其他外来宗教影响
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Slavic polytheism, centered on sky and thunder gods, deities of land and waters, ancestors, and local sacred places; some border regions had already felt the influence of Christianity, steppe beliefs, or other outside faiths

philosophy.zh: 缺乏成体系的书面哲学，思想主要体现为口传传统、祖先崇敬、部族荣誉、互助义务与自然循环观念
philosophy.en: There was no developed written philosophical system; ideas were expressed through oral tradition, ancestor reverence, tribal honor, obligations of mutual aid, and a worldview tied to natural cycles

writingSystem.zh: 尚无通行的本土书写体系，信息主要靠口述、记忆、符号记号与外来文字接触传播；在与拜占庭、法兰克及草原世界接触地带，偶见希腊文、拉丁文或符文式记号
writingSystem.en: There was no widely used native writing system; information was mainly transmitted orally, by memory, symbolic marks, and contact with foreign scripts. In zones interacting with Byzantium, the Franks, and the steppe, Greek, Latin, or runic-style markings could occasionally be encountered

culturalAchievements.zh: 文化成就主要体现在口头史诗、婚礼与葬礼仪式、木构堡寨、土木防御工事、陶器、织物、金属饰件、独木舟与河运技术，以及对森林—河流环境的适应性聚落建设
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements were expressed chiefly in oral epic tradition, wedding and funerary rites, timber strongholds, earth-and-wood defenses, pottery, textiles, metal ornaments, dugout boats and river transport, and settlement forms adapted to forest-river environments

languageFamily.zh: 斯拉夫语族
languageFamily.en: Slavic language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业、畜牧、狩猎、渔猎、采集与地方交换为基础，货币化程度较低。沿河贸易和边境转口可带来额外财富，但总体生产率仍受技术水平、森林覆盖、交通条件与政治分散所限制
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on subsistence agriculture, herding, hunting, fishing, gathering, and local exchange, with a low degree of monetization. Riverine trade and frontier transit generated additional wealth, but overall productivity remained constrained by technology, forest cover, transport limits, and political fragmentation

mainIndustries.zh: 粟麦农业、畜牧、养蜂、皮毛采集、木材与焦炭、铁器锻造、陶器、纺织、盐贸易、河运与奴隶贸易
mainIndustries.en: Millet and grain farming, herding, beekeeping, fur gathering, timber and charcoal production, ironworking, pottery, textiles, salt trade, river transport, and slave trading

tradeGoods.zh: 皮毛、蜂蜜、蜂蜡、奴隶、琥珀、木材、兽皮、盐、铁器、陶器与牲畜
tradeGoods.en: Furs, honey, beeswax, slaves, amber, timber, hides, salt, iron goods, pottery, and livestock

name.en: Weighed silver and payment in kind

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要表现为土地使用权、谷物储备、牲畜、蜂箱、工具、陶器与木屋；富裕家族则拥有更多牲畜、铁制武器、饰物、俘虏与贸易联系
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed in access to land, grain stores, livestock, beehives, tools, pottery, and timber houses; richer families possessed more animals, iron weapons, ornaments, captives, and trade connections

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给与部族再分配为核心的混合经济，辅以酋长贡赋、互惠交换、季节性集市与长距离河道贸易
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy centered on village subsistence and tribal redistribution, supplemented by chieftainly tribute, reciprocal exchange, seasonal markets, and long-distance river trade

source.en: Tribal tribute and collections in kind
Labor dues and commuted military service
Trade tolls and river crossing dues
Shares of booty and raiding proceeds
Gifts from dependents and alliance exchanges

category.zh: 宴飨、赠礼与政治再分配
堡寨、仓储与道路渡口维护
category.en: Stronghold, storage, road, and crossing maintenance
Diplomatic marriages, alliances, and ransom payments

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“国库”并非集中式财政机构，而是分散在各公爵与酋长手中的银锭、外来硬币、武器、牲畜、谷仓存粮、皮毛与礼品储备
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a centralized fiscal institution but a dispersed reserve of silver, foreign coins, weapons, livestock, grain stores, furs, and gifts held by different dukes and chieftains

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持战士忠诚、确保部族团结与储备歉年物资为主，倾向通过再分配、宴飨与礼物巩固权威，而非建立固定税率的官僚财政
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining warrior loyalty, preserving tribal cohesion, and storing resources for poor harvests, relying more on redistribution, feasts, and gifts than on a bureaucratic regime of fixed taxation

description.zh: 由首领直属战士组成，装备较好，承担护卫、突袭、镇压叛离与指挥核心任务
description.en: Composed of the rulers' personal warriors, better equipped than most, serving as guards, raiders, enforcers, and the command nucleus

description.zh: 由自由农民在战争时征发，使用矛、斧、盾、弓与简单护具，适合森林、沼泽与堡寨防御作战
description.en: Raised from free peasants in wartime, armed with spears, axes, shields, bows, and simple protection, well suited to fighting in forests, marshes, and stronghold defenses

name.en: Mounted Scouts and Frontier Allied Horsemen

description.zh: 数量有限，来自较富裕首领、边地部落或与草原民族接触地带，主要用于侦察、追击、信使与机动作战
description.en: Limited in number, drawn from wealthier leaders, frontier tribes, or areas in contact with steppe peoples, mainly used for scouting, pursuit, messaging, and mobile warfare

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各部公爵或酋长在战时临时推举盟主
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; leading dukes or chieftains could be chosen as temporary war leaders in time of conflict

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁矛、斧、木盾、弓箭、简易锁甲或皮甲、木土堡寨、壕沟与河道机动为主。攻城能力有限，更擅长伏击、围困、小规模突袭与地形防御
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spears, axes, wooden shields, bows, simple mail or leather armor, timber-earth forts, ditches, and river mobility. Siege capacity was limited; forces were better at ambush, encirclement, small-scale raiding, and terrain-based defense

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自阿瓦尔余部、保加尔扩张、法兰克东进、草原骑马民族袭扰、邻近部族竞争以及内部继承与联盟破裂
threats.en: Main threats came from Avar remnants, Bulgarian expansion, Frankish eastward pressure, raids by steppe horse peoples, rivalry among neighboring tribes, and internal succession disputes or alliance breakdowns

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布广泛而稀疏，多聚居于河谷、森林边缘、湖沼周边与可耕地带。多数人居于小型村落和木土堡寨附近，区域差异显著
populationDescription.en: The population was widely dispersed and relatively sparse, concentrated in river valleys, forest margins, lakeside-marsh regions, and arable zones. Most people lived in small villages and near timber-earth strongholds, with strong regional variation

name.zh: 诺夫哥罗德前身北方贸易点

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括部族贵族与公爵家族、战士随从、自由农民、工匠与商旅、祭祀与占卜者，以及少量奴仆、债役者和战俘
socialClasses.en: Major social groups included tribal nobles and ducal families, warrior retainers, free peasants, craftsmen and traders, ritual specialists and diviners, and a smaller group of servants, debt bondsmen, and war captives

allies.zh: 无持久统一盟友体系，常与邻近斯拉夫部族、部分草原势力或地方边境政权缔结临时婚盟、互市与军事合作
allies.en: There was no permanent unified alliance system; temporary marriage alliances, trading arrangements, and military cooperation were often made with neighboring Slavic tribes, some steppe groups, or nearby frontier polities

enemies.zh: 敌对关系因地区而异，常见对手包括保加尔、阿瓦尔残余、法兰克边区势力、敌对斯拉夫部落与草原袭扰者
enemies.en: Enemies varied by region, but frequent opponents included the Bulgars, Avar remnants, Frankish frontier powers, rival Slavic tribes, and steppe raiders

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策务实而地方化，以保全部族自主、控制贸易通道、缔结婚盟、交换人质与在强邻之间周旋为主要目标
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic and highly local, aimed at preserving tribal autonomy, controlling trade routes, arranging marriage alliances, exchanging hostages, and maneuvering between stronger neighbors

era.zh: 早期中世纪
era.en: Early Middle Ages

keyInnovations.zh: 木土堡寨营建、铁器农具与武器普及、独木舟与河运网络、轮作与刀耕火种结合、养蜂与蜂蜡生产、森林环境下的聚落防御经验
keyInnovations.en: Timber-earth fortification building, broader use of iron tools and weapons, dugout-boat and river transport networks, mixed rotation and slash-and-burn cultivation, beekeeping and wax production, and defensive settlement practices adapted to forest environments

strengths.zh: 优势在于人口基数尚可、地域广阔、森林与河流地形有利防御、社会韧性强、战时动员灵活，并可依赖多样化生计和边境贸易获取资源
strengths.en: Strengths included a respectable population base, wide territory, defensible forest-and-river terrain, strong social resilience, flexible wartime mobilization, and access to resources through diverse subsistence strategies and frontier trade

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分裂、缺乏统一常备政权与文字行政、财政分散、城市稀少、攻坚能力有限，且常受邻国干预与内部争斗削弱
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses lay in political fragmentation, the absence of a unified permanent state and written administration, dispersed finances, limited urbanization, weak siege capacity, and vulnerability to intervention by neighbors and internal feuding

outlook.zh: 750年前后局势总体稳定，但长期前景取决于若干部族能否整合为更稳固的公国与王权。随着贸易增长、堡寨中心强化以及与法兰克、拜占庭、保加尔和草原世界的接触加深，未来数代中可能出现更集中的早期国家形态
outlook.en: Around 750 the overall situation was stable, but the long-term outlook depended on whether some tribes could consolidate into more durable duchies and kingships. With growing trade, stronger fortified centers, and deeper contact with the Franks, Byzantium, Bulgaria, and the steppe world, more centralized early state forms were likely to emerge over the coming generations

description.zh: 中欧与东欧森林地带分布的多支斯拉夫部族与早期公国。
description.en: A range of Slavic tribes and early duchies across Central and Eastern Europe.

ruler.en: Various local chieftains and petty kings

dynasty.en: Local royal lines and chiefly families

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族王权体制，以地方酋长、王族家系与公众集会共同维系统治
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal kingship system in which local chieftains, royal lineages, and public assemblies jointly sustained rule

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王族与大首领、随从武士、自由农民、工匠商人、半自由民与奴隶构成；地方共同体和亲族纽带比抽象国家机构更重要
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of royal families and great chieftains, warrior retainers, free farmers, artisans and traders, semi-free dependents, and thralls; local communities and kinship ties mattered more than abstract state institutions

rulingClass.zh: 王族、小王、地方酋长及其武士随从阶层
rulingClass.en: Royal lineages, petty kings, local chieftains, and their warrior retinues

succession.zh: 以世袭为主，但需获得地方精英与集会认可；兄终弟及、宗族内竞争和分封并存
succession.en: Primarily hereditary, but requiring acceptance by local elites and assemblies; succession could involve brother-to-brother transfer, intra-dynastic rivalry, and partition among kin

structure.zh: 750年前后的丹麦并非统一国家，而是日德兰、菲英、西兰及周边岛屿上的多个权力中心。统治依赖大厅政治、馈赠关系、海陆交通节点控制以及地方集会裁决，而非固定官僚机构。
structure.en: Around 750, Denmark was not a unified state but a set of power centers across Jutland, Funen, Zealand, and nearby islands. Rule depended on hall-based politics, gift exchange, control of maritime and land routes, and judgments of local assemblies rather than a fixed bureaucracy.

function.zh: 管理首领居所、宴饮、贡礼分配、接待使者与维持首领威望
function.en: Managed the ruler's residence, feasting, gift distribution, reception of envoys, and maintenance of chiefly prestige

function.zh: 组织地方集会、见证誓言、调解赔偿、确认地产与习惯法判决
function.en: Organized local assemblies, witnessed oaths, mediated compensation, and confirmed land claims and customary-law judgments

function.zh: 征集实物贡赋、管理粮食与畜产储备、为宴饮和军事动员提供物资
function.en: Collected tribute in kind, managed stored grain and livestock, and supplied feasts and military mobilization

function.zh: 在里伯等港市维持秩序、征收市税、监督度量与接待外来商人
function.en: Maintained order at trading places such as Ribe, collected market dues, supervised weights and measures, and received foreign merchants

function.zh: 在发生冲突时召集随从武士和自由民、组织船只与边境防务
function.en: Mustered retainers and free men in times of conflict and organized ships and frontier defense

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村社、百户区式聚落网络、酋长领地和区域集会为基础。没有成熟的郡县官僚体系，行政多由首领亲信、地主和法律见证人承担。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on village communities, settlement networks, chiefly domains, and regional assemblies. There was no mature county-style bureaucracy; administration was handled by retainers, landholders, and legal witnesses.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为口传习惯法，以赔偿、和解、誓言、担保人与集会裁断为核心。杀伤、盗窃、侮辱与财产争议通常以罚金和血亲协商处理，若调解失败则可能引发私斗。
legalSystem.en: Law was mainly oral customary law centered on compensation, settlement, oaths, sureties, and assembly judgments. Homicide, theft, insult, and property disputes were typically handled through fines and kin negotiation; failed mediation could lead to feud.

taxationSystem.zh: 尚无统一成文税制。统治者收入来自实物贡赋、港口与市场收费、司法罚金、战利品分配、从属地区贡献及对交通要道的控制。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified written tax code. Rulers drew income from tribute in kind, port and market dues, legal fines, shares of plunder, contributions from dependent areas, and control of key routes.

religion.zh: 北欧多神信仰占主导，崇奉奥丁、托尔、弗雷等神祇，并伴有祖先崇拜、圣地祭祀、季节性献祭与埋葬礼仪；基督教影响尚极其有限。
religion.en: Norse polytheism predominated, with devotion to deities such as Odin, Thor, and Freyr, alongside ancestor veneration, sacred-site worship, seasonal sacrifices, and funerary rites; Christian influence remained extremely limited.

philosophy.zh: 无成体系哲学传统，但社会价值强调荣誉、互惠、勇武、守誓、家族声望与命运观念，伦理多通过诗歌、传说与法律习俗表达。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical tradition, but social values emphasized honor, reciprocity, courage, oath-keeping, family reputation, and ideas of fate, with ethics expressed through poetry, legend, and customary law.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用古弗萨克与向青年弗萨克过渡时期的如尼文字，书写规模有限，多见于短铭文、器物、纪念与标记用途；口述传统远比书写重要。
writingSystem.en: The main script was the runic tradition, in transition from Elder Futhark to Younger Futhark, used on a limited scale for short inscriptions, objects, memorials, and marks; oral tradition remained far more important than writing.

culturalAchievements.zh: 里伯等早期贸易城镇的形成、精致金属工艺、珠饰与骨角器制作、船只建造技术、长屋建筑、口头英雄传统和如尼铭刻，标志着丹麦社会正向更高整合度与更广阔海上世界迈进。
culturalAchievements.en: The rise of early trading towns such as Ribe, refined metalwork, bead and bone-antler craftsmanship, shipbuilding techniques, longhouse architecture, oral heroic traditions, and runic inscription marked Danish society's movement toward greater integration and a wider maritime world.

languageFamily.zh: 北日耳曼语支，使用古诺斯语早期方言
languageFamily.en: North Germanic, with early Old Norse dialects

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和畜牧业为基础，辅以渔业、手工业和区域贸易。按8世纪北欧部族社会标准属中等偏上，因控制北海—波罗的海通道和里伯等港市而具有增长潜力，但整体货币化程度较低。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on subsistence agriculture and livestock raising, supplemented by fishing, handicrafts, and regional trade. By eighth-century northern standards it was moderately strong, with growth potential thanks to control of North Sea–Baltic routes and emporia such as Ribe, though monetization remained low.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧业、渔业、制船、铁器锻造、骨角器制作、纺织、琥珀与珠饰加工、区域转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, livestock husbandry, fishing, shipbuilding, ironworking, bone and antler craft, textiles, amber and bead working, and regional transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 皮毛、兽皮、琥珀、蜂蜡、奴隶、铁器、角骨制品、纺织品、磨石、盐鱼、畜产品，以及输入的玻璃珠、酒、精制金属器和奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Furs, hides, amber, beeswax, slaves, iron goods, horn and bone products, textiles, whetstones, salted fish, animal products, and imported glass beads, wine, fine metalware, and luxury goods

name.en: Weighed silver and mixed media of exchange

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、牛羊猪、工具、船只份额、谷物储备和亲族网络。少数首领家庭拥有金银饰物、输入奢侈品与更多奴隶和随从。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was embodied in land-use rights, cattle, sheep, pigs, tools, shares in boats, grain stores, and kin connections. A small number of chiefly families held silver and gold ornaments, imported luxuries, and larger numbers of dependents and thralls.

unit.en: grams of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、庄园式首领经济、贡赋、馈赠交换与季节性市场贸易相结合的混合经济。财富流通常通过宴饮、婚盟、赔偿、战利品与长途交换实现。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining household production, chiefly estate-style management, tribute, gift exchange, and seasonal market trade. Wealth circulated through feasting, marriage alliances, compensation payments, plunder, and long-distance exchange.

source.en: Tribute in kind and dependent contributions
Port dues, market fees, and transit levies
Legal fines and shares of compensation

source.zh: 战争战利品与突发征集

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中于单一机构，而是分散保存在首领大厅、神圣场所、埋藏银器与流动贡物中。财富多以贵金属饰物、称量银、武器、牲畜和储粮形态存在。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not centralized in a single institution but dispersed among chiefly halls, sacred sites, buried silver, and movable tribute. Wealth existed in precious-metal ornaments, weighed silver, weapons, livestock, and stored grain.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持首领威望与联盟网络，而非追求制度化税收最大化。收入往往再分配为宴饮、礼物、防务和港市经营，以换取忠诚与影响力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed more at preserving chiefly prestige and alliance networks than at maximizing institutionalized taxation. Revenue was often redistributed through feasting, gifts, defense, and port management to secure loyalty and influence.

description.zh: 由各地小王和大酋长维持的核心战斗力量，装备较好，擅长近战、护卫与突袭。
description.en: The core fighting force maintained by petty kings and major chieftains, generally better equipped and suited for close combat, guard duty, and raiding.

description.zh: 战时由自由民临时集结，使用矛、盾、斧和短剑，防卫聚落、海岸与通道。
description.en: Temporarily mustered in wartime from free farmers, armed with spears, shields, axes, and short swords to defend settlements, coasts, and routes.

description.zh: 依托中小型船只执行沿海巡逻、运输、护航与局部海上冲突，是日后维京海上力量的前身。
description.en: Using small and medium-sized vessels for coastal patrol, transport, escort, and limited naval fighting, they formed the precursor to later Viking seaborne power.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅；由各王与酋长分别统兵
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; each king or chieftain led his own forces

technology.zh: 以铁制矛头、剑、斧、木盾、锁子甲少量使用和浅吃水船只为主。尚无后世成熟长船体系，但造船、航海与沿海机动能力已相当突出。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spearheads, swords, axes, wooden shields, limited mail use, and shallow-draft vessels. The fully developed later longship system had not yet emerged, but shipbuilding, seamanship, and coastal mobility were already notable.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自内部王族竞争、边界部族冲突、与萨克森地区的摩擦，以及与南斯堪的纳维亚和波罗的海沿岸势力争夺贸易与海上通道控制。
threats.en: Primary threats came from internal dynastic rivalry, frontier tribal conflict, friction with Saxon regions, and competition with southern Scandinavian and Baltic coastal powers for trade and sea-route control.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于日德兰半岛、菲英、西兰及若干丹麦岛屿，聚落以农村长屋社区为主。人口密度不高，但海岸、河口与贸易节点的聚集趋势已十分明显。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed mainly across Jutland, Funen, Zealand, and several Danish islands, with settlement centered on rural longhouse communities. Density was low, but concentration around coasts, estuaries, and trade nodes was increasingly evident.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族和大酋长，其下是武士随从和地方有力自由民；多数人口为自由农民、牧民和渔民；另有工匠、商人、半自由依附者和奴隶。社会流动有限，但战功、婚盟和贸易成功可提高地位。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood royal families and great chieftains, below them warrior retainers and influential free landholders; most people were free farmers, herders, and fishers; there were also artisans, traders, semi-free dependents, and thralls. Social mobility was limited, but military success, marriage alliances, and trade could improve status.

allies.zh: 无稳定统一盟友体系；通常与部分弗里西亚商人网络、北海沿岸社群及某些斯堪的纳维亚邻近首领保持临时合作关系
allies.en: There was no stable unified alliance system; temporary cooperation existed with parts of the Frisian merchant networks, North Sea coastal communities, and some neighboring Scandinavian chiefs

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括南方萨克森势力、竞争性的丹麦与瑞典地方首领，以及任何威胁贸易口岸和海上通道的邻近部族集团
enemies.en: Main adversaries included southern Saxon powers, rival Danish and Swedish local rulers, and any neighboring tribal groups threatening trade harbors and maritime routes

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以务实为主，侧重婚盟、交换、互市、质子安排和局部武力展示。尚未形成统一对外战略，但各首领都试图扩大对港口、航道和边境地区的控制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was pragmatic, emphasizing marriage alliances, exchange, regulated trade, hostage arrangements, and limited displays of force. There was no unified external strategy, but individual rulers sought to expand control over ports, sea lanes, and frontier zones.

era.zh: 北欧晚期日耳曼铁器时代向维京时代过渡期
era.en: Late Germanic Iron Age to pre-Viking transitional period in Northern Europe

keyInnovations.zh: 高质量铁器锻造、熟练木工与船体建造、海岸航行技术、里伯式商业聚落组织、如尼铭刻传统，以及长屋与仓储结构的改进
keyInnovations.en: High-quality ironworking, skilled woodworking and hull construction, coastal navigation techniques, Ribe-style commercial settlement organization, the runic inscription tradition, and improvements in longhouse and storage structures

strengths.zh: 控制北海与波罗的海之间的重要门户，拥有坚韧的自由民社会、成熟的海岸航行能力、兴起中的港市经济和强烈的首领—随从纽带。这些因素为稍后的维京扩张奠定了基础。
strengths.en: The Danish lands controlled an important gateway between the North Sea and Baltic, possessed a resilient free-farmer society, developed coastal seafaring skills, emerging port economies, and strong chief-retainer bonds. These factors laid the groundwork for the later Viking expansion.

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分裂，缺乏统一王权和稳定官僚体系，财政与军事动员依赖个人威望和临时联盟；内部争斗容易削弱整体实力，农业基础也容易受气候和收成波动影响。
weaknesses.en: Politics were highly fragmented, lacking a unified monarchy and stable bureaucracy, while fiscal and military mobilization depended on personal prestige and temporary alliances; internal struggles could easily weaken overall strength, and the agrarian base remained vulnerable to climate and harvest fluctuation.

outlook.zh: 若贸易中心继续成长、首领间竞争推动军事与造船革新，并逐步形成更强王权，丹麦地区将在未来一个世纪迅速崛起，成为维京时代最重要的核心区域之一。
outlook.en: If trade centers continued to grow, rivalry among chieftains drove military and shipbuilding innovation, and stronger kingship gradually emerged, the Danish region would rise rapidly within the next century to become one of the principal core areas of the Viking Age.

description.zh: 斯堪的纳维亚南部的早期北欧政体，正迈向维京时代。
description.en: Early Norse polities in southern Scandinavia on the eve of the Viking Age.

governmentForm.zh: 以部族王权、地方首领与民会为基础的分散王国联合体；斯韦阿王在宗教与象征层面地位较高，但对约塔地区与沿海聚落的直接控制有限。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized cluster of tribal kingdoms based on sacral kingship, local chieftains, and popular assemblies; the king of the Svear held elevated religious and symbolic status, but exercised only limited direct control over Geatish districts and coastal settlements.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王族与大首领、自由农民、依附者与奴隶构成。自由农民是武装与生产核心，地方豪强通过馈赠、婚姻和追随者网络维系权力。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around royal families and great chieftains, free farmers, dependents, and thralls. Free farmers formed the core of both production and military service, while local magnates maintained authority through gift-giving, marriage alliances, and retinues.

rulingClass.zh: 王族、祭祀性首领、地方雅尔与大地主家族
rulingClass.en: Royal lineages, sacral leaders, local jarls, and major landholding families

succession.zh: 王位继承并非严格长子继承，通常在王族内部竞争，并需经民会、首领支持及宗教合法性确认。
succession.en: Succession was not strict primogeniture; kingship was often contested within royal kin groups and required recognition by assemblies, chieftains, and sacral legitimacy.

structure.zh: 政体以地区性王权与地方民会并存。乌普萨拉的王权依靠宗教中心、宴饮馈赠和军事追随者维持；各地区由首领主持地方事务，重大事务在庭会或民会中裁决。
structure.en: The polity combined regional kingship with local assemblies. Royal authority at Uppsala rested on cultic centrality, feasting and gift exchange, and military followers; local affairs were managed by chieftains, while major matters were decided in courts or assemblies.

function.zh: 管理王室地产、仓储、贡品、宴饮安排与对追随者的馈赠分配。
function.en: Managed royal estates, storage, tribute goods, feasting arrangements, and the distribution of gifts to followers.

function.zh: 维护乌普萨拉等圣地、组织季节祭祀、管理祭品与宗教仪式中的王权角色。
function.en: Maintained sacred centers such as Uppsala, organized seasonal sacrifices, managed offerings, and upheld the king's role in cult ritual.

function.zh: 协助召集地方民会、记录习惯法裁决、监督赔偿与誓约执行。
function.en: Assisted in convening local assemblies, preserving customary legal decisions, and supervising compensation settlements and oath enforcement.

function.zh: 监管集市、泊船点、通行费、度量习惯及与波罗的海商人的往来。
function.en: Oversaw markets, landing places, tolls, customary measures, and exchanges with Baltic merchants.

function.zh: 维持王家侍从队、征召船队与地方武装，并筹备军事远征。
function.en: Maintained the royal retinue, organized levies of ships and local fighters, and prepared military expeditions.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由各菲尔基、百户区与村社的首领、长者和法律代言人承担。行政分工松散，很多职责与宗教、司法和军事职能重叠。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out chiefly by district leaders, elders, and law-speakers in regional units and village communities. Administrative specialization was limited, and many duties overlapped with cultic, judicial, and military functions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法为基础，由民会裁决，强调誓言、见证、赔偿金和和解。杀人、伤害、财产与婚姻纠纷多以赔偿方式处理，严重冲突可导致放逐或私斗升级。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice and decided at assemblies, emphasizing oaths, witnesses, compensation, and settlement. Homicide, injury, property, and marriage disputes were commonly resolved through compensation, while severe conflicts could lead to outlawry or escalating feuds.

taxationSystem.zh: 尚无高度制度化的普遍税制。王权收入主要来自贡赋、宴饮征收、港埠通行费、战利品、宗教中心供奉与王室地产产出。
taxationSystem.en: There was no highly systematized universal tax regime. Royal income came primarily from tribute, levies for feasting and hosting, harbor tolls, war booty, offerings at cult centers, and produce from royal estates.

religion.zh: 北欧多神信仰，以奥丁、托尔、弗雷等神祇崇拜为核心，并重视祖先、圣林、墓冢和季节性献祭。乌普萨拉是重要宗教中心。
religion.en: Norse polytheism centered on the worship of gods such as Odin, Thor, and Freyr, together with reverence for ancestors, sacred groves, burial mounds, and seasonal sacrifice. Uppsala was a major cult center.

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成体系的哲学传统，价值观体现于口述诗歌与习惯伦理中，强调荣誉、忠诚、命运、勇武、慷慨与家族声望。
philosophy.en: No formal philosophical school existed; values were embodied in oral poetry and customary ethics, stressing honor, loyalty, fate, courage, generosity, and family reputation.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用古北欧如尼文字，刻写于石、木、骨与金属之上；文字用途多为纪念、标记、宗教和象征性表达，行政记录仍以口述为主。
writingSystem.en: The primary script was the older Norse runic tradition, inscribed on stone, wood, bone, and metal; writing was used mainly for memorial, marking, cultic, and symbolic purposes, while administration remained largely oral.

culturalAchievements.zh: 在船只建造、金工、木雕、口头诗歌、墓葬礼制与节庆文化方面具有鲜明特色。动物纹装饰与武器、饰品工艺反映出高度审美与跨区域交流。
culturalAchievements.en: Distinctive achievements included boatbuilding, metalworking, wood carving, oral poetry, funerary ritual, and festive culture. Animal-style ornamentation and the craftsmanship of weapons and jewelry reflected both high aesthetic standards and broad regional contacts.

languageFamily.zh: 北日耳曼语支
languageFamily.en: North Germanic

unit.en: grams of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业、畜牧、渔猎、铁器生产和地方交换为主，辅以波罗的海贸易。总体财富水平不高，但在毛皮、铁、琥珀与奴隶贸易中逐渐形成区域性优势。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence agriculture, animal husbandry, fishing and hunting, iron production, and local exchange, supplemented by Baltic trade. Overall wealth levels were modest, but the realms were developing regional advantages in furs, iron, amber, and slave trading.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、渔业、沼铁与铁器冶炼、木材加工、造船、毛皮采集、琥珀与地方手工业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, fishing, bog iron extraction and smithing, timber working, boatbuilding, fur gathering, amber exchange, and local handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 出口毛皮、铁、磨刀石、琥珀、蜂蜡、蜂蜜、皮革、猎隼与部分奴隶；输入盐、玻璃珠、精制金属制品、葡萄酒、织物与奢侈饰品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included furs, iron, whetstones, amber, beeswax, honey, hides, hunting falcons, and some slaves; imports included salt, glass beads, refined metal goods, wine, textiles, and luxury ornaments.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距明显。多数自由农户拥有住房、农具、牲畜与小块耕地；首领家族控制更大的土地、船只、侍从和进口奢侈品。奴隶无独立财产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Most free farming households possessed dwellings, tools, livestock, and small plots, while chieftainly families controlled larger lands, ships, retainers, and imported luxuries. Thralls held no independent property.

unit.en: grams of silver per worker per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、地方互惠、首领再分配和有限长途贸易并存的早期混合经济。政治权力依赖财富再分配，尤其是通过宴饮、礼物和战利品分发。
economicSystem.en: An early mixed economy combining household production, local reciprocity, chieftainly redistribution, and limited long-distance trade. Political power depended heavily on redistributing wealth, especially through feasts, gifts, and the distribution of booty.

category.en: Cult observance and sacred site maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财富多以银器、碎银、金饰、武器、牲畜和可分发货物形式保存，而非统一货币金库。储备规模有限，但足以支撑季节性宴饮、结盟和小规模军事行动。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal wealth was stored largely as silver objects, hacksilver, gold ornaments, weapons, livestock, and distributable goods rather than as a uniform monetary treasury. Reserves were limited but sufficient to support seasonal feasting, alliance-building, and small-scale military action.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持首领网络与宗教中心声望，而非扩大官僚征收。盈余通常用于馈赠、婚盟、圣地活动和强化王权象征。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed primarily at maintaining chieftainly networks and the prestige of cult centers rather than expanding bureaucratic extraction. Surpluses were typically used for gifts, marriage alliances, sacred observances, and reinforcing the symbols of kingship.

description.zh: 由国王与大首领维持的常备追随者组成，装备较好，承担护卫、执法与战时核心突击任务。
description.en: Composed of permanent followers maintained by kings and major chieftains, better equipped than ordinary levies, serving as guards, enforcers, and the core assault force in war.

description.zh: 由各地区自由农民按义务集结，持矛、盾、斧和部分剑，适合短期地方防御与集体远征。
description.en: Raised from free farmers by regional obligation, armed with spears, shields, axes, and some swords, suitable for short-term local defense and collective expeditions.

description.zh: 依托长船和沿海航行传统形成的海上力量，用于波罗的海航运护卫、突袭与跨海联络。
description.en: A maritime force based on longboats and coastal navigation traditions, used for Baltic convoying, raiding, and overseas communication.

commanderInChief.zh: 乌普萨拉之王及各地首领联席统帅
commanderInChief.en: The King at Uppsala together with regional chieftain-commanders

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制矛、剑、斧、圆盾、锁子甲与木制长船为主。尚未形成高度标准化军制，但造船、近岸航海和小规模快速突袭能力突出。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron spears, swords, axes, round shields, mail shirts, and wooden longboats. There was no highly standardized military system, but boatbuilding, coastal navigation, and rapid small-scale raiding capability were notable strengths.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与早期丹麦诸王、波罗的海沿岸部族及内部首领竞争相关的冲突；更大的风险常是继承争端、私斗与贸易路线争夺，而非帝国式征服。
threats.en: The main threats came from conflict with early Danish kings, Baltic coastal peoples, and rival internal chieftains; the greater dangers were often succession disputes, feuds, and competition over trade routes rather than imperial-style conquest.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布稀疏，主要集中在梅拉伦湖地区、约塔兰的可耕地带及沿海聚落。聚落以村庄、长屋农场和季节性贸易点为主。
populationDescription.en: The population was thinly distributed, concentrated mainly around the Mälaren region, arable parts of Götaland, and coastal settlements. Settlements consisted chiefly of villages, longhouse farmsteads, and seasonal trading sites.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王族与大首领、地方贵族与武装追随者、自由农民、手工业者与商人、依附佃户以及奴隶。自由农民拥有较强公共地位，是民会和兵役的主体。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included royal families and great chieftains, local nobles and armed retainers, free farmers, artisans and traders, dependent tenants, and thralls. Free farmers held strong public standing and formed the basis of assembly politics and military obligation.

allies.zh: 缺乏稳定国家级盟友，更多依靠与波罗的海沿岸部族、芬兰湾地区社群及部分斯拉夫和芬兰语族群体的临时贸易与婚盟关系。
allies.en: There were no stable state-level allies; relations instead relied on temporary trade and marriage ties with Baltic coastal peoples, communities around the Gulf of Finland, and some Slavic and Finnic groups.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是早期丹麦诸王、波罗的海竞争部族以及本地敌对王族。敌友关系经常随婚盟、贸易和首领更替而变化。
enemies.en: The principal rivals were the early Danish kingdoms, competing Baltic tribes, and hostile local dynasties. Relations of enmity and alliance frequently shifted with marriages, trade, and changes in leadership.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制区域贸易节点、维持航路安全、通过婚盟与馈赠建立声望为主。尚未形成远距离常驻外交，但已具备面向波罗的海扩展影响的倾向。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling regional trade nodes, maintaining navigational security, and building prestige through marriage alliances and gift exchange. No system of long-distance permanent diplomacy existed yet, but there was a growing tendency to project influence across the Baltic.

era.zh: 北欧铁器时代晚期至维京时代前夜
era.en: Late Nordic Iron Age to the eve of the Viking Age

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括适合浅海与河口航行的船体设计、铁器锻造、木工连接技术、毛皮与皮革加工、骨角工艺以及如尼铭刻传统。其技术重点在实用性、机动性和寒冷环境适应。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included hull designs suited to shallow seas and estuaries, iron smithing, advanced woodworking joints, fur and leather processing, bone and antler craft, and the runic inscription tradition. Technical strengths lay in practicality, mobility, and adaptation to cold environments.

strengths.zh: 该文明的优势在于强健的自由农民社会、良好的船只制造能力、宗教中心带来的凝聚力，以及逐渐扩大的波罗的海贸易网络。地方社会韧性较高，适应寒冷环境的能力突出。
strengths.en: The civilization's strengths lay in its robust free-farmer society, strong boatbuilding capacity, the cohesion provided by cult centers, and an expanding Baltic trade network. Local communities were resilient and well adapted to a cold environment.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于政治分散、继承不稳定、行政能力有限、财政资源薄弱，以及各地区间协调困难。王权对边远地区的控制依赖个人威望而非制度。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were political fragmentation, unstable succession, limited administrative capacity, thin fiscal resources, and difficulty coordinating across regions. Royal authority over peripheral areas depended more on personal prestige than institutions.

outlook.zh: 到750年前后，斯韦阿与约塔诸王国处于稳定但尚未统一的阶段。随着贸易增长、船只技术成熟与首领竞争加剧，这一地区很可能在未来数十年内进入更活跃的海外扩张与政治整合时期。
outlook.en: Around 750 CE, the Svear and Geatish realms were stable but not yet unified. With growing trade, maturing ship technology, and intensifying competition among chieftains, the region was likely to enter a period of more active overseas expansion and political consolidation in the coming decades.

description.zh: 斯堪的纳维亚东部的北欧部族王国，依托贸易与祭祀中心发展。
description.en: Norse tribal kingdoms in eastern Scandinavia developing around trade and cult centers.

governmentForm.zh: 以基督教君主为中心的世袭王国，王权在高原核心地区较强，在边缘地区依赖地方贵族与教会合作施政。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Christian monarchy centered on the king, with stronger royal authority in the core highlands and governance in peripheral regions relying on cooperation with local nobles and the church.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与贵族、教会神职人员、地方首领、自由农民、工匠与商人以及附属劳役人口构成；高原农业定居人口为主体，边地则保留较多部族性结构。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and nobility, clergy, local chiefs, free peasants, artisans and merchants, and dependent laboring populations; settled agricultural highlanders formed the majority, while frontier regions retained stronger tribal structures.

rulingClass.zh: 阿克苏姆王室、高原贵族地主、军政首领以及与王权密切合作的基督教高级教士。
rulingClass.en: The Aksumite royal family, highland landed nobility, military-administrative chiefs, and senior Christian clergy closely aligned with the crown.

succession.zh: 王位一般由王室内部世袭继承，但具体继承规则并不完全固定，常受贵族、宫廷派系与教会支持影响。
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the royal line, though succession was not fully fixed and was often shaped by noble factions, court politics, and ecclesiastical support.

structure.zh: 750年前后的阿克苏姆已明显衰落，中央政府规模较早期缩小。政体仍以国王为核心，宫廷兼具行政、司法、军事与礼仪职能；地方治理多经由贵族家族、驻地官员、附庸首领和教会网络执行。
structure.en: By around 750, Aksum had clearly declined and its central government was smaller than in its earlier peak. The polity remained king-centered, with the court combining administrative, judicial, military, and ceremonial roles; local governance was carried out through noble families, stationed officials, subordinate chiefs, and church networks.

function.zh: 负责王命起草、宫廷仪式、使节接待、诏令发布与王室档案保管。
function.en: Handled drafting royal orders, court ceremony, reception of envoys, promulgation of decrees, and custody of royal records.

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、蜂蜜、纺织品及其他实物贡赋，并管理王室仓储与转运。
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, honey, textiles, and other payments in kind, and managed royal storage and redistribution.

function.zh: 协调高原地方长官、边地首领与附属群体的效忠、贡纳与治安事务。
function.en: Coordinated loyalty, tribute, and order among highland governors, frontier chiefs, and subordinate groups.

function.zh: 组织王家亲兵、征召部队、边境守备、道路护卫与突发军事动员。
function.en: Organized the royal guard, levies, frontier defense, road security, and emergency military mobilization.

function.zh: 协调王权与教会关系，处理婚姻、继承、土地争端、宣誓与部分刑事案件。
function.en: Managed relations between crown and church, and handled marriage, inheritance, land disputes, oaths, and some criminal cases.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以高原核心区的王室任命官员和世袭贵族为主，外围地区则多依赖地方首领、部族领袖和教会机构间接统治。税赋和兵役征发常通过社区与宗族网络完成。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on royal appointees and hereditary nobles in the highland core, while outer regions were governed more indirectly through local chiefs, tribal leaders, and church institutions. Taxation and military levies were often organized through community and kinship networks.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系融合君主裁断、习惯法和基督教规范。土地、婚姻、继承及誓约常受教会影响，地方纠纷多由贵族、长老或神职人员仲裁，重大案件则上达王廷。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal judgment, customary law, and Christian norms. Land, marriage, inheritance, and oaths were strongly influenced by the church; local disputes were often arbitrated by nobles, elders, or clergy, while major cases went to the royal court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以实物征收为主，包括粮食、牲畜、蜂蜜、盐、纺织品及劳役义务；贸易税与通道税在王国早期更重要，到750年时其比重已下降，但仍对红海及内陆商路节点有意义。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was primarily in kind, including grain, livestock, honey, salt, textiles, and labor obligations; customs and transit dues had been more important in earlier centuries, and by 750 their share had declined, though they still mattered at Red Sea and inland trade nodes.

religion.zh: 以东方基督教传统下的阿克苏姆基督教为国教，受亚历山大科普特教会影响极深；乡间地区仍可能残存前基督教习俗与地方性信仰。
religion.en: Aksumite Christianity, within the Eastern Christian tradition and deeply shaped by the Coptic Church of Alexandria, was the state religion; in rural areas some pre-Christian customs and local beliefs likely persisted.

philosophy.zh: 思想核心以基督教神学、王权神授观念、修道主义伦理和圣经释义为主，同时保留本地王室传统与长者裁判文化。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual life centered on Christian theology, sacral kingship, monastic ethics, and biblical exegesis, while also preserving local royal traditions and elder-based adjudicatory culture.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用吉兹文书写系统；到此时期吉兹语已主要作为礼仪、宗教和书面语言，口语层面逐渐与后来的埃塞俄比亚闪米特诸语分化。
writingSystem.en: The main script was Ge'ez; by this period Ge'ez was increasingly a liturgical, religious, and written language, while spoken usage was gradually diverging toward later Ethiopian Semitic languages.

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿克苏姆延续了石碑纪念传统、早期教堂建筑、基督教石刻与手抄文献传统；其最持久的文化遗产是将吉兹文、王权和基督教制度结合为埃塞俄比亚高原文明核心。
culturalAchievements.en: Aksum preserved traditions of monumental stelae, early church building, Christian stone carving, and manuscript culture; its most enduring achievement was the fusion of Ge'ez literacy, kingship, and Christianity into the core of highland Ethiopian civilization.

languageFamily.zh: 主要书面语言吉兹语属亚非语系闪米特语族埃塞俄比亚支；王国内同时存在库施特语与其他地方语言。
languageFamily.en: The principal written language, Ge'ez, belonged to the Afroasiatic family, Semitic branch, Ethiopian subgroup; Cushitic and other local languages were also spoken within the kingdom.

gdpDescription.zh: 阿克苏姆在750年已不复晚古时期作为红海贸易强国的盛况，经济重心转向埃塞俄比亚高原农业、畜牧、地方手工业和区域贸易。总体经济仍具韧性，但货币化程度和远程海贸能力明显低于鼎盛时代。
gdpDescription.en: By 750, Aksum no longer possessed the prosperity of its late antique era as a Red Sea trading power. Its economy had shifted toward highland agriculture, pastoralism, local crafts, and regional exchange. Overall it remained resilient, but monetization and long-distance maritime trade were clearly weaker than at its peak.

mainIndustries.zh: 高原农耕、牲畜饲养、蜂蜜与蜂蜡生产、织物制作、陶器、金属加工、石材建筑和区域商队贸易。
mainIndustries.en: Highland farming, livestock raising, honey and beeswax production, textile making, pottery, metalworking, stone construction, and regional caravan trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牲畜、象牙、奴隶、蜂蜡、皮革、盐、纺织品以及少量金属制品；同时输入奢侈品、珠饰、部分金属器和外来织物。
tradeGoods.en: Grain, livestock, ivory, slaves, beeswax, hides, salt, textiles, and limited metal goods; it also imported luxury items, beads, some metalware, and foreign textiles.

name.zh: 吉兹旧制钱币与称量金属并用

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为耕地使用权、牛羊、谷仓储备、蜂箱和宗族关系，而非大量流通货币。高原核心区农户通常拥有基本生计资料，但抗灾能力有限。
