treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were not held in a single central vault but dispersed among major tribal leaders, town granaries, religious endowments, and local strongholds. Their security depended on alliance networks rather than purely bureaucratic control

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视维持部落忠诚、保障粮仓储备与战时动员能力。税负通常通过谈判调整，以避免激发部族叛离或商路改道
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, prioritizing tribal loyalty, granary reserves, and wartime mobilization. Tax burdens were often negotiated to avoid provoking tribal defection or diversion of trade routes

description.zh: 以轻骑兵为主，是柏柏尔军事核心，擅长突袭、侦察、山地与草原机动作战，常配长矛、短剑、圆盾与少量弓箭
description.en: Mostly light cavalry, the core of Berber warfare, skilled in raiding, reconnaissance, and mobile fighting in mountains and steppe, typically armed with spears, short swords, round shields, and some bows

description.zh: 由农牧民在战时召集，适合守卫山口、村社和要塞，多使用长矛、投枪、盾牌与简易护具
description.en: Raised from farming and pastoral communities in wartime, suited for defending passes, villages, and forts, using spears, javelins, shields, and simple armor

description.zh: 驻守旧城、粮仓、道路关口与绿洲据点，战斗力较稳定但规模有限
description.en: Stationed in old towns, granaries, road gates, and oasis strongpoints, offering steadier but limited military capacity

name.zh: 商队护卫与盟友辅助兵

description.zh: 负责护送商队、巡逻商路，并在大战时补充联军，来源复杂，包括附属部族与雇佣战士
description.en: Protected caravans, patrolled trade routes, and reinforced larger campaigns, drawing from subordinate tribes and hired fighters

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各部落酋长、埃米尔或临时联军领袖分区指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; command was divided among tribal chiefs, amirs, or temporary coalition leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以轻骑机动、山地防御、投枪战术、简易攻城器械和旧罗马—拜占庭遗存工事利用为主。金属甲胄和大型攻城能力有限，但熟悉地形与快速集结是其优势
technology.en: Military technology centered on light cavalry mobility, mountain defense, javelin tactics, simple siege devices, and the reuse of older Roman-Byzantine fortifications. Heavy armor and major siege capacity were limited, but knowledge of terrain and rapid assembly were major strengths

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自倭马亚与新兴阿拔斯势力在北非的权力重组、伊夫里基亚总督的军事征讨、穆斯林伊比利亚与马格里布间海峡战略竞争、部族间内斗，以及干旱导致的迁徙冲突
threats.en: Main threats came from the Umayyad and rising Abbasid struggle for North Africa, military expeditions by governors of Ifriqiya, strategic competition across the straits with Muslim Iberia, intertribal conflict, and migration pressures caused by drought

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于摩洛哥、阿特拉斯山地、沿海平原、草原与部分绿洲地带，聚落形态从旧罗马城市遗址到山地村社和游牧营地并存。人口密度总体不高，但西北部农业区与商路节点较为集中
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across Morocco, the Atlas, coastal plains, steppe, and some oasis zones, with settlement forms ranging from former Roman towns to mountain villages and pastoral camps. Overall density was low, but northwestern agricultural zones and trade nodes were more concentrated

name.zh: 锡吉勒马萨早期商站区

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括部落贵族与酋长家系、宗教人士与法官、自由农民与牧民、商人和工匠、附庸社群、俘虏与少量奴隶。城市居民与山地部族在生活方式和政治归属上差异明显
socialClasses.en: Social classes included tribal nobles and chiefly lineages, religious specialists and judges, free farmers and pastoralists, merchants and artisans, dependent communities, captives, and a limited number of slaves. Urban dwellers and mountain tribes differed sharply in lifestyle and political attachment

allies.zh: 盟友关系高度流动，常见对象包括同盟柏柏尔部族、部分反对地方阿拉伯总督的伊斯兰派系，以及与撒哈拉商路相连的南方伙伴。短期合作常基于共同利益而非长期忠诚
allies.en: Alliances were highly fluid, often involving fellow Berber tribes, Islamic factions opposed to local Arab governors, and southern partners tied to Saharan trade. Cooperation was usually based on immediate interest rather than lasting loyalty

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量包括试图加强直接控制的倭马亚及其继承权力网络、部分伊夫里基亚总督军、敌对柏柏尔部族，以及在税赋和宗教问题上发生冲突的外来驻军
enemies.en: Main enemies included Umayyad and successor power networks seeking tighter control, some armies of Ifriqiyan governors, rival Berber tribes, and foreign garrisons that clashed over taxation and religion

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以务实自治为主：在强敌压境时接受名义宗主权或宗教归附，在中央力量衰弱时恢复独立行动。控制商路、避免被单一外部政权完全吞并，是各邦联的共同目标
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was guided by pragmatic autonomy: accepting nominal overlordship or religious alignment when threatened by stronger powers, then resuming independent action when central authority weakened. Control of trade routes and avoidance of total absorption by any single outside state were common goals

era.zh: 中世纪早期北非部落—伊斯兰过渡时代
era.en: Early medieval North African tribal-Islamic transitional era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与实践包括干旱环境下的蓄水与灌溉方法、山地村社防御工事、轻骑兵机动作战、跨区域商队组织、皮革与羊毛加工，以及对罗马时期道路、桥梁和废弃城址的再利用
keyInnovations.en: Key techniques and practices included water storage and irrigation for dry environments, mountain village fortifications, mobile light cavalry warfare, long-distance caravan organization, leather and wool processing, and the reuse of Roman-era roads, bridges, and abandoned urban sites

strengths.zh: 优势在于地形适应力强、部族动员迅速、骑兵机动优秀、地方自治传统深厚，并掌握连接地中海与撒哈拉的关键商路节点
strengths.en: Strengths included strong adaptation to terrain, rapid tribal mobilization, excellent cavalry mobility, deep traditions of local autonomy, and control of key routes linking the Mediterranean and the Sahara

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于政治分裂、缺乏统一常备官僚与统帅体系、财政征收不稳定、城镇化水平有限，以及容易受宗教分歧、部族竞争和外部帝国干预影响
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included political fragmentation, the absence of a unified standing bureaucracy and command system, unstable revenue extraction, limited urbanization, and vulnerability to religious disputes, tribal rivalry, and intervention by outside empires

outlook.zh: 750年前后局势总体稳定但潜在变动极大。随着倭马亚崩溃和阿拔斯革命冲击北非，柏柏尔地区很可能继续出现以宗教与部族联盟为基础的新政权。若能整合商路与伊斯兰合法性，未来数十年有形成更持久地方王朝的可能
outlook.en: Around 750 the situation was broadly stable but highly volatile beneath the surface. With the collapse of the Umayyads and the Abbasid revolution reshaping North Africa, Berber regions were likely to produce new polities built on religion and tribal alliance. If trade routes and Islamic legitimacy could be consolidated, more durable local dynasties might emerge in the coming decades

description.zh: 西北非广泛分布的柏柏尔部族政权，与阿拉伯统治时合时离。
description.en: Widely distributed Berber polities in northwest Africa, alternately aligned with and resisting Arab rule.

dynasty.zh: 蒂卡尔、卡拉克穆尔及诸城邦王朝
dynasty.en: Dynasties of Tikal, Calakmul, and other city-states

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭神权王权为核心、由众多独立城邦构成的城邦体系
governmentForm.en: A city-state system of numerous independent polities centered on hereditary divine kingship

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈高度等级化结构，顶层为王室与贵族，其下为祭司、书记官、武士与地方领主，再下为工匠、商人、农民与劳役者，另有战俘与奴隶处于最底层。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly stratified, with royal families and nobles at the top, followed by priests, scribes, warriors, and local lords, then artisans, merchants, peasants, and laborers, with captives and slaves at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 王族、贵族宗族、祭司集团与高级军事领袖
rulingClass.en: Royal lineages, noble houses, priestly elites, and senior military leaders

succession.zh: 通常为父系世袭，但因战争、摄政、王位争夺与婚姻联盟，继承实践在各城邦之间并不完全一致。
succession.en: Succession was generally patrilineal and hereditary, though warfare, regencies, dynastic disputes, and marriage alliances created variation among city-states.

structure.zh: 玛雅并无统一帝国官僚机构，而是由相互竞争并结盟的城邦组成。每个城邦以圣主为中心，辅以王室亲族、贵族议事圈、祭司、书记官与附庸地方首领共同治理。
structure.en: The Maya had no single imperial bureaucracy; instead they consisted of competing and allied city-states. Each polity was centered on a holy lord, assisted by royal kin, noble councils, priests, scribes, and subordinate local chiefs.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、贡赋储藏、礼仪安排、王族地产与日常政务协调。
function.en: Managed the court, tribute storage, ceremonial arrangements, royal estates, and coordination of daily governance.

function.zh: 主持宗教仪式、天文观测、历法计算、吉日占卜与合法性宣传。
function.en: Conducted religious rituals, astronomical observations, calendar calculations, divination of auspicious days, and legitimation of rule.

function.zh: 负责象形文字记录、王朝史叙述、贡赋登记、外交文书与碑铭制作。
function.en: Handled hieroglyphic records, dynastic histories, tribute registration, diplomatic texts, and monument inscriptions.

function.zh: 征收玉米、可可、棉布、盐、羽毛与劳役，管理仓储及再分配。
function.en: Collected maize, cacao, cotton cloth, salt, feathers, and labor dues, and managed storage and redistribution.

function.zh: 组织战争动员、监督附庸城镇、安排俘虏处置与维持道路节点安全。
function.en: Organized war mobilization, supervised subordinate towns, arranged handling of captives, and maintained security over route nodes.

localAdmin.zh: 基层由附属城镇、村社与地方首领管理，向核心城市缴纳贡物和劳役。中心对腹地的控制强弱不一，往往通过婚盟、军事威慑和宗教声望维系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by subordinate towns, village communities, and local chiefs who rendered tribute and labor to core cities. Central control over hinterlands varied and was often maintained through marriage alliances, military pressure, and ritual prestige.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要基于习惯法、王权裁断与宗教规范。贵族、平民和战俘适用标准不同，惩罚包括赔偿、劳役、羞辱、处死与祭祀性处置。
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on customary practice, royal judgment, and religious norms. Different standards applied to nobles, commoners, and captives, with punishments including compensation, labor, humiliation, execution, and ritual disposal.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制主要表现为贡赋与徭役，而非统一货币税。常见项目包括玉米、豆类、可可、棉布、盐、陶器、黑曜石制品、羽毛以及建筑与农业劳役。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation took the form primarily of tribute and corvée rather than a uniform money tax. Common obligations included maize, beans, cacao, cotton cloth, salt, ceramics, obsidian goods, feathers, and labor for construction and agriculture.

religion.zh: 玛雅宗教为多神信仰，重视祖先崇拜、王权神圣性、献血仪式与人祭。主要神祇包括玉米神、雨神恰克、太阳神与地下世界相关神灵。
religion.en: Maya religion was polytheistic, emphasizing ancestor veneration, divine kingship, bloodletting rituals, and human sacrifice. Major deities included the Maize God, the rain god Chaac, solar deities, and gods associated with the underworld.

philosophy.zh: 其思想核心并非抽象哲学体系，而是宇宙循环、时间神圣性、人与神的互惠关系及王朝历史在宇宙秩序中的位置。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual core was not an abstract philosophical system but a worldview centered on cyclical cosmos, the sacred nature of time, reciprocity between humans and gods, and the place of dynastic history within cosmic order.

writingSystem.zh: 使用成熟的玛雅象形文字体系，兼具表意与音节功能，用于碑铭、陶器、壁画、法典式文献与王朝纪事。
writingSystem.en: They used a mature Maya hieroglyphic system combining logographic and syllabic elements, employed in inscriptions, ceramics, murals, codex-style manuscripts, and dynastic records.

culturalAchievements.zh: 在金字塔神庙建筑、石碑艺术、壁画、天文学、历法计算、长纪历、城市规划与象形文字文学方面成就卓著。蒂卡尔、帕伦克、科潘、亚克斯奇兰等城邦留下高度精美的建筑与纪念碑。
culturalAchievements.en: They achieved distinction in pyramid-temple architecture, stela art, mural painting, astronomy, calendar calculation, the Long Count, urban planning, and hieroglyphic literature. City-states such as Tikal, Palenque, Copán, and Yaxchilán left highly refined architecture and monuments.

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 古典晚期玛雅经济以农业剩余、精英再分配、地区贸易和礼仪性消费为基础。其总体生产力在前工业农业文明中属中等偏上，但受生态承载、季节波动与政治分裂影响明显。
gdpDescription.en: The Late Classic Maya economy rested on agricultural surplus, elite redistribution, regional trade, and ritual consumption. Its overall productivity was above middling for a preindustrial agrarian civilization, but was strongly constrained by ecology, seasonality, and political fragmentation.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、可可栽培、盐业、石灰烧制、建筑工程、陶器、纺织、黑曜石与玉石工艺、羽饰制作
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, cacao growing, salt production, lime burning, construction, ceramics, textiles, obsidian and jade craftsmanship, and featherwork

tradeGoods.zh: 可可、盐、玉石、黑曜石、燧石、羽毛、棉布、陶器、贝壳、蜂蜜、蜡、染料与奢侈性礼品
tradeGoods.en: Cacao, salt, jade, obsidian, flint, feathers, cotton cloth, ceramics, shells, honey, wax, dyes, and luxury gift goods

name.zh: 无统一铸币；以可可豆与布匹等作价值媒介
name.en: No unified coinage; cacao beans and cloth served as media of value

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富体现在住房、耕地使用权、储粮、陶器、工具、衣物与亲属网络上；精英财富则集中于玉器、羽饰、奴仆、纪念碑赞助与控制贸易节点的能力。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was embodied in dwellings, access to fields, food stores, pottery, tools, clothing, and kin networks; elite wealth was concentrated in jade, featherwork, retainers, monument patronage, and control over trade nodes.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭农业为基础、由精英征收贡赋并进行再分配的礼仪—政治经济，辅以区域性市场交换与长距离奢侈品贸易。
economicSystem.en: A ritual-political economy based on household agriculture, elite tribute extraction, and redistribution, supplemented by regional market exchange and long-distance luxury trade.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

category.zh: 宗教仪式与纪念碑建设

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“国库”主要分散保存在各大城邦宫殿仓库与精英储藏中，以粮食、布匹、盐、可可、玉器和礼仪物资形式存在，而非集中金银现款。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was mainly dispersed across palace storehouses and elite holdings in major city-states, existing as foodstuffs, cloth, salt, cacao, jade, and ritual goods rather than centralized cash reserves in precious metals.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策着重维持宫廷威望、祭祀活动、建筑工程和战争能力，依赖对农业剩余和劳役的持续动员；在歉收或战乱时财政弹性有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining court prestige, ritual activity, construction, and war-making capacity, relying on continued mobilization of agrarian surplus and labor; fiscal flexibility was limited during poor harvests or warfare.

description.zh: 由王室侍从、贵族武士与经验丰富的战士组成，负责保卫核心城市、王宫与重要仪式场所，并在战争中担任突击力量。
description.en: Composed of royal retainers, noble warriors, and experienced fighters, they defended core cities, palaces, and major ritual spaces and served as shock troops in war.

description.zh: 战争时从平民与附属社群中征召，装备矛、投矛器、盾牌、木剑与燧石刀刃武器，是数量最多的作战主体。
description.en: Raised in wartime from commoners and dependent communities, they were equipped with spears, atlatls, shields, wooden swords, and flint-edged weapons, forming the bulk of the fighting force.

description.zh: 由从属城镇和盟邦提供，常随宗主城作战，但忠诚度受政治局势、婚盟与胜负变化影响。
description.en: Provided by subordinate towns and allies, they often campaigned with hegemonic cities, though their loyalty depended on politics, marriage ties, and shifts in military fortune.

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦圣主兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: Each city-state's holy lord as supreme commander

technology.zh: 军事技术以石器时代晚期与复杂木制武器为主，使用投矛器、长矛、弓箭、盾牌、棉甲与城防工事；无铁器、骑兵与大型攻城机械。
technology.en: Military technology remained based on advanced stone-age and complex wooden weaponry, using atlatls, spears, bows, shields, quilted cotton armor, and fortifications; there was no iron weaponry, cavalry, or large siege engines.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自玛雅内部城邦争霸、附庸叛离、贸易路线争夺与邻近后特奥蒂瓦坎诸势力的间接竞争。战争常以夺取俘虏、削弱敌方合法性和控制要道为目标。
threats.en: The main threats came from intra-Maya city-state rivalries, vassal defections, competition over trade routes, and indirect rivalry with neighboring post-Teotihuacan successor states. Warfare often aimed to seize captives, undermine enemy legitimacy, and control strategic routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于尤卡坦半岛、危地马拉佩滕低地、伯利兹、恰帕斯和洪都拉斯西部等地区，低地稠密核心与高地、边缘森林地带并存。
populationDescription.en: The population was widely distributed across the Yucatán Peninsula, the Petén lowlands of Guatemala, Belize, Chiapas, and western Honduras, with dense lowland cores alongside highland and forest-margin zones.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族、祭司与书记官、武士与地方首领、工匠与商人、普通农民、劳役者与奴隶／战俘
socialClasses.en: Royalty and nobles, priests and scribes, warriors and local chiefs, artisans and merchants, common farmers, laborers, and slaves/captives

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友；各城邦依具体时期形成复杂婚盟、宗主—附庸关系与临时军事联盟，如蒂卡尔系、卡拉克穆尔系及其附属城镇网络。
allies.en: There were no unified external allies; individual city-states formed complex marriage alliances, hegemon-vassal relations, and temporary military coalitions, such as the networks centered on Tikal, Calakmul, and their subordinate towns.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象为彼此竞争的玛雅城邦与反复倒向敌对阵营的附庸势力；不同地区也与邻近非玛雅社群存在边境摩擦。
enemies.en: Their principal enemies were rival Maya city-states and subordinate powers that repeatedly shifted into opposing camps; some regions also experienced frontier friction with neighboring non-Maya groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以联姻、朝贡、俘虏交换、礼品馈赠、立碑宣传和武力威慑并行为特征，目标是扩大威望、控制贸易通道并维持附庸忠诚。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was characterized by marriage alliances, tribute relations, captive exchange, gift diplomacy, propagandistic monuments, and coercive force, aiming to expand prestige, control trade corridors, and maintain vassal loyalty.

era.zh: 古典晚期美洲中部前金属高文明时代
era.en: Late Classic Mesoamerican pre-metal high civilization era

keyInnovations.zh: 长纪历与复杂历法系统、精密天文观测、玛雅象形文字、拱顶式石构建筑、灰泥工艺、蓄水设施与高强度热带农业适应技术
keyInnovations.en: The Long Count and complex calendrical systems, precise astronomical observation, Maya hieroglyphic writing, corbel-vaulted stone architecture, stucco craftsmanship, water-storage works, and intensive tropical agricultural adaptation techniques

strengths.zh: 文化高度发达，文字与历法成熟，王权仪式体系强大，城市建筑壮观，农业和区域贸易网络支撑了密集人口与精英政治竞争。
strengths.en: Its culture was highly developed, with mature writing and calendrics, a powerful ritual kingship system, monumental urban architecture, and agricultural and regional trade networks that supported dense populations and elite political competition.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂严重，城邦长期战争消耗资源，生态压力与局地干旱风险上升，财政依赖贡赋和劳役，难以形成持久统一的跨区域治理。
weaknesses.en: It suffered from severe political fragmentation, resource-draining inter-city warfare, rising ecological pressure and local drought risk, and finances dependent on tribute and labor, making durable supra-regional governance difficult.

outlook.zh: 至750年总体仍具稳定与活力，许多中心城市保持繁荣，但持续的争霸、环境脆弱性与合法性竞争已埋下未来部分低地城邦衰退的隐患。
outlook.en: By 750 the civilization remained broadly stable and vigorous, with many major centers still flourishing, but ongoing hegemonic struggles, environmental fragility, and competition over legitimacy were already laying the groundwork for the future decline of some lowland city-states.

description.zh: 中美洲低地和高地分布着众多竞争与结盟并存的玛雅城邦。
description.en: Numerous Maya city-states in the lowlands and highlands of Mesoamerica competed and allied with one another.

name.zh: 南阿拉伯行省与地方势力
name.en: Southern Arabian Provinces and Local Powers

dynasty.zh: 倭马亚王朝与地方部族

governmentForm.zh: 哈里发名义宗主权下的行省统治与部族协商并存体制，城市由总督、税吏与驻军管理，乡村和高地则依赖部族首领、宗教名望者与地方豪族维持秩序。
governmentForm.en: A mixed provincial regime under nominal caliphal suzerainty combined with tribal consultation: towns were managed by governors, tax officials, and garrisons, while the countryside and highlands relied on tribal chiefs, religious notables, and local magnates to maintain order.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由阿拉伯部族贵族、宗教与司法人士、城市商人、手工业者、农民、椰枣与谷物种植者、山地部族民众及少量奴隶和依附民组成；沿海港口社会较为多元，内陆高地则更重血缘与部族纽带。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of Arab tribal elites, religious and legal specialists, urban merchants, artisans, peasants, date and grain cultivators, highland tribal populations, and smaller numbers of slaves and dependents; coastal ports were more diverse, while the inland highlands remained more strongly organized by kinship and tribal ties.

rulingClass.zh: 执政阶层主要由受哈里发任命或认可的总督、阿拉伯驻军家族、部族谢赫、地方征税豪族以及伊斯兰法学与宗教名流构成。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was formed chiefly by governors appointed or recognized by the caliphate, Arab garrison families, tribal shaykhs, local tax-farming magnates, and Islamic legal and religious notables.

succession.zh: 总督通常由上级哈里发政权任命或确认，地方酋长则按部族共识、家族资望与实力继承；因此政治继承兼具官僚任命与习惯法选举色彩。
succession.en: Governors were generally appointed or confirmed by the superior caliphal regime, whereas local chieftains succeeded through tribal consensus, family standing, and effective power; succession therefore combined bureaucratic appointment with customary selection.

structure.zh: 750年前后南阿拉伯处于倭马亚晚期向阿拔斯革命过渡的敏感阶段。正式结构包括设于萨那的行省总督府、若干城市税务与司法机构、驻防军指挥体系，以及在高地、哈德拉毛与沿海由部族与地方豪族实际掌控的半自治网络。
structure.en: Around 750 Southern Arabia stood in the sensitive transition from the late Umayyad order to the Abbasid revolution. Its formal structure included a provincial governor's seat at Sanaa, urban tax and judicial offices, a garrison command system, and semi-autonomous networks in the highlands, Hadramawt, and the coast that were effectively controlled by tribes and local magnates.

function.zh: 负责传达哈里发政令、协调财政、军务、地方调停与对外联络。
function.en: Handled transmission of caliphal orders, coordination of finance, military affairs, local mediation, and external communications.

function.zh: 征收地税、什一税、市场税、关税及管理地方贡赋与仓储。
function.en: Collected land tax, tithes, market dues, customs, and managed local tribute and storage.

function.zh: 管理卡迪法庭、契约、公证、婚姻继承事务，并协调清真寺与宗教人士。
function.en: Supervised qadi courts, contracts, notarization, marriage and inheritance matters, and coordinated with mosques and religious personnel.

function.zh: 监督亚丁等港口的船舶、仓库、商税和对红海、印度洋贸易的抽税。
function.en: Oversaw shipping, warehouses, commercial taxes, and duties on Red Sea and Indian Ocean trade at ports such as Aden.

function.zh: 维护信使路线、官方文书、命令抄录及与汉志、伊拉克方向的往来记录。
function.en: Maintained courier routes, official documents, copied orders, and kept records of exchanges with the Hijaz and Iraq.

function.zh: 管理驻军名册、兵粮、兵器、城防与山地要塞维护。
function.en: Managed garrison rolls, provisions, arms, urban defenses, and maintenance of highland strongholds.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政呈多层次结构：萨那及主要城市由总督委派官员、税吏和卡迪管理；港口由商税官与驻军协同控制；山地和内陆谷地则往往通过部族谢赫、盟约和人质制度维系对中央的有限服从。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was multi-layered: Sanaa and the principal towns were governed by officials, tax collectors, and qadis delegated by the governor; ports were controlled jointly by customs officers and garrisons; the highlands and inland valleys were often held in limited obedience to the center through tribal shaykhs, pacts, and hostage arrangements.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以早期伊斯兰法、总督政令和部族习惯法并行。城市商贸与婚姻继承多由卡迪审理，部族争端、放牧权、水利权和血亲复仇则常按部族调解与赔偿惯例处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined early Islamic law, gubernatorial decrees, and tribal customary law. Urban trade and questions of marriage and inheritance were mostly judged by qadis, while tribal disputes, grazing rights, water rights, and blood-feud settlements were often handled through tribal mediation and compensation norms.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自农业地税、穆斯林什一税、非穆斯林人头税在个别残存社群中的征收、市场税、港口关税、商旅通行税及地方贡赋。征税能力在城市与沿海较强，在高地部族区则高度不均。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue derived mainly from agricultural land tax, the Muslim tithe, poll tax levied on some surviving non-Muslim communities, market taxes, port customs, caravan transit dues, and local tribute. Tax extraction was relatively strong in towns and along the coast, but highly uneven in the highland tribal zones.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教为主，主要属于逊尼派早期形态，但地方上亦存在较强的部族宗教实践、圣裔与宗教名望者影响，以及少量犹太教和基督教残余社群，特别在也门高地和商贸城镇中仍可见其痕迹。
religion.en: Islam was dominant, mainly in early Sunni forms, though local society also retained strong tribal religious practices, the influence of sacred lineages and religious notables, and small residual Jewish and Christian communities, especially in the Yemeni highlands and mercantile towns.

philosophy.zh: 思想上以《古兰经》伦理、阿拉伯部族荣誉观、实用行政传统和口传诗歌文化为核心；宗教与政治尚未严格分离，忠诚往往在哈里发、地方总督与部族联盟之间分层分配。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Qur'anic ethics, Arab tribal concepts of honor, practical administrative traditions, and an oral poetic culture; religion and politics were not sharply separated, and loyalty was often distributed in layers among the caliph, the local governor, and tribal alliances.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿拉伯字母书写阿拉伯语；在地方铭刻、契约和商业记载中，南阿拉伯旧传统已大幅衰退，但前伊斯兰南阿拉伯文字遗产仍被记忆于古迹与地方历史中。
writingSystem.en: Arabic script was used principally to write Arabic; in local inscriptions, contracts, and commercial records the older South Arabian traditions had greatly declined, though the pre-Islamic South Arabian epigraphic heritage still survived in monuments and local historical memory.

culturalAchievements.zh: 该地区的文化成就在于将古代也门灌溉农业、山地梯田和香料贸易传统，与伊斯兰时代的阿拉伯文书行政、清真寺文化、法学传播和印度洋商业网络结合起来；诗歌、家谱记忆和口述历史尤为重要。
culturalAchievements.en: The region's cultural achievement lay in combining ancient Yemeni irrigation agriculture, mountain terracing, and aromatics trade with the Arabic documentary administration, mosque culture, spread of legal learning, and Indian Ocean commercial networks of the Islamic age; poetry, genealogical memory, and oral history were especially important.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属于闪米特语系，以阿拉伯语为行政和通用高位语言；地方亦存在也门阿拉伯方言及若干现代南阿拉伯语言的早期形态。
languageFamily.en: Its languages belonged mainly to the Semitic family, with Arabic serving as the administrative and prestige lingua franca; local Yemeni Arabic dialects and early forms of several Modern South Arabian languages were also present.

gdpDescription.zh: 南阿拉伯经济在750年属于中等偏上的区域性经济体，基础在于高地农业、沿海贸易与穿越红海—印度洋的转运。虽然不如伊拉克或埃及富庶，但凭借也门山地的灌溉传统、港口税收和商旅网络，仍具有可观财力。
gdpDescription.en: In 750 Southern Arabia was a moderately prosperous regional economy based on highland agriculture, coastal trade, and transshipment across the Red Sea and Indian Ocean. It was less wealthy than Iraq or Egypt, yet its irrigated Yemeni highlands, port duties, and merchant networks still gave it substantial resources.

mainIndustries.zh: 主要产业包括梯田农业、谷物与高粱种植、椰枣、葡萄与果园经营、乳香与没药等香料贸易、畜牧、皮革、纺织、陶器、金属小手工业、港口仓储和长途商贸服务。
mainIndustries.en: Main industries included terraced agriculture, cultivation of grain and sorghum, date palms, grapes and orchards, trade in aromatics such as frankincense and myrrh, pastoralism, leatherworking, textiles, pottery, small-scale metal crafts, port warehousing, and long-distance commercial services.

tradeGoods.zh: 输出品有乳香、没药、皮革、马匹、小规模纺织品、干果、盐、奴隶和转口的印度洋商品；输入品有谷物补给、金属器、细布、香料、木材、奢侈陶瓷和来自印度洋与伊拉克方向的高值货物。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included frankincense, myrrh, leather, horses, limited textiles, dried fruits, salt, slaves, and re-exported Indian Ocean goods; imports included grain supplements, metalwares, fine cloth, spices, timber, luxury ceramics, and high-value merchandise from the Indian Ocean and Iraq.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距显著：商人、税务承包者和部族显贵拥有土地、牲畜、仓储与银币积蓄，而普通农户财富多体现在梯田、水权、牲畜和宗族互助关系中。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal: merchants, tax-farmers, and tribal notables possessed land, livestock, storage facilities, and silver coin reserves, while ordinary peasants held wealth mainly in terraced plots, water rights, animals, and kin-based mutual support.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度属于以农业税基为核心、辅以部族贡赋和港口商业税的前近代混合经济。国家直接控制有限，但可通过城镇、港口与商路节点汲取收入；地方部族则在土地、水利和牧场上保有强大自治权。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a premodern mixed economy centered on an agrarian tax base, supplemented by tribal tribute and port commercial taxation. Direct state control was limited, but income could be extracted through towns, ports, and trade-route nodes; local tribes retained strong autonomy over land, waterworks, and pasture.

source.zh: 罚金、公证费与杂项收入
source.en: Fines, notarization fees, and miscellaneous income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模中等，足以维持驻军与短期赈济，但不足以长期承担大规模外战。实际可动用储备常因向上级政权输送、地方截留和部族谈判而波动。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was of moderate size, sufficient to sustain garrisons and short-term relief but not to support prolonged major external warfare. The actually available reserves fluctuated because of remittances to higher authorities, local retention, and tribal bargaining.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏务实保守，以维持秩序、确保税源不断和安抚部族为优先。征税往往不追求绝对最大化，而重在避免叛乱、保障港口贸易和保持谷物与水利体系正常运作。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was pragmatic and conservative, prioritizing order, continuity of revenue, and tribal appeasement. Taxation did not always seek absolute maximization; instead it aimed to avoid rebellion, preserve port trade, and keep grain and irrigation systems functioning.

description.zh: 驻守萨那、沿海道路与要塞的常备步兵，负责治安、征税支援、守城与镇压骚乱。
description.en: Regular infantry stationed in Sanaa, along coastal routes, and in forts, tasked with security, support for tax collection, urban defense, and suppression of unrest.

description.zh: 由各部族在危机时提供的战士，机动性强，熟悉山地作战，但忠诚主要系于部族协议与酬赏。
description.en: Warriors furnished by tribes in times of crisis; highly mobile and familiar with mountain warfare, but loyal chiefly to tribal agreements and reward.

description.zh: 用于山间巡逻、沙漠通信和追击的机动兵力，兼具威慑与联络功能。
description.en: Mobile forces used for mountain patrols, desert communications, and pursuit, serving both coercive and liaison roles.

description.zh: 部署于亚丁及其他海岸据点，保护仓库、码头和商船，防备海盗与地方叛乱。
description.en: Deployed at Aden and other coastal positions to protect warehouses, docks, and merchant shipping against piracy and local rebellion.

commanderInChief.zh: 萨那总督兼行省军政长官
commanderInChief.en: Governor of Sanaa as provincial military-administrative chief

technology.zh: 军事技术以早期伊斯兰时代常见装备为主，包括长矛、剑、弓、皮甲与锁子甲、盾牌、攻城梯和简易投射器械。真正优势不在技术突破，而在山地防御、熟悉地形和部族动员能力。
technology.en: Military technology was typical of the early Islamic age, including spears, swords, bows, leather armor and mail, shields, scaling ladders, and simple siege engines. The real advantage lay less in innovation than in mountain defense, knowledge of terrain, and tribal mobilization.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自阿拔斯革命导致的宗主权更替、地方部族拒税或叛乱、海岸海盗活动、与汉志及阿曼方向的权力竞争，以及在政权交替时期对港口与税源的争夺。
threats.en: Main threats came from the transfer of suzerainty caused by the Abbasid revolution, local tribal tax resistance or revolt, coastal piracy, power competition with forces from the Hijaz and Oman, and struggles over ports and revenue during regime transition.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于也门高地梯田农业区、内陆谷地与沿海贸易带。萨那及若干港口和市场城镇构成城市骨架，但绝大多数居民仍居于村落、灌溉农地和部族控制的山地聚落中。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated chiefly in the terraced agricultural highlands of Yemen, inland valleys, and coastal trade belts. Sanaa and several ports and market towns formed the urban framework, but the great majority of inhabitants still lived in villages, irrigated farmlands, and tribal mountain settlements.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为总督家族、部族谢赫、驻军军官、宗教学者和大商人；中层为小地主、市场商人、书记员、法官助手与手工业者；下层为农民、佃户、牧民、码头劳工、仆役与奴隶。部族身份常与阶层身份交织，不能简单按城市模式划分。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of governor's families, tribal shaykhs, garrison officers, religious scholars, and major merchants; the middle layers included small landholders, market traders, scribes, legal assistants, and artisans; the lower layers comprised peasants, tenants, pastoralists, dock laborers, servants, and slaves. Tribal identity often overlapped with class status and cannot be reduced to a purely urban social model.

allies.zh: 名义上与倭马亚帝国残存权威及忠于哈里发秩序的阿拉伯行省合作，地方上则依赖与周边也门部族、红海商人网络和部分港口势力的临时联盟。
allies.en: Nominally allied with the remaining authority of the Umayyad Empire and Arab provinces loyal to caliphal order, while locally depending on temporary alliances with neighboring Yemeni tribes, Red Sea merchant networks, and selected port powers.

enemies.zh: 敌对力量主要包括支持阿拔斯运动的派系、拒绝纳税或争夺自治的部族联盟、海盗与扰乱商路的武装集团，以及任何试图夺取也门港口税收的外来势力。
enemies.en: Hostile forces chiefly included factions supporting the Abbasid movement, tribal coalitions resisting taxation or seeking autonomy, pirates and armed groups disrupting trade routes, and any outside power attempting to seize Yemeni port revenues.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护商路、保持对红海与印度洋港口的控制、在哈里发更替中保存地方利益为核心。其外交实践多为务实平衡：对名义宗主表示忠诚，同时尽量避免与邻近部族和海上商人网络全面破裂。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on securing trade routes, maintaining control over Red Sea and Indian Ocean ports, and preserving local interests amid the change of caliphal rule. In practice it was a pragmatic balancing policy: professing loyalty to nominal overlords while avoiding a total rupture with neighboring tribes and maritime merchant networks.

era.zh: 早期伊斯兰时代的南阿拉伯区域技术水平
era.en: Southern Arabian regional technology in the Early Islamic Age

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术并非全新发明，而是成熟体系的延续与整合：山地梯田与引水工程、蓄水池和堤坝维护、骆驼与海运结合的长途物流、阿拉伯文书行政、币制流通，以及清真寺与市场为核心的城市组织方式。
keyInnovations.en: Its key technologies were less new inventions than the continuation and integration of mature systems: mountain terracing and water-channel engineering, cistern and dam maintenance, long-distance logistics combining camel transport and seafaring, Arabic documentary administration, monetized exchange, and mosque- and market-centered urban organization.

strengths.zh: 优势在于地处红海与印度洋交通要冲、山地农业适应性强、部族动员能力高、港口税收可观，并拥有深厚的古代也门灌溉与商业传统。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in its position on the Red Sea and Indian Ocean crossroads, resilient mountain agriculture, high tribal mobilization capacity, substantial port revenues, and a deep inheritance of ancient Yemeni irrigation and commercial traditions.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于中央控制力有限、部族政治碎片化、财政征收不均、沿海与高地利益常相冲突，且在750年恰逢倭马亚向阿拔斯转换，宗主权合法性极不稳定。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were limited central control, fragmented tribal politics, uneven fiscal extraction, frequent tension between coastal and highland interests, and the acute instability of overlord legitimacy in 750 during the transition from Umayyad to Abbasid rule.

outlook.zh: 短期前景总体稳定但充满变数：若地方总督能及时向新兴阿拔斯政权输诚并安抚部族，南阿拉伯可继续凭借贸易与农业维持秩序；若宗主权交替处理失当，则港口争夺、税收断裂和部族叛乱将迅速削弱地区统一。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was broadly stable but highly contingent: if local governors quickly transferred allegiance to the rising Abbasid regime and pacified the tribes, Southern Arabia could continue to sustain order through trade and agriculture; if the transition of suzerainty was mishandled, struggles over ports, fiscal breakdown, and tribal rebellion would rapidly undermine regional unity.

description.zh: 阿拉伯半岛南部由哈里发政权与地方部族共同塑造的地区。
description.en: A southern Arabian region shaped jointly by caliphal administration and local tribal power.

name.zh: 中墨西哥后古典前期诸国
后特奥蒂瓦坎继承诸邦

governmentForm.zh: 分裂的区域性城邦寡头统治，兼具世袭君主与贵族议政特征
governmentForm.en: Fragmented regional city-state oligarchies combining hereditary lordship with noble council governance

socialStructure.zh: 社会以统治贵族、祭司、武士、工匠、商人、农民与依附劳役人口构成；各城邦内部等级分明，宗教与军事地位紧密相连。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of ruling nobles, priests, warriors, artisans, merchants, farmers, and dependent laborers; within each city-state hierarchy was pronounced, and religious and military status were closely linked.

rulingClass.zh: 地方世袭贵族、祭司集团与军事首领共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of local hereditary nobles, priestly elites, and military leaders

succession.zh: 多为贵族家族内部世袭继承，但常受联姻、祭司认可与武力争夺影响
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary within noble houses, but was often shaped by marriage alliances, priestly approval, and armed contest

structure.zh: 霍奇卡尔科代表性的政体由城邦领主居于顶端，下辖贵族议事层、祭司机构、军事首领与贡赋管理者；外围附属聚落通过地方头人和宗族首领接受统治。
structure.en: The representative polity at Xochicalco placed the city lord at the top, supported by a noble deliberative layer, priestly institutions, military captains, and tribute administrators; outlying dependent settlements were governed through local headmen and kin leaders.

function.zh: 协助领主决策、协调贵族家族利益、主持外交与继承安排
function.en: Assisted the ruler in decision-making, coordinated noble family interests, and oversaw diplomacy and succession arrangements

function.zh: 管理神庙、主持祭祀、维护历法与天象知识，并为统治提供神圣合法性
function.en: Managed temples, conducted rituals, maintained calendrical and astronomical knowledge, and provided sacred legitimacy to rule

function.zh: 征收实物贡赋、登记附属聚落义务、管理粮食、棉布、玉石与其他储备
function.en: Collected tribute in kind, recorded obligations of dependent settlements, and managed stores of food, cotton cloth, jade, and other reserves

function.zh: 组织守军、征召战士、维护堡垒与交通要点，并指挥对外战争
function.en: Organized garrisons, mobilized warriors, maintained fortifications and strategic routes, and directed external warfare

function.zh: 监督黑曜石加工、陶器生产、奢侈品工坊与区域贸易网络
function.en: Supervised obsidian working, pottery production, luxury workshops, and regional trade networks

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖附属村社首领、宗族头人和驻点贵族代理人；核心城邦对周边地区实行贡赋与军事控制，而非高度文书化的官僚直辖。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on subordinate village chiefs, lineage heads, and resident noble agents; the core city-state exercised tribute and military control over surrounding areas rather than direct rule through a highly literate bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要基于习惯法、领主裁断、宗教禁忌与赔偿原则；重大案件如叛乱、拒贡、亵渎祭祀与谋杀可能受到严厉惩罚。
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on customary practice, lordly judgment, religious taboo, and compensation principles; major offenses such as rebellion, refusal of tribute, sacrilege, and murder could be punished severely.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入以实物贡赋和劳役为主，包括玉米、豆类、辣椒、棉布、黑曜石制品、羽毛、盐及公共工程劳役；市场交换抽取有限费用。
taxationSystem.en: Public revenue relied primarily on tribute in kind and labor service, including maize, beans, chili, cotton cloth, obsidian goods, feathers, salt, and corvée for public works; market exchange yielded limited fees.

religion.zh: 多神信仰延续中部美洲传统，崇拜羽蛇神、雨神、火神、太阳与祖先神灵，祭祀、天象与政治权威密切结合。
religion.en: Polytheistic religion continued central Mesoamerican traditions, venerating the Feathered Serpent, rain deities, fire gods, the sun, and ancestral powers; ritual, celestial observation, and political authority were tightly linked.

philosophy.zh: 其思想并非哲学体系化传统，而是以宇宙循环、历法秩序、祭祀义务、战争荣誉与祖先合法性为核心的宗教宇宙观。
philosophy.en: Its thought was not philosophy in a systematized textual sense, but a religious cosmology centered on cyclical time, calendrical order, ritual obligation, martial honor, and ancestral legitimacy.

writingSystem.zh: 使用有限的图像文字、地名符号、日历与数字记号，铭文能力不及玛雅地区发达，但在纪年、神庙装饰和权力象征上具有重要作用。
writingSystem.en: They used limited pictographic notation, place signs, calendrical and numerical symbols; inscriptional practice was less developed than in the Maya area, but remained important for dating, temple decoration, and symbols of authority.

culturalAchievements.zh: 霍奇卡尔科以山顶设防城市规划、精美浮雕、宗教建筑群、天文观测设施和区域艺术融合著称，反映出特奥蒂瓦坎之后中部墨西哥文化重组的高度创造力。
culturalAchievements.en: Xochicalco was noted for its hilltop fortified urban plan, refined relief sculpture, ceremonial architecture, astronomical observation features, and synthesis of regional artistic traditions, reflecting the creative cultural reorganization of central Mexico after Teotihuacan.

languageFamily.zh: 以奥托-曼格语系诸语言为主，并与纳瓦-托托纳克等周边语言群长期接触
languageFamily.en: Primarily Oto-Manguean languages, with long-term contact involving surrounding Nahuan-related and Totonacan language groups

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、区域手工业和城邦间交换为基础，整体富裕程度中等；财富主要集中于神庙、贵族宅邸和控制贸易节点的统治者手中，而广大农村人口维持基本自给。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, regional craft production, and exchange among city-states, with a moderate overall level of prosperity; wealth was concentrated in temples, noble households, and rulers controlling trade nodes, while most rural populations remained near subsistence.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、梯田与旱作耕种、黑曜石开采与加工、陶器制造、棉布与纤维品、建筑营造、羽毛与玉石奢侈品制作
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, terrace and dry-field cultivation, obsidian extraction and working, pottery manufacture, cotton and fiber textiles, construction, and luxury production in feathers and jade

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石刀片、陶器、盐、棉布、可可少量转运、羽毛、贝壳、玉石、颜料、农产品
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian blades, pottery, salt, cotton cloth, limited transit cacao, feathers, shells, jade, pigments, and agricultural produce

name.zh: 无统一铸币，实物交换与贡赋体系
name.en: No unified coinage; barter and tribute system

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现在住房、农具、储粮、陶器和家庭劳动力；贵族家庭则掌握土地、附属劳役、奢侈品与远距离交换关系。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in dwellings, tools, grain stores, pottery, and family labor; noble households controlled land, dependent labor, prestige goods, and long-distance exchange ties.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业自给为基础、辅以精英控制的贡赋再分配和区域市场交换的混合经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on agrarian subsistence, elite-controlled tribute redistribution, and regional market exchange

source.en: Agricultural and village tribute in kind
Commuted value of corvée labor from dependent settlements

source.zh: 贸易节点、市场与手工业征收
source.en: Levies from trade nodes, markets, and craft production

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要并非金银，而是储存在宫殿、神庙和仓库中的粮食、布匹、黑曜石器物、奢侈贡品与可调动劳力；贵金属当量仅用于现代估算。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly not of precious metal, but of grain, cloth, obsidian goods, prestige tribute, and mobilizable labor stored in palaces, temples, and warehouses; precious-metal equivalents are modern estimates only.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视维持粮仓、祭祀支出和战时动员能力，在城邦竞争环境下尽量保留一定实物储备。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, emphasizing maintained granaries, ritual expenditure, and wartime mobilization capacity, with efforts to preserve in-kind reserves amid city-state competition.

description.zh: 负责防守霍奇卡尔科及其附属要塞、城门、台地和仓储区，是最稳定的常备力量。
description.en: Responsible for defending Xochicalco and its associated forts, gates, terraces, and storage areas; this was the most stable standing force.

description.zh: 由贵族家族维持的精锐战士组成，承担突袭、仪式战争和精英护卫任务。
description.en: Composed of elite fighters maintained by noble families, carrying out raids, ritual warfare, and high-status guard duties.

description.zh: 来自附属村社与周边聚落，战时大量动员，装备较轻，适合守备、运输和随军支援。
description.en: Drawn from dependent villages and surrounding settlements, heavily mobilized in wartime; lightly equipped and suited for defense, transport, and campaign support.

description.zh: 负责监视山地通道、护送商队并传递战时报信，在分裂政治环境中极为重要。
description.en: Tasked with monitoring mountain passes, escorting caravans, and carrying wartime intelligence, a crucial role in a fragmented political landscape.

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦领主及其首席战首
commanderInChief.en: Each city ruler and his principal war captain

technology.zh: 以黑曜石刃器、投矛器、长矛、棍棒武器、盾牌、棉甲和石砌防御工事为主；善于利用山地城址和阶台防守，但缺乏冶金武器传统。
technology.en: Military technology centered on obsidian-edged weapons, atlatls, spears, clubs, shields, padded cotton armor, and stone fortifications; they made effective defensive use of hilltop cities and terraces, but lacked a metallurgical weapons tradition.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自中部墨西哥与邻近高地的竞争城邦、贸易路线争夺、附属聚落叛离，以及来自玛雅区域与瓦里影响圈之外的间接地缘竞争。
threats.en: Major threats came from rival city-states in central Mexico and adjacent highlands, competition over trade routes, secession by subordinate settlements, and indirect geopolitical rivalry beyond the Maya and Wari spheres.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约二百万，分散于中部墨西哥多个相互竞争的城邦、村社与山地聚落；人口密度在农业条件较好的高地盆地和贸易通道附近较高。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about two million, dispersed across multiple competing city-states, villages, and hill settlements in central Mexico; density was highest in better agricultural basins and near trade corridors.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大体包括世袭贵族、祭司、武士、专业工匠与商人、普通农民，以及依附性的劳役群体与战俘；社会流动有限，但军事功绩和宗教服务可带来一定上升机会。
socialClasses.en: Social strata broadly included hereditary nobles, priests, warriors, specialized artisans and merchants, common farmers, and dependent labor groups and captives; mobility was limited, though military achievement and religious service could offer some upward advancement.

allies.zh: 不存在持久统一盟邦体系；多以临时城邦联盟、婚姻结盟和共同对敌安排为主。
allies.en: There was no durable unified alliance system; diplomacy relied mainly on temporary city-state coalitions, marriage alliances, and ad hoc arrangements against common enemies.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象是邻近竞争城邦及争夺黑曜石、盐源、商路和附属村社的区域强权。
enemies.en: The principal enemies were neighboring rival city-states and regional powers contesting obsidian sources, salt supplies, trade routes, and subordinate villages.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持区域均势、控制商路、吸纳附庸和阻止单一强邻崛起为核心；外交通常与战争、联姻和宗教威望并行。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on maintaining a regional balance of power, controlling trade routes, attracting tributaries, and preventing any single strong neighbor from dominating; diplomacy normally operated alongside war, marriage, and religious prestige.

era.zh: 中部美洲古典期晚期至终末古典期过渡
era.en: Late Classic to Epiclassic transition in Mesoamerica

keyInnovations.zh: 山顶防御性城市规划、复杂排水与梯台建筑、黑曜石专业化加工、历法与天象观测传统、区域艺术风格融合
keyInnovations.en: Defensive hilltop urban planning, complex drainage and terraced construction, specialized obsidian working, calendrical and astronomical observation traditions, and synthesis of regional artistic styles

strengths.zh: 优势在于区域贸易网络灵活、山地设防城市坚固、黑曜石与手工业发达、文化整合能力强，并能在特奥蒂瓦坎崩溃后迅速重建政治中心。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in flexible regional trade networks, strong hilltop fortified cities, advanced obsidian and craft production, strong cultural integrative capacity, and the ability to rebuild political centers rapidly after Teotihuacan's collapse.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点是政治分裂严重、缺乏统一王朝和持久行政整合，对附属聚落的控制多依赖威望与武力，内部竞争容易削弱长期稳定。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were severe political fragmentation, the absence of a unified dynasty and durable administrative integration, and control over dependent settlements that relied heavily on prestige and force, making long-term stability vulnerable to internal rivalry.

outlook.zh: 750年前后局势相对稳定，但未来数代仍可能因城邦竞争、贸易重组与生态压力而再度洗牌；霍奇卡尔科虽居强势地位，却难以像特奥蒂瓦坎那样实现持久统一。
outlook.en: Around 750 the situation was relatively stable, but over the coming generations city-state competition, shifting trade patterns, and ecological pressures were likely to reshuffle power again; although Xochicalco held a strong position, it was unlikely to achieve the durable unity once seen at Teotihuacan.

description.zh: 特奥蒂瓦坎崩溃后，中墨西哥由多个区域城邦竞争主导权。
description.en: After Teotihuacan's fall, multiple regional city-states competed for dominance in central Mexico.

governmentForm.zh: 以宗教权威为核心、由祭司贵族和地方附属领袖共同维系的神权政治
governmentForm.en: A theocratic polity centered on religious authority, maintained by priestly elites and subordinate local leaders

socialStructure.zh: 社会由祭司贵族、行政与礼仪管理者、工匠、农牧民、运输劳役群体以及受中心影响的地方社群构成；国家通过仪式整合、劳役调配与殖民聚落网络维系秩序。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of priestly nobles, administrative and ceremonial managers, artisans, agropastoral commoners, transport labor groups, and local communities influenced by the core; order was maintained through ritual integration, labor mobilization, and a network of colonies.

rulingClass.zh: 掌控神庙、礼仪、仓储与再分配体系的祭司贵族集团
rulingClass.en: A priestly aristocratic elite controlling temples, ritual, storage, and redistribution

succession.zh: 继承方式不明，可能在统治家族与祭司精英内部协商决定，并以宗教合法性确认
succession.en: Succession is unclear; it was likely decided within ruling lineages and priestly elites, with legitimacy confirmed through religion

structure.zh: 中央圣城统辖仪式中心、仓储设施、工坊与周边农业区，并通过地方殖民据点、附属社区及交换网络向的的喀喀湖盆地外延伸影响。政权更像以宗教与再分配为核心的区域体系，而非高度成文官僚国家。
structure.en: The central sacred city governed ceremonial precincts, storage facilities, workshops, and surrounding agricultural zones, projecting influence beyond the Lake Titicaca basin through colonies, subordinate communities, and exchange networks. The polity resembled a regional system centered on religion and redistribution rather than a highly codified bureaucratic state.

name.zh: 大神庙与礼仪事务机构
name.en: Great Temple and Ritual Affairs Office

function.zh: 主持历法、祭祀、祖灵崇拜、公共庆典与统治合法性的宗教宣示
function.en: Oversaw calendrical rites, sacrifices, ancestor veneration, public festivals, and religious expressions of political legitimacy

function.zh: 管理谷物、藜麦、马铃薯干、羊驼制品、铜器与礼品的征集、储藏和分配
function.en: Managed the collection, storage, and distribution of grain, quinoa, chuño, camelid products, copper goods, and prestige items

function.zh: 维护高地 raised fields、灌排系统、堤埂、道路和生产性劳役组织
function.en: Maintained raised fields, irrigation and drainage systems, embankments, roads, and productive labor organization

name.zh: 殖民地与地方联络机构

function.zh: 联络外围殖民聚落、山谷据点和地方首领，保障资源流动与仪式从属
function.en: Liaised with outlying colonies, valley enclaves, and local chiefs to secure resource flows and ritual subordination

function.zh: 组织石雕、建筑、陶器、纺织品、金属工艺与象征性艺术生产
function.en: Organized stone carving, construction, ceramics, textiles, metallurgy, and symbolic artistic production

function.zh: 调度羊驼驮队，运输玉米、可卡叶、海产、羽毛、铜矿与礼仪物资
function.en: Directed llama caravans transporting maize, coca leaves, marine products, feathers, copper ore, and ritual supplies

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖附属首领、殖民社区管理者和宗教代理人。中心通常不以统一文书官僚直接统治远地，而是通过祭仪、婚姻联系、交换和劳役义务维持控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on subordinate chiefs, colony managers, and religious agents. The center usually did not directly govern distant regions through a uniform documentary bureaucracy, but maintained control through ritual, marriage ties, exchange, and labor obligations.

legalSystem.zh: 无成文法典证据。社会规范主要由习惯、祭司裁决、群体义务和礼仪禁忌构成，违法行为更可能被视为对秩序与神圣关系的破坏。
legalSystem.en: There is no evidence for a written law code. Social norms were mainly defined by custom, priestly judgment, communal obligations, and ritual taboos; offenses were likely seen as disruptions of order and sacred relationships.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以实物与劳役为主，而非货币征税。附属社群向中心提供农产品、纺织品、羊驼、手工业品、建筑劳力和驮运服务。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was primarily in goods and labor rather than coin. Subject communities provided agricultural produce, textiles, llamas, craft goods, construction labor, and caravan service to the center.

religion.zh: 以国家礼仪和祖先崇拜为核心的多神信仰，重视天象、山岳、湖泊与农业丰产神圣性；著名的“持杖神”形象可能代表核心神权象征。
religion.en: A polytheistic religious system centered on state ritual and ancestor veneration, emphasizing the sacredness of celestial cycles, mountains, lakes, and agricultural fertility; the famous 'Staff God' image likely represented a central symbol of sacred authority.

philosophy.zh: 其思想并非成体系文字哲学，而是通过宇宙秩序、季节循环、祖灵、祭祀与共同体再分配观念表达政治与宗教世界观。
philosophy.en: Its thought was not a systematic textual philosophy, but a political and religious worldview expressed through cosmic order, seasonal cycles, ancestors, ritual, and communal redistribution.

writingSystem.zh: 无已知成熟文字系统；信息传递依靠图像符号、建筑布局、纪念碑图像、口述传统与实物管理。
writingSystem.en: No known mature writing system existed; information was conveyed through iconography, architectural layout, monumental imagery, oral tradition, and material administration.

culturalAchievements.zh: 成就包括阿卡帕纳、卡拉萨萨亚和半地下神庙等巨石建筑；高度风格化的石雕与门廊艺术；精致纺织与陶器；在高海拔环境下发展 raised-field 农业与区域整合的仪式都市文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Achievements included megalithic constructions such as Akapana, Kalasasaya, and the Semi-Subterranean Temple; highly stylized stone sculpture and gateway art; refined textiles and ceramics; and the development of raised-field agriculture and a ritual urban culture in a high-altitude environment.

languageFamily.zh: 可能主要使用普基纳语，并与艾马拉语及其他安第斯语言接触；语言情况仍有学术争议。
languageFamily.en: Puquina was likely important, with contact involving Aymaran and other Andean languages; the linguistic situation remains debated.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高原农业、羊驼牧养、国家再分配、区域交换和礼仪消费为基础。由于缺乏货币与文字财政记录，数值仅为现代比较性重建，用于表示其在前近代安第斯属于强盛区域中心。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on highland agriculture, llama herding, state redistribution, regional exchange, and ceremonial consumption. Because coinage and written fiscal records are absent, the figures are modern comparative reconstructions meant to indicate that Tiwanaku was a powerful regional center in the premodern Andes.

mainIndustries.zh: 高地农业、raised-field 湿地耕作、羊驼与羊驼毛制品、陶器、纺织、石工、铜器制造、驮运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Highland agriculture, raised-field wetland cultivation, llama herding and wool products, ceramics, textiles, stoneworking, copper manufacture, and caravan trade

tradeGoods.zh: 藜麦、马铃薯及其冻干制品、玉米、可卡叶、羊驼毛与织物、陶器、铜器、海贝、热带羽毛、黑曜石、鱼干与礼仪用品
tradeGoods.en: Quinoa, potatoes and freeze-dried products, maize, coca leaves, llama wool and textiles, ceramics, copper goods, seashells, tropical feathers, obsidian, dried fish, and ritual items

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、羊驼数量、储粮能力、纺织工具与亲属劳动力，而非金属货币积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to land, numbers of llamas, storage capacity, textile tools, and kin labor rather than accumulations of money metal.

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯）/人/年
unit.en: international dollars (1990 Geary-Khamis) per person per year

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯）/年

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的再分配经济与地方互惠体系并存，市场交换存在但并非主导；权力通过礼物、仓储、劳役动员和长距离驮运网络运作。
economicSystem.en: A state-directed redistributive economy coexisted with local reciprocity systems; market exchange existed but was not dominant. Power operated through gifts, storage, labor mobilization, and long-distance caravan networks.

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯）/年
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯）/年
国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯）/年

source.en: Labor obligations and public works mobilization

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯）等值

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要不是铸币金银，而是仓储中的粮食、纺织品、铜器、驮畜、劳动力控制权及礼仪珍品。此处折算仅为比较目的。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly not of coined bullion but of stored food, textiles, copper goods, pack animals, control over labor, and prestige items. The valuation here is only for comparison.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在维持圣城、扩大礼仪影响、资助公共工程并通过再分配稳定附属群体；倾向保有一定储备以应对霜冻、歉收与地区动荡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining the sacred capital, expanding ritual influence, funding public works, and stabilizing subordinate groups through redistribution; it favored retaining reserves against frost, harvest failures, and regional instability.

description.zh: 负责首都核心建筑、祭司精英和仓储设施安全的常备武装
description.en: A standing force protecting the capital's core monuments, priestly elites, and storage facilities

description.zh: 由附属社群在冲突时期提供，以棍棒、投石索、矛和护具作战
description.en: Provided by subordinate communities in times of conflict, fighting with clubs, slings, spears, and protective gear

name.zh: 驮队护卫与边地巡逻队

description.zh: 保护长途商路、殖民据点和高原边缘通道的小型机动部队
description.en: Small mobile forces protecting long-distance routes, colonial enclaves, and passageways on the highland margins

commanderInChief.zh: 祭司王及其贵族军事代理人
commanderInChief.en: The Priest-King and his noble military deputies

technology.zh: 军备以安第斯传统兵器为主，包括投石索、矛、棍棒、石铜刃工具、棉甲或皮革护具；不具备铁器、骑兵或攻城器传统。军事力量更多用于威慑、护路和地区控制，而非大规模征服战。
technology.en: Military equipment was based on traditional Andean weapons including slings, spears, clubs, stone- and copper-edged implements, and quilted or leather protection; it lacked iron weapons, cavalry, and siege traditions. Military power was used more for deterrence, route protection, and regional control than for massive conquest.

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖里-卡米斯）/年

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与瓦里文明在安第斯中南部的竞争、地方附属社群离心、高地生态波动以及长途补给线脆弱性。
threats.en: Main threats came from competition with the Wari civilization in the south-central Andes, centrifugal tendencies among subordinate communities, ecological fluctuations in the highlands, and the fragility of long-distance supply routes.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约五十万，包含蒂亚瓦纳科核心区、的的喀喀湖盆地周边农业人口、殖民据点居民及受其政治宗教影响的附属群体。首都本身是南美当时最重要的礼仪中心之一。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 500,000, including the Tiwanaku core, agricultural populations around the Lake Titicaca basin, colonial enclaves, and subordinate groups under its political-religious influence. The capital itself was among the most important ceremonial centers in South America at the time.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为祭司贵族与仪式管理者；中间层包括工匠、殖民地管理者、驮运组织者和地方精英；下层为广大农民、牧民、劳役承担者与附属社区成员。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of priestly nobles and ritual administrators; the middle included artisans, colony managers, caravan organizers, and local elites; the lower consisted of farmers, herders, laboring populations, and members of subordinate communities.

allies.zh: 无严格意义上的远距离国家盟友；其实际伙伴主要是受其礼仪与交换网络吸纳的高原和山谷地方社群、殖民据点与附属首领。
allies.en: There were no long-distance state allies in the strict sense; its real partners were highland and valley communities, colonial enclaves, and subordinate chiefs integrated into its ritual and exchange networks.

enemies.zh: 主要竞争者是瓦里文明；此外，部分边缘地区社群可能周期性反抗中心影响。
enemies.en: Its principal rival was the Wari civilization; additionally, some peripheral communities likely resisted central influence from time to time.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在通过宗教威望、殖民据点、婚姻联系、驮队交换和象征物传播扩大影响，而不是依赖持续大规模军事征服。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on expanding influence through religious prestige, colonial enclaves, marriage ties, caravan exchange, and the spread of symbols rather than through sustained large-scale military conquest.

era.zh: 中期地平线时期安第斯高地文明
era.en: Middle Horizon Andean highland civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 高海拔 raised-field 农业与霜害调适、巨石切割与拼装、标准化宗教图像传播、驮运网络组织、精细纺织和铜器加工。
keyInnovations.en: High-altitude raised-field agriculture and frost adaptation, megalithic stone cutting and fitting, diffusion of standardized religious iconography, organized caravan networks, fine textiles, and copper working.

strengths.zh: 拥有强大的宗教感召力、适应高寒环境的农业技术、广泛的殖民与交换网络、显著的纪念性建筑传统，以及能够将多样地方社群纳入共同仪式体系的整合能力。
strengths.en: It possessed strong religious appeal, agricultural techniques adapted to harsh high-altitude conditions, broad colonial and exchange networks, impressive monumental architecture, and the capacity to integrate diverse local communities into a shared ritual system.

weaknesses.zh: 政治结构依赖宗教合法性与再分配网络，行政直接控制有限；缺乏文字与货币使复杂治理受限；生态压力、气候波动和外围离心都可能迅速削弱中心。
weaknesses.en: Its political structure depended heavily on religious legitimacy and redistribution networks, while direct administrative control was limited; the absence of writing and money constrained complex governance; ecological stress, climate fluctuation, and peripheral fragmentation could weaken the center quickly.

outlook.zh: 公元750年时蒂亚瓦纳科仍处于繁盛阶段，圣城威望与区域影响显著。若高原农业系统持续稳定并能维持与外围社群的资源联系，其势力仍可延续；但长期看，环境变迁和政治网络松动将构成严重风险。
outlook.en: In 750 CE Tiwanaku remained in a flourishing phase, with a prestigious sacred capital and substantial regional influence. If the highland agricultural system remained stable and ties to peripheral communities were maintained, its power could continue; over the longer term, however, environmental change and loosening political networks posed serious risks.

description.zh: 的的喀喀湖盆地的宗教—政治中心，影响安第斯南部广泛区域。
description.en: A religious-political center in the Lake Titicaca basin influencing wide areas of the southern Andes.

ruler.zh: 姓名不详的瓦里统治者

governmentForm.zh: 以首都为核心、通过行政中心与殖民据点整合各地区的早期官僚帝国
governmentForm.en: An early bureaucratic empire centered on the capital and integrated through provincial administrative centers and colonial enclaves

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与祭祀贵族、行政官员、军事首领、专业工匠、地方社区领袖、农民与牧民构成；国家通过重新安置人口、征调劳役和控制仓储来重组地方社会。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of royal and ritual elites, administrators, military leaders, specialist artisans, local community heads, farmers, and herders; the state reshaped local society through population resettlement, labor drafts, and control of storage systems.

rulingClass.zh: 以瓦里都城贵族、祭祀精英、军事行政官僚及其附属地方精英为统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of Wari capital nobles, ritual elites, military-administrative officials, and subordinate local elites

succession.zh: 继承制度可能以王室血统与精英共识相结合，具体规则不明，但应由都城核心贵族集团确认新统治者
succession.en: Succession likely combined royal lineage with elite consensus; exact rules are unclear, but the core noble group at the capital probably confirmed a new ruler

structure.zh: 中央政权设于瓦里都城，下辖若干区域行政中心，如皮基利亚克塔、维拉卡乔潘帕和哈通瓦里；国家以道路、仓库、仪式建筑和驻防据点维持统治。
structure.en: The central government was based at Wari and supervised regional administrative centers such as Pikillaqta, Viracochapampa, and other provincial hubs; the state maintained control through roads, storehouses, ceremonial architecture, and garrisoned enclaves.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、官方祭祀、祖先崇拜、国家仪式与统治合法性象征
function.en: Managed the court, official cult activity, ancestor veneration, state ceremonies, and symbols of political legitimacy

function.zh: 监督地方行政中心、人口调配、社区整编、劳役征发与地方官员任命
function.en: Oversaw provincial centers, population transfers, community reorganization, labor levies, and appointment of local officials

function.zh: 征收实物贡赋，管理玉米、藜麦、纺织品、陶器、金属器和羊驼毛等国家储备
function.en: Collected tribute in kind and managed state reserves of maize, quinoa, textiles, ceramics, metal goods, and camelid wool

function.zh: 修建与维护道路、梯田、行政建筑、围墙复合体、水利和仓库
function.en: Built and maintained roads, terraces, administrative compounds, walled complexes, waterworks, and storehouses

function.zh: 协调驻军、边疆防御、征服行动、后勤运输与地方安全
function.en: Coordinated garrisons, frontier defense, expansion campaigns, logistics, and local security

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托国家建立的区域中心与附属聚落运行，原有地方酋长常被纳入瓦里体系，负责组织社区劳役、贡赋与治安；在关键地区则直接驻扎来自核心区的管理者和工匠群体。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through state-founded regional centers and dependent settlements. Existing local chiefs were often incorporated into the Wari system and made responsible for labor drafts, tribute, and order; in strategic zones, managers and artisan groups from the core were installed directly.

legalSystem.zh: 瓦里没有已知成文法典，治理更可能依靠行政命令、礼仪等级、再分配义务和地方习惯法；对反抗的控制主要通过军事威慑、人口迁移和资源分配来实现。
legalSystem.en: The Wari had no known written legal code; governance likely relied on administrative command, ritual hierarchy, redistributive obligations, and local customary law. Control over resistance was enforced mainly through military intimidation, resettlement, and regulation of resources.

taxationSystem.zh: 赋税主要以实物与劳役形式征收，而非标准货币税；国家从附属社区获取粮食、纺织品、畜产品、手工业品及建筑和运输劳力，再经仓储体系进行再分配。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was collected mainly in kind and labor rather than in standardized money; the state drew food, textiles, livestock products, craft goods, and construction and transport labor from subordinate communities, then redistributed them through storehouse networks.

religion.zh: 瓦里宗教为安第斯多神信仰，强调与山岳、祖先、圣地和农业周期相关的祭祀；图像中常见与“权杖神”传统有关的超自然形象，显示其与更广泛高地宗教象征的联系。
religion.en: Wari religion was a polytheistic Andean system emphasizing rituals tied to mountains, ancestors, sacred places, and agricultural cycles; imagery often featured supernatural figures linked to the Staff God tradition, showing connections to broader highland religious symbolism.

philosophy.zh: 其思想核心并非抽象哲学体系，而是以宇宙秩序、祖先权威、互惠义务、国家仪式和对生态层带的组织控制为基础的政治宗教世界观。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual core was not an abstract philosophical system but a politico-religious worldview based on cosmic order, ancestral authority, reciprocal obligation, state ritual, and organized control over ecological zones.

writingSystem.zh: 无已知成熟文字系统；行政与记忆可能依赖结绳、视觉符号、建筑空间秩序以及口述传统，但对瓦里是否使用早期记事结绳尚无定论。
writingSystem.en: No fully developed writing system is known; administration and memory likely relied on cords, visual symbols, architectural ordering, and oral tradition, though whether the Wari used early record-keeping cords remains uncertain.

culturalAchievements.zh: 瓦里以高水平城市规划、矩形围墙建筑群、远距离行政中心布局、精致多色纺织品、标准化陶器、道路整合和跨地区艺术风格传播著称，是安第斯最早形成广域国家体系的文明之一。
culturalAchievements.en: The Wari were notable for advanced urban planning, rectilinear walled compounds, long-distance provincial centers, refined polychrome textiles, standardized ceramics, road integration, and the spread of a transregional artistic style, making them one of the earliest creators of a broad Andean state system.

languageFamily.zh: 语言情况不确定，统治核心可能使用已灭绝或难以辨识的中安第斯语言，也可能与克丘亚语或艾马拉语前身群体发生接触；帝国内部显然是多语言环境。
languageFamily.en: The linguistic situation is uncertain: the ruling core may have spoken an extinct or poorly identifiable Central Andean language, and likely interacted with early Quechuan or Aymaran-related groups; the empire was clearly multilingual.

gdpDescription.zh: 瓦里经济以国家组织农业、牧业、手工业与再分配为核心，不属于货币化市场经济。其总量在前现代安第斯属强大水平，但财富主要表现为粮仓、劳动力、纺织品、牲畜和对生态层带资源的控制，而非金银流通。
gdpDescription.en: The Wari economy was centered on state-organized agriculture, herding, craft production, and redistribution rather than monetized markets. Its total scale was strong by premodern Andean standards, but wealth was expressed chiefly in storehouses, labor power, textiles, livestock, and control over ecological zones rather than circulation of precious metals.

mainIndustries.zh: 梯田农业、灌溉农业、羊驼与美洲驼牧养、纺织、陶器制造、石工与建筑、青铜和铜器制作、长距离运输与仓储管理
mainIndustries.en: Terraced agriculture, irrigated farming, llama and alpaca herding, textile production, ceramic manufacture, stonework and construction, bronze and copper working, long-distance transport, and storehouse management

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、藜麦、马铃薯、辣椒、羊驼毛纺织品、陶器、黑曜石、贝壳、铜器、青铜器、干肉、可卡及沿海与高地之间交换的多样特产
tradeGoods.en: Maize, quinoa, potatoes, chili peppers, camelid-wool textiles, ceramics, obsidian, shell goods, copper tools, bronze objects, dried meat, coca, and a variety of products exchanged between coast and highlands

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、家屋、储粮、纺织工具、陶器及少量骆驼科牲畜；精英财富则体现在精美纺织品、金属器、礼仪器物、对劳动力的控制和接近国家仓储网络的能力。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was mainly held in cultivation rights, dwellings, food stores, weaving tools, ceramics, and small numbers of camelids; elite wealth lay in fine textiles, metal objects, ritual goods, control over labor, and privileged access to state storehouse networks.

economicSystem.zh: 以国家再分配、社区劳动义务和生态互补为基础的指令性经济体系，市场交换存在但从属于政治与礼仪网络。
economicSystem.en: A directive economic system based on state redistribution, communal labor obligations, and ecological complementarity, with market exchange present but subordinate to political and ritual networks

source.zh: 劳役征发与公共工程义务
source.en: Labor levies and public works obligations

category.zh: 赈济、再分配与边疆整合

treasuryDescription.zh: 瓦里“国库”主要不是金银库存，而是分布在首都与各地行政中心的大量仓储资源，包括谷物、干肉、纺织品、工具、礼仪用品和可调配劳动力；贵金属在财政中的作用较小，象征与礼仪意义更强。
treasuryDescription.en: The Wari 'treasury' was not primarily a stockpile of precious metals but a distributed reserve held in the capital and provincial storehouses, including grain, dried meat, textiles, tools, ritual items, and mobilizable labor. Precious metals played a smaller fiscal role and had stronger symbolic and ceremonial value.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向扩大仓储、保障军政中心供给、用再分配换取忠诚，并通过新建行政据点把新征服地区纳入贡赋体系；在丰年积储，在边疆扩张期加强劳役与实物征发。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored expanding storehouses, securing supplies for military and administrative centers, purchasing loyalty through redistribution, and incorporating newly conquered regions into the tribute system through new provincial establishments; in good years reserves were accumulated, while frontier expansion brought heavier labor and in-kind demands.

description.zh: 军队主体，由持矛、投石器、棍棒、斧和盾的步兵组成，适合山地与高原作战，也承担守卫行政中心的任务。
description.en: The core of the army, composed of infantry armed with spears, slings, clubs, axes, and shields, suited to mountain and plateau warfare and also used to guard administrative centers.

description.zh: 部署在省区中心、交通节点和新征服地区，负责治安、威慑与后勤保护，常与行政人员协同行动。
description.en: Stationed at provincial centers, transport nodes, and newly conquered regions, these troops handled order, deterrence, and logistics protection, often operating alongside administrators.

description.zh: 来自附属社区的临时兵力，在远征、修筑工事和危机防御时动员，战斗力低于核心常备军。
description.en: Temporary manpower drawn from subordinate communities, mobilized for campaigns, fortification works, and emergency defense, with lower effectiveness than the core standing troops.

commanderInChief.zh: 瓦里君主及其首席军事贵族
commanderInChief.en: The Wari ruler and his leading military nobles

technology.zh: 军事技术以安第斯传统兵器为主，包括投石器、矛、标枪、棍棒、战斧、防护头饰与纺织护具；强项不在金属武器，而在山地行军、道路后勤、驻防体系和组织动员能力。
technology.en: Military technology was based on traditional Andean weapons including slings, spears, darts, clubs, battle axes, protective headgear, and quilted textile armor; its strength lay less in metal weaponry than in mountain mobility, road logistics, garrison systems, and organizational mobilization.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自尚未整合的安第斯地方势力、边疆反抗、补给线过长以及与蒂亚瓦纳科势力在南部高地和交换网络上的竞争。
threats.en: Main threats came from unconsolidated Andean local powers, frontier resistance, overextended supply lines, and competition with Tiwanaku influence in the southern highlands and exchange networks.

populationDescription.zh: 约一百万人口分布在中央安第斯高地、山谷与部分沿海控制区，人口受国家迁徙政策和省区中心建设影响明显；都城与若干行政中心集中了工匠、官员和驻军。
populationDescription.en: About one million people were distributed across the central Andean highlands, valleys, and some controlled coastal zones, with settlement patterns strongly shaped by state resettlement and the construction of provincial centers; the capital and major administrative hubs concentrated artisans, officials, and garrisons.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、祭司贵族与军事行政精英；中层为地方首领、专业工匠、管理人员和运输组织者；下层为农民、牧民、劳役承担者及被迁移社区。社会流动有限，但通过服务国家与被纳入行政中心体系可获得地位提升。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal house, priestly nobles, and military-administrative elites; the middle included local chiefs, specialist artisans, managers, and transport organizers; below were farmers, herders, labor draftees, and resettled communities. Social mobility was limited, but service to the state and incorporation into administrative centers could improve status.

allies.zh: 无现代意义上的远距离正式盟友；在安第斯内部依靠附属地方精英、合作社区和受保护交换网络维系统治。
allies.en: It had no formal long-distance allies in the modern sense; within the Andes it relied on subordinate local elites, cooperating communities, and protected exchange networks.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是拒绝纳入瓦里体系的地方政体与社群，以及在南部高地具有竞争性的蒂亚瓦纳科势力圈。
enemies.en: Its principal opponents were local polities and communities resisting incorporation into the Wari system, as well as the competing Tiwanaku sphere in the southern highlands.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以区域扩张、建立行政飞地、控制交通与资源节点、吸纳地方首领并通过礼仪和再分配巩固服从为核心，而非通过书面条约维系国际关系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on regional expansion, establishment of administrative enclaves, control of transport and resource nodes, co-optation of local leaders, and reinforcement of submission through ritual and redistribution rather than written treaty diplomacy.

era.zh: 中期地平线时代
era.en: Middle Horizon

keyInnovations.zh: 大规模行政城市规划、标准化围墙复合体、跨区域道路与仓储网络、梯田与高地农业强化、精细挂毯织造、多色陶器风格传播、铜与早期青铜器应用，以及利用国家迁置制度重构人口与生产。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included large-scale administrative urban planning, standardized walled compounds, interregional road and storehouse networks, intensified terraced and highland agriculture, fine tapestry weaving, diffusion of polychrome ceramic styles, use of copper and early bronze objects, and state-directed resettlement to restructure population and production.

strengths.zh: 瓦里在750年左右处于兴盛阶段，优势在于强有力的行政中心网络、道路与仓储后勤、跨生态区资源整合、优秀纺织与工艺生产，以及通过仪式与再分配塑造帝国认同的能力。
strengths.en: Around 750 CE the Wari were in a flourishing phase, with strengths in their powerful network of administrative centers, road and storehouse logistics, integration of resources across ecological zones, excellent textile and craft production, and ability to forge imperial identity through ritual and redistribution.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于对持续劳役征发和地方服从的高度依赖、缺乏文字行政记录的稳定性、边疆控制成本高、核心与外围差异大，以及扩张过快可能导致补给与忠诚体系失衡。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in heavy dependence on continued labor levies and local compliance, limited stability from the absence of writing-based administration, high costs of frontier control, major differences between core and periphery, and the risk that rapid expansion could unbalance supply and loyalty systems.

outlook.zh: 短期前景积极，若能继续维持省区中心、仓储和精英合作，瓦里仍将扩展其影响并主导中安第斯；但从长期看，生态压力、地方离心、政治继承不稳和区域竞争可能削弱帝国整合。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was positive: if it could continue sustaining provincial centers, storehouses, and elite cooperation, the Wari would likely extend their influence and dominate the central Andes. In the longer run, however, ecological stress, local centrifugal tendencies, unstable succession, and regional rivalry could weaken imperial integration.

description.zh: 安第斯中部高地与海岸扩张中的复杂国家体系。
description.en: A complex expanding state system across the central Andean highlands and coast.

dynasty.zh: 中爪哇马打兰王国早期散查亚王朝
dynasty.en: Early Central Javanese Mataram under the Sanjaya line

governmentForm.zh: 以国王为中心的印度化君主制王国，依宫廷、地方领主与宗教权威共同维系统治。
governmentForm.en: An Indianized monarchical kingdom centered on the king, sustained through the court, local lords, and religious authority.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族与地方首领、婆罗门祭司、自由农民、工匠与劳役人口构成。稻作农业与灌溉组织塑造了乡村社会，而宫廷与寺庙则整合了政治与宗教资源。印度教等级观念存在，但在爪哇地方社会中与本土氏族关系相互交织。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, nobles and local chiefs, Brahman priests, free cultivators, artisans, and laboring dependents. Wet-rice agriculture and irrigation organization shaped village life, while court and temple institutions integrated political and religious resources. Indic notions of status existed, but in Java they were intertwined with local clan and territorial relations.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层包括国王、王族、地方领主与受王室供养的宗教精英。王权依靠控制土地、灌溉节点与宗教建造来巩固合法性。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included the king, royal kin, local lords, and religious elites supported by the court. Royal power depended on control over land, irrigation nodes, and religious construction to reinforce legitimacy.

succession.zh: 王位通常由王室世袭继承，但实际安排常受宗族联盟、地方贵族支持与宗教合法性影响。若王支分立，继承可能出现竞争与共治色彩。
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the royal family, but actual succession depended heavily on kin alliances, support from local nobles, and religious legitimacy. Where branches of the dynasty competed, succession could become contested or partially shared.

structure.zh: 马打兰王国的中央行政规模较小，以王宫为核心，通过地方首领与宗教地产管理农村与劳役。王命多通过碑铭、赐地和祭祀网络落实，行政与礼仪高度结合。
structure.en: The central administration of Mataram was relatively compact, centered on the palace and operating through local chiefs and religious landholding institutions to manage villages and labor. Royal authority was often implemented through inscriptions, land grants, and ritual networks, making administration closely tied to ceremony.

function.zh: 处理诏令、土地赐封、贡赋记录与宫廷日常政务。
function.en: Handled decrees, land grants, tribute records, and routine palace administration.

function.zh: 征收稻米、劳役与地方贡品，并调配王室仓储。
function.en: Collected rice, labor dues, and regional tribute, and managed royal storehouses.

function.zh: 管理王室祭祀、婆罗门与寺庙赐地，维护宗教合法性。
function.en: Managed royal ritual, grants to Brahmans and temples, and upheld religious legitimacy.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托村社与地区首领，王权通过任命、联姻与赐地整合各地。对偏远地区的控制较间接，更多依赖贡纳与效忠关系而非严密官僚制。
localAdmin.en: Local rule depended on village communities and regional chiefs, whom the crown integrated through appointments, marriage ties, and land grants. Control over distant areas was relatively indirect, relying more on tribute and loyalty than on dense bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要体现为王命、习惯法与宗教规范的结合，土地边界、贡赋义务与祭祀特权常以碑铭确认。司法较重调解与等级秩序，而非成文法典化。
legalSystem.en: Law was expressed through royal command, customary norms, and religious rules, with land boundaries, tribute obligations, and ritual privileges often confirmed by inscription. Justice emphasized mediation and hierarchical order more than a fully codified legal system.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以稻米、劳役、林产与手工业贡纳为主，部分土地可赐为免役或供寺庙享用。王国财政高度依赖农业剩余与对村社劳动力的组织能力。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based mainly on rice, labor service, forest products, and craft tribute, while some lands were granted exemption or assigned to temples. The kingdom's finances depended heavily on agricultural surplus and the ability to mobilize village labor.

religion.zh: 印度教
religion.en: Hinduism

culturalAchievements.zh: 中爪哇在八世纪已形成重要的碑铭传统、宗教建筑与灌溉社会组织，为后续宏大寺庙群建设奠定基础。王权通过建筑、祭祀与铭文展现印度化王朝文化的地方化形式。
culturalAchievements.en: By the eighth century, Central Java had developed important inscriptional traditions, religious architecture, and irrigation-based social organization that laid the groundwork for later monumental temple complexes. Royal authority expressed a localized form of Indianized court culture through architecture, ritual, and inscriptions.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系马来-波利尼西亚语族，以古爪哇语为本地主要语言，梵语用于高等宗教与铭文。
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Malayo-Polynesian branch, with Old Javanese as the main local language and Sanskrit used for high religion and inscriptions.

philosophy.zh: 以湿婆派印度教为主，融合本土祖灵崇拜与山岳圣地观念。
philosophy.en: Predominantly Shaivite Hinduism, blended with indigenous ancestor veneration and sacred mountain concepts.

writingSystem.zh: 使用源自婆罗米系的古爪哇—南印度文字传统，铭文多见于梵语与古爪哇语。
writingSystem.en: It used an Indic-derived script tradition linked to Old Javanese and South Indian models, with inscriptions commonly in Sanskrit and Old Javanese.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 马打兰经济以中爪哇火山平原的稻作农业为核心，辅以林产、手工业与海上交换。虽然人口规模有限，但土地肥沃、灌溉有效，使其人均产出在区域内相对可观。
gdpDescription.en: The Mataram economy was centered on wet-rice agriculture in the fertile volcanic plains of Central Java, supplemented by forest products, crafts, and maritime exchange. Though small in population, fertile land and effective irrigation made per capita output relatively strong by regional standards.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、木材与林产、铜铁手工业、纺织、石雕与宗教建筑
mainIndustries.en: Wet-rice agriculture, timber and forest products, copper and iron craft production, textiles, stone carving, and religious construction

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、木材、树脂、香料转运品、龟甲、纺织品、金属器、珠饰
tradeGoods.en: Rice, timber, resins, transshipped spices, tortoiseshell, textiles, metal goods, and bead ornaments

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在稻田使用权、灌溉参与权、牲畜与储粮。贵族与寺庙则掌握更多土地、劳役人口与礼器财富。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth lay mainly in rice-field rights, access to irrigation, livestock, and stored grain. Nobles and temples held larger concentrations of land, dependent labor, and ritual valuables.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济形态以农业贡赋与劳役组织为基础，辅以区域海贸和王室分配。市场存在但规模有限，政治权力与土地控制紧密相连。
economicSystem.en: The economic system rested on agrarian tribute and labor mobilization, supplemented by regional maritime trade and royal redistribution. Markets existed but were limited in scale, and political power was closely tied to control over land.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Forest products, crafts, and tribute goods

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
