taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财富多以粮仓、金银器、祭器、牲畜与珍贵林产形式储存，现钱化程度不高。储备足以支撑宫廷与宗教工程，但难以长期承担大规模远征。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal wealth was stored mainly as grain stocks, gold and silver objects, ritual goods, livestock, and valuable forest products rather than highly monetized cash reserves. These reserves were enough for court maintenance and religious building, but less suited to sustained large-scale campaigning.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在确保稻作产出、维持灌溉、巩固王室对赐地与劳役的控制，并借宗教工程强化权威。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on securing rice output, maintaining irrigation, preserving royal control over grants and labor, and strengthening authority through religious construction.

description.zh: 守卫王宫与都城，兼具仪仗与精锐作战职能。
description.en: Protected the palace and capital and served as both ceremonial troops and elite fighters.

description.zh: 由地方首领率领，负责内地治安、征发与陆上作战。
description.en: Led by regional chiefs, they handled internal security, mobilization, and land warfare.

description.zh: 使用舟船执行沿海巡逻、河运护卫与岛际联络。
description.en: Used boats for coastal patrol, river transport protection, and inter-island communication.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王散查亚为最高统帅，实际由王族将领与地方领主分领军队
commanderInChief.en: King Sanjaya was the supreme commander, with royal kinsmen and local chiefs leading forces in practice

technology.zh: 军备以长矛、刀剑、弓箭、藤木盾与轻甲为主，适应热带山地与稻田地形。水上力量依赖木船机动，攻城与重装能力有限。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on spears, swords, bows, rattan or wooden shields, and light armor suited to tropical uplands and rice-field terrain. Waterborne forces relied on wooden craft mobility, while siege and heavy-armored capabilities were limited.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自爪哇内部地方势力竞争、邻近王国的权力角逐，以及海上交通线的不稳定。
threats.en: The main threats came from internal competition among Javanese regional powers, rivalry with neighboring polities, and instability along maritime routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于中爪哇肥沃火山平原与灌溉稻作区，村社组织是社会基本单元。城市化程度较低，政治中心更接近宫廷—宗教复合聚落而非大型古典城市。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the fertile volcanic plains and irrigated rice zones of Central Java, with the village as the basic social unit. Urbanization was limited, and political centers resembled court-ritual complexes more than large classical cities.

socialClasses.zh: 王族、贵族与地方首领、婆罗门与祭司、自由农民、工匠商人、劳役人口与附庸
socialClasses.en: Royal kin, nobles and regional chiefs, Brahmans and priests, free farmers, artisans and traders, laboring dependents, and subordinates

allies.zh: 与部分爪哇地方势力及群岛贸易伙伴保持灵活联盟，并可能通过婚姻与宗教关系巩固联系。
allies.en: It maintained flexible alliances with local Javanese powers and archipelagic trade partners, likely strengthened through marriage and religious ties.

enemies.zh: 潜在对手包括爪哇岛内竞争王权以及控制海上贸易节点的邻近势力。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included rival Javanese dynastic centers and neighboring powers competing for control of maritime trade nodes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以区域均衡、海贸联络与宗教威望展示为主，重点在于稳固中爪哇腹地并参与群岛交换网络。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on regional balance, maritime commercial contact, and displays of religious prestige, with priority given to securing the Central Javanese heartland and participating in archipelagic exchange networks.

era.zh: 早期东南亚印度化王国阶段
era.en: Early Indianized Southeast Asian kingdom phase

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的稻田灌溉组织、碑铭文化、石构宗教建筑技术与群岛航海经验是其关键能力。
keyInnovations.en: Its key capabilities included organized wet-rice irrigation, inscriptional culture, stone religious architecture, and archipelagic seafaring knowledge.

strengths.zh: 土地肥沃、农业效率较高、王权与宗教结合紧密，并掌握群岛贸易中的有利位置。
strengths.en: It benefited from fertile land, relatively efficient agriculture, a close union of kingship and religion, and a favorable position in archipelagic trade.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与财政规模较小，中央官僚能力有限，对地方首领和礼仪合法性的依赖较强。
weaknesses.en: Its population and fiscal scale were small, central bureaucratic capacity was limited, and it depended heavily on local chiefs and ritual legitimacy.

outlook.zh: 若能继续整合中爪哇农业腹地并扩大宗教工程与海贸影响，王国有望上升为群岛重要强权。
outlook.en: If it continued to consolidate the Central Javanese agrarian heartland and expand religious building and maritime influence, the kingdom could rise into a major regional power.

description.zh: 约750年的马打兰王国是一个以中爪哇稻作与印度教王权为基础、正在形成区域影响力的兴盛王国。
description.en: Around 750, the Mataram Kingdom was a thriving Central Javanese realm built on wet-rice agriculture and Hindu kingship, and was growing into a significant regional power.

ruler.zh: 拉利塔迪迪亚·穆克塔皮达

dynasty.zh: 迦尔拘多王朝时期的克什米尔王国
dynasty.en: Kingdom of Kashmir under the Karkota dynasty

governmentForm.zh: 以国王为核心的山地君主国，兼具梵化宫廷政治、地方豪强网络与军事扩张色彩。
governmentForm.en: A mountain monarchy centered on the king, combining Sanskritized court politics, local elite networks, and an expansionist military character.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、婆罗门与文官、军事贵族、村社农民、工匠商旅及寺院附属人口构成。山谷农业、山地通道与国际商路并存，使社会兼具定居农耕与边疆军事特征。宗教上印度教占主导，但佛教遗产与地方信仰仍有影响。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, Brahmans and officials, military nobles, village cultivators, artisans and merchants, and dependents of temples and estates. Valley agriculture, mountain corridors, and international trade routes gave it both settled agrarian and frontier-military features. Hinduism predominated, though Buddhist legacies and local cults still retained influence.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要包括国王、王族、婆罗门官僚与军事贵族。拉利塔迪迪亚时期王权强势，能够在较大范围内调动人力、财富与远征资源。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was made up chiefly of the king, royal kin, Brahman bureaucrats, and military nobles. Under Lalitaditya, kingship was especially strong and could mobilize manpower, wealth, and resources for long-distance campaigns.

succession.zh: 王位原则上由王族世袭，但宫廷政争、王族分支与大臣支持常决定继承成败。若君主强势，继承较稳；一旦中央衰弱，则易生政变。
succession.en: The throne was in principle hereditary within the royal house, but court intrigues, rival branches, and ministerial backing often determined the outcome. Succession was steadier under a strong king, but coups became more likely when the center weakened.

structure.zh: 克什米尔王国中央政务由王宫与文官机构主导，王命通过税收、军役与寺庙土地管理落实。由于地形复杂，地方控制依赖谷地行政中心、要塞与附庸关系相结合。
structure.en: Central government in Kashmir was directed by the palace and civil officials, with royal orders enforced through taxation, military obligations, and oversight of temple lands. Because of difficult terrain, local control relied on valley administrative centers, forts, and subordinate political ties.

function.zh: 起草诏令、记录封赐、处理宫廷文书与外交往来。
function.en: Drafted decrees, recorded grants, and handled palace documentation and diplomacy.

function.zh: 征收谷物、关税与附庸贡赋，并管理仓储与调运。
function.en: Collected grain, customs dues, and tributary payments, and managed storage and transport.

function.zh: 统筹征兵、山口防御、远征补给与要塞守备。
function.en: Oversaw recruitment, mountain-pass defense, expedition logistics, and fortress garrisons.

function.zh: 管理宗教地产、地权争议与王国司法裁断。
function.en: Managed religious estates, land disputes, and royal judicial decisions.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政围绕克什米尔谷地、山口要塞与附属地区展开，地方官与军事首领并行。对外围山区和通道地带的控制常依赖驻军与藩属关系，而非完全直接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized around the Kashmir Valley, mountain forts, and subordinate regions, with civil officers and military commanders operating side by side. Control over outer highlands and corridor zones often depended on garrisons and tributary ties rather than fully direct rule.

legalSystem.zh: 司法结合王命、婆罗门法学传统与地方习惯，土地、赋税与贵族权利是常见争议主题。国王在重大案件中拥有最终裁断权。
legalSystem.en: Justice combined royal decree, Brahmanical legal learning, and local custom, with land, taxation, and elite privilege among the most common matters of dispute. The king retained final authority in major cases.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以谷物地租、畜产、山口商税、手工业贡赋与附庸输纳为主。王国凭借地理位置，能够从跨山贸易与通道控制中获得重要收入。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation drew on grain land revenues, livestock dues, mountain customs, craft tribute, and payments from subordinate territories. Thanks to its location, the kingdom earned important income from trans-montane trade and corridor control.

religion.zh: 印度教
religion.en: Hinduism

culturalAchievements.zh: 拉利塔迪迪亚时期建筑、寺庙营建与宫廷赞助显著，马尔坦德太阳神庙代表了克什米尔艺术与王权象征的高峰。克什米尔亦是北印度与中亚之间重要的学术与宗教交流节点。
culturalAchievements.en: Under Lalitaditya, architecture, temple building, and court patronage were especially notable, with the Martand Sun Temple representing a peak of Kashmiri art and royal symbolism. Kashmir also served as an important scholarly and religious contact zone between northern India and Central Asia.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系印度-雅利安语支，梵语为高文化语言，地方通俗语属达尔德—印度-雅利安连续体。
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Indo-Aryan branch, with Sanskrit as the high cultural language and local vernaculars belonging to the Dardic-Indo-Aryan continuum.

philosophy.zh: 以印度教王权思想和婆罗门学术传统为主，同时保留佛教与地方山岳圣地文化遗存。
philosophy.en: Its outlook was dominated by Hindu kingship and Brahmanical scholarship, while preserving Buddhist and local sacred-mountain traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 使用梵文书写传统及早期夏拉达文字发展形态，碑铭与宫廷文书以梵语文化为核心。
writingSystem.en: It used the Sanskrit scribal tradition and early forms developing toward Sharada script, with inscriptions and court documents rooted in Sanskrit culture.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 克什米尔经济建立在谷地农业、手工业与山地商路收益之上，人均产出在同时代山区国家中较高。其财富既来自稻麦果园等定居农业，也来自连接印度、中亚与喜马拉雅区域的贸易通道。
gdpDescription.en: Kashmir's economy rested on valley agriculture, craft production, and income from mountain trade routes, giving it relatively high per capita output for a contemporary upland kingdom. Its wealth came both from settled farming in rice, wheat, and orchards and from corridors linking India, Central Asia, and the Himalayan world.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷地农业、果园、畜牧、纺织、木石建筑、金属工艺与转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Valley agriculture, orchards, pastoralism, textiles, wood and stone construction, metalwork, and transit trade

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、羊毛、毛织品、木材、药材、藏红花类香料、马匹、金属器、宝石转运品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wool, woolen textiles, timber, medicinal goods, saffron-like aromatics, horses, metalware, and transshipped gemstones

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富体现在耕地、果树、牲畜、羊毛与储粮，城市与宫廷周边则聚集工匠与贸易财富。贵族与寺庙掌握较多地产和税收特权。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held in fields, orchards, livestock, wool, and grain stores, while towns and the court zone concentrated craft and trade wealth. Nobles and temples controlled large estates and important tax privileges.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济兼具农业地租、王室再分配与商路税收特征，国家对通道、要塞与附庸区具有较强汲取能力。
economicSystem.en: The economy combined agrarian land revenue, royal redistribution, and trade-route taxation, with the state exercising significant extractive power over passes, forts, and subordinate regions.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Agrarian land revenue and grain taxes

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Tribute from subordinates and gains from campaigns

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备包含粮食、金银器、货币、牲畜与战利品，山国财政对战争成败与商路畅通较为敏感。拉利塔迪迪亚时期因扩张与通商，财政能力处于高位。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury held grain, precious vessels, coin, livestock, and war gains, and the finances of this mountain kingdom were sensitive to both military success and secure trade routes. Under Lalitaditya, expansion and commerce placed fiscal capacity near its peak.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调控制谷地农业与山口贸易，维持军队机动与大型建筑赞助，并通过附庸体系扩大资源输入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized control of valley agriculture and mountain trade, support for military mobility and monumental patronage, and the expansion of resource inflows through subordinate relations.

description.zh: 直属国王，适合快速打击与保护王都及远征主力。
description.en: Directly attached to the king, suited for rapid strikes and protection of the capital and expeditionary core.

description.zh: 熟悉山谷、山口与要塞作战，是王国防御与征讨的基础兵力。
description.en: Experienced in valley, pass, and fortress warfare, they formed the backbone of defense and campaigning.

description.zh: 由附属地区与地方贵族提供，在大战时扩充军势。
description.en: Supplied by subordinate territories and local nobles, they expanded the army in major campaigns.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王拉利塔迪迪亚·穆克塔皮达为最高统帅，下设王族将领与边关统军
commanderInChief.en: King Lalitaditya Muktapida served as supreme commander, beneath whom stood royal generals and frontier commanders

technology.zh: 军队装备弓箭、长枪、刀剑、甲胄、战马与攻守城器械，重视山地行军与要塞控制。其战法结合印度北方传统与山地机动，适于跨谷地和通道作战。
technology.en: The army used bows, spears, swords, armor, warhorses, and siege equipment, with strong emphasis on mountain movement and fortress control. Its methods combined northern Indian traditions with highland mobility, making it effective in valley and corridor warfare.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西北通道势力、旁遮普与喜马拉雅周边竞争者，以及过度扩张导致的补给压力和附庸离心。
threats.en: The chief threats came from powers along the northwestern corridors, rivals in Punjab and the Himalayan margins, and from the logistical strain and centrifugal tendencies produced by overexpansion.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中在克什米尔谷地及其周边可耕区，山地居民分布较稀疏。都城与宗教中心兼具行政、商业与手工业职能，但总体仍以农业人口为主。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated mainly in the Kashmir Valley and nearby cultivable zones, while highland populations were more sparse. The capital and religious centers served administrative, commercial, and craft functions, but the kingdom remained predominantly agrarian.

socialClasses.zh: 王族、婆罗门与官僚、军事贵族、商人与工匠、自由农民、佃户与寺院附属人口
socialClasses.en: Royal kin, Brahmans and officials, military nobles, merchants and artisans, free peasants, tenants, and temple dependents

allies.zh: 与部分喜马拉雅山地政权、附庸领地及商路节点保持从属或合作关系。
allies.en: It maintained subordinate or cooperative ties with certain Himalayan hill polities, dependent territories, and key trade-route nodes.

enemies.zh: 主要对手包括旁遮普与西北方向的竞争政权，以及任何威胁山口与商路控制的地区势力。
enemies.en: Its main adversaries included competing polities in Punjab and the northwest, along with any regional powers threatening control of passes and trade routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以军事扩张、控制山口商道和树立王权威望为核心，兼顾与中亚、北印度及喜马拉雅地区的贸易与政治联系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on military expansion, control of mountain trade corridors, and the projection of royal prestige, while maintaining commercial and political links with Central Asia, northern India, and the Himalayan world.

era.zh: 早期中世纪喜马拉雅—北印度高地王国阶段
era.en: Early medieval Himalayan-north Indian highland kingdom phase

keyInnovations.zh: 山地要塞体系、石构寺庙建筑、跨山商路组织与高地军队机动能力是其关键技术与制度优势。
keyInnovations.en: Its key technological and institutional strengths lay in mountain fortification systems, stone temple architecture, management of trans-montane trade routes, and highland military mobility.

strengths.zh: 地理位置优越、王权强势、军队善于山地作战，且能同时受益于农业与商路税收。
strengths.en: It enjoyed an advantageous location, strong kingship, an army skilled in mountain warfare, and revenues drawn from both agriculture and trade corridors.

weaknesses.zh: 国土受地形限制，人口基础不大，远征补给困难，扩张成果难以长期稳定整合。
weaknesses.en: Its terrain constrained population size, long-distance logistics were difficult, and gains from expansion were hard to integrate on a lasting basis.

outlook.zh: 在强君统治下仍具扩张与繁荣潜力，但若继承不稳或对外战线过长，王国将迅速面临财政与统治压力。
outlook.en: Under a strong ruler it retained considerable room for expansion and prosperity, but unstable succession or overextended campaigns could quickly create fiscal and political strain.

description.zh: 约750年的克什米尔王国是在拉利塔迪迪亚统治下兼具山地军事力量、宗教建筑辉煌与跨区域贸易优势的强盛王国。
description.en: Around 750, the Kingdom of Kashmir under Lalitaditya was a powerful realm combining mountain military strength, impressive religious patronage, and advantages in interregional trade.

ruler.zh: 穆罕默德·本·卡西姆/倭马亚总督

dynasty.zh: 倭马亚王朝治下信德行省
dynasty.en: Province of Sind under the Umayyad Caliphate

governmentForm.zh: 由倭马亚哈里发任命总督统治的伊斯兰边疆行省，兼具军事占领与地方协商色彩。
governmentForm.en: An Islamic frontier province ruled by a governor appointed by the Umayyad caliph, combining military occupation with local negotiation.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由阿拉伯驻军与官员、皈依或受保护的本地地主与商人、乡村耕作者及手工业者构成。穆斯林社群在法律与军政上居优势地位，而佛教徒、印度教徒等非穆斯林多以缴纳人头税和地税换取自治与保护。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of Arab garrisons and officials, local landholders and merchants who either converted or accepted protected status, and rural cultivators and artisans. Muslim communities held advantages in law and military administration, while non-Muslims such as Buddhists and Hindus generally retained local autonomy in exchange for paying poll and land taxes.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层以阿拉伯军政精英为核心，并吸纳部分本地首领、税吏与城市商人协助治理。沿河城市中的穆斯林法学者与商人地位日益上升。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was centered on Arab military-administrative elites, while incorporating select local chiefs, tax officials, and urban merchants into governance. Muslim jurists and merchants in riverine towns were gaining increasing influence.

succession.zh: 最高权力并非本地世袭，而由大马士革朝廷任命与撤换总督。地方层面则常保留原有豪族与酋长的半世袭地位。
succession.en: Ultimate authority was not locally hereditary but depended on appointment and dismissal by Damascus. At the local level, however, many aristocratic and chieftain positions remained semi-hereditary.

structure.zh: 信德作为倭马亚帝国的边疆行省，由总督统辖军政、税务与司法。城市设驻军和征税机构，乡村地区依赖本地头人与旧有行政网络执行命令。
structure.en: Sind functioned as a frontier province of the Umayyad Empire, with the governor overseeing military affairs, taxation, and justice. Cities housed garrisons and tax offices, while rural areas relied on local headmen and older administrative networks to implement orders.

function.zh: 统筹军政命令、任命地方官并处理与哈里发朝廷的往来。
function.en: Coordinated military and civil orders, appointed local officials, and handled communications with the caliphal court.

function.zh: 征收地税、贡赋与人头税，登记灌溉地、耕地与城镇商税。
function.en: Collected land tax, tribute, and poll tax, and recorded irrigated land, cultivated fields, and urban commercial dues.

function.zh: 处理穆斯林社群的宗教法事务，并监督受保护民的契约与纠纷裁决。
function.en: Handled matters of Islamic law for Muslim communities and supervised contracts and dispute settlement involving protected non-Muslim subjects.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以曼苏拉、代布尔等城市为节点，周边乡村由酋长、村社长老和税吏协作管理。阿拉伯官员通常掌握财政与驻军，本地精英维持日常征收与治安。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized around towns such as Mansura and Daybul, while surrounding countryside was managed through chiefs, village elders, and tax collectors. Arab officials generally controlled finance and garrisons, whereas local elites handled routine collection and order.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系呈双轨并行：穆斯林适用伊斯兰法，非穆斯林在婚姻、社群事务上多保留本习惯法。重大的赋税、叛乱与军政案件则由总督和卡迪裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was dual in character: Muslims were governed by Islamic law, while non-Muslims often retained customary law in marriage and communal matters. Major tax, rebellion, and military-administrative cases were decided by the governor and qadi.

taxationSystem.zh: 主要税收包括地税、非穆斯林人头税、贸易关税与战利性收入。河谷灌溉农业是税基核心，港口贸易则提供额外现金收入。
taxationSystem.en: Main taxes included land tax, poll tax on non-Muslims, customs duties, and income derived from conquest. Irrigated agriculture in the Indus valley formed the fiscal core, while port trade supplied additional cash revenue.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教
religion.en: Islam

culturalAchievements.zh: 信德是伊斯兰世界进入印度次大陆的重要门户，促进了阿拉伯行政语言、货币与宗教制度的传播。港口与河运城镇也推动了跨区域商业文化的融合。
culturalAchievements.en: Sind served as a major gateway for the Islamic world into the Indian subcontinent, helping spread Arabic administrative language, coinage, and religious institutions. Its ports and river towns also fostered the blending of long-distance commercial cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 闪含语系阿拉伯语与印度-雅利安语支的早期信德语、普拉克里特语并存
languageFamily.en: Semitic Arabic coexisted with Indo-Aryan early Sindhi and Prakrit varieties

philosophy.zh: 以早期伊斯兰政治宗教秩序为主，同时与印度河流域既有的佛教、婆罗门教和地方传统并存。
philosophy.en: It was shaped primarily by early Islamic politico-religious order while coexisting with the older Buddhist, Brahmanical, and regional traditions of the Indus basin.

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯字母与当地婆罗米系书写并用。
writingSystem.en: Arabic script coexisted with local Brahmi-derived scripts.

unit.en: silver dirham equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础在于印度河下游灌溉农业、港口转运与区域商税。相较内地大国，信德规模较小，但因连接阿拉伯海与内陆河网而具有高于人口规模的贸易价值。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on lower Indus irrigation agriculture, port transit, and regional customs revenues. Compared with major inland states, Sind was small in scale, but its links between the Arabian Sea and inland river routes gave it commercial importance beyond its population size.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、港口贸易、纺织、盐业、畜产、河运
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, port trade, textiles, salt production, livestock, river transport

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、靛蓝、棉布、枣、盐、马匹、香料转口品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, indigo, cotton cloth, dates, salt, horses, re-exported spices

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人与地主家庭财富较高，乡村农户多依赖灌溉收成维持温饱，贫富差距明显。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchants and landholding families enjoyed relatively high wealth, while rural households depended heavily on irrigation harvests for subsistence, creating marked inequality.

unit.en: silver dirham equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以农业税基和港口贸易为核心的行省经济，结合伊斯兰财政制度与本地地权、村社和商人网络。
economicSystem.en: A provincial economy centered on agrarian taxation and port commerce, blending Islamic fiscal practice with local landholding, village, and merchant networks.

treasuryDescription.zh: 行省财政有一定结余，但仍受哈里发政局、边疆战事和地方叛服变化影响。储备足以应对中等规模军事行动与赈济。
treasuryDescription.en: The provincial treasury held modest reserves, though it remained vulnerable to caliphal politics, frontier warfare, and shifts in local loyalty. Its holdings were sufficient for medium-scale military action and relief.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调稳定征地税与维护港口税源，并以减免或协商方式争取地方豪族合作。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized stable land-tax collection and protection of port revenues, while using negotiated exemptions and arrangements to secure cooperation from local elites.

description.zh: 核心机动部队，负责镇压叛乱、边境巡防与快速突击。
description.en: The core mobile arm used for suppressing revolts, frontier patrols, and rapid strikes.

description.zh: 驻守曼苏拉、代布尔等据点，承担城防、治安与河道控制。
description.en: Stationed in Mansura, Daybul, and other strongpoints, they handled defense, security, and control of river routes.

description.zh: 由本地部族和附庸首领提供，熟悉地形但忠诚度不一。
description.en: Supplied by local tribes and subordinate chiefs; familiar with terrain but uneven in loyalty.

commanderInChief.zh: 倭马亚总督
commanderInChief.en: Umayyad Governor

technology.zh: 装备以骑兵弓矛、剑盾、皮甲和攻城器械为主，兼用河运补给与城寨体系。军事技术承袭阿拉伯—伊拉克传统，并吸收印度地方战法。
technology.en: Equipment centered on cavalry bows and lances, swords and shields, leather armor, and siege equipment, supported by river transport and fortified positions. Military practice drew on Arab-Iraqi traditions while absorbing Indian local methods.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自本地反抗、上游印度势力、沙漠与边境部族骚动，以及倭马亚帝国内部权力更替带来的统治不稳。
threats.en: Main threats came from local resistance, Indian powers upstream, unrest among desert and frontier tribes, and instability caused by succession struggles within the Umayyad Empire.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于印度河下游灌溉带和沿海港口，乡村农民占绝大多数。城市中阿拉伯军民、地方商人、工匠与宗教社群混居。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the irrigated lower Indus and coastal ports, with peasants forming the large majority. Urban centers hosted mixed communities of Arab settlers, local merchants, artisans, and religious groups.

socialClasses.zh: 阿拉伯军政阶层、地方豪族与商人、城镇工匠、农民、附庸部族与奴仆。
socialClasses.en: Arab military-administrative elites, local aristocrats and merchants, urban artisans, peasants, subordinate tribes, and slaves/servants.

allies.zh: 名义上依附倭马亚哈里发国，并与部分地方首领保持协作关系。
allies.en: Nominally aligned with the Umayyad Caliphate and maintained cooperative arrangements with selected local chiefs.

enemies.zh: 敌对者包括反抗的本地王公、印度西北诸势力，以及敌视阿拉伯扩张的边地部族。
enemies.en: Enemies included rebellious local rulers, powers in northwestern India, and frontier tribes hostile to Arab expansion.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以稳固边疆、控制河海交通和确保赋税来源为主，较少追求大规模内陆扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on securing the frontier, controlling river-sea communications, and preserving revenue sources, with less emphasis on major inland expansion.

era.zh: 8世纪中叶早期伊斯兰—南亚边疆技术阶段
era.en: Mid-8th-century early Islamic-South Asian frontier technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 阿拉伯铸币与行政文书、灌溉管理、港口税务组织、骑兵机动与攻城技术的结合。
keyInnovations.en: The combination of Arabic coinage and documentary administration, irrigation management, port customs organization, and mobile cavalry with siege techniques.

strengths.zh: 扼守印度河口与阿拉伯海航线，财政依赖农业与商税双重来源，且有帝国支援与伊斯兰军政组织优势。
strengths.en: It controlled the Indus delta and Arabian Sea routes, drew revenue from both agriculture and trade, and benefited from imperial backing and organized Islamic military administration.

weaknesses.zh: 人口与腹地有限，统治依赖少数驻军和地方合作，若帝国中心动荡则易失控。
weaknesses.en: Its limited population and hinterland meant rule depended on small garrisons and local collaboration, making it vulnerable when the imperial center was unstable.

outlook.zh: 短期内可维持稳定边疆行省地位，但长期命运取决于哈里发国政局与对本地精英的整合能力。
outlook.en: In the short term it could remain a stable frontier province, but its long-term fate depended on caliphal politics and its ability to integrate local elites.

description.zh: 信德王国在750年前后实为倭马亚治下的伊斯兰边疆行省，以印度河农业和港口贸易支撑其统治。
description.en: Around 750, Sind was effectively an Islamic frontier province under Umayyad rule, sustained by Indus agriculture and port commerce.

dynasty.zh: 后期遮娄其王朝（巴达米遮娄其）

governmentForm.zh: 由世袭君主统治、以地方封臣和宗教土地授予体系支撑的印度王国。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Indian monarchy supported by regional feudatories and a land-grant system tied to religious institutions.

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构以王室、封臣贵族、婆罗门、寺庙机构、商人行会、农民与工匠为主。种姓与地域共同塑造社会秩序，王权通过赐地、仪式和军事征服维系统合。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the royal house, feudatory nobles, Brahmins, temple establishments, merchant guilds, peasants, and artisans. Caste and region jointly shaped social order, while royal authority was maintained through land grants, ritual, and military conquest.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层包括王族、王朝宗亲、地方封臣、军政贵族与受赐地的婆罗门精英。寺庙与大地主在地方事务中拥有重要影响力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included the royal family, dynastic kinsmen, regional feudatories, military-administrative nobles, and Brahmin elites endowed with land grants. Temples and major landholders exerted strong influence in local affairs.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部继承，但实践中常受兄弟支系、王子与强势贵族支持所左右。军功与实际控制力在继位中与血统同样重要。
succession.en: The throne normally passed within the royal family, but in practice succession was shaped by rival collateral lines, princes, and powerful noble backing. Military success and effective control were often as important as bloodline.

structure.zh: 遮娄其政权以国王为中心，中央朝廷负责财政、军务、赐地与宗教赞助。地方由封臣、省级长官和村社共同管理，中央对边远地区多采取间接控制。
structure.en: The Chalukya state was centered on the king, with the royal court managing finance, warfare, land grants, and religious patronage. Local administration rested on feudatories, provincial governors, and village communities, with the center often exercising indirect control over distant areas.

function.zh: 起草敕令、记录赐地、管理碑铭与朝廷文书。
function.en: Drafted royal orders, recorded land grants, and managed inscriptions and court documents.

function.zh: 核定土地、征收田赋、监督王室直辖地和部分贸易税。
function.en: Assessed land, collected agrarian taxes, and supervised crown lands and certain trade levies.

function.zh: 调度王军与封臣兵，组织边境防御和远征。
function.en: Mobilized royal troops and feudatory contingents, organizing frontier defense and campaigns.

function.zh: 管理对寺庙、婆罗门和学术机构的土地及税收豁免。
function.en: Managed land and tax exemptions granted to temples, Brahmins, and learned institutions.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由省、区、村多层组织组成，封臣与地方长官承担征税、治安与征兵。村社在灌溉、耕地分配和纠纷处理上拥有实际自治功能。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through multiple levels of province, district, and village, with feudatories and local governors responsible for tax collection, order, and levies. Village communities exercised practical autonomy in irrigation, land allocation, and dispute resolution.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以达摩沙斯特拉传统、王命和地方习惯法并行为特征。土地、婚姻和种姓事务常由婆罗门、村社或寺庙参与裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law combined Dharmashastra tradition, royal command, and local customary practice. Land, marriage, and caste matters were often adjudicated with participation from Brahmins, village bodies, or temples.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以土地税为主，辅以灌溉费、市场税、道路税和附庸贡赋。大量宗教赐地享有减免，使国家财政对王室直辖地和商税更为依赖。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was centered on land revenue, supplemented by irrigation dues, market taxes, road tolls, and tribute from subordinates. Extensive religious endowments enjoyed exemptions, making the state more dependent on crown lands and commercial taxation.

religion.zh: 印度教
religion.en: Hinduism

culturalAchievements.zh: 遮娄其时代以巴达米、帕塔达卡尔、艾霍勒等地的岩窟与结构式神庙建筑著称，形成南北建筑风格交汇的重要阶段。宫廷和宗教赞助也推动了梵语与卡纳达语文学的发展。
culturalAchievements.en: The Chalukya age is renowned for the rock-cut and structural temples of Badami, Pattadakal, and Aihole, marking a major stage in the meeting of northern and southern architectural forms. Courtly and religious patronage also advanced both Sanskrit and Kannada literary culture.

languageFamily.zh: 德拉威语系卡纳达语与印欧语系梵语并用
languageFamily.en: Dravidian Kannada coexisted with Indo-European Sanskrit

philosophy.zh: 以婆罗门教—印度教思想为主，兼有耆那教和佛教余绪，重视王权仪式、达摩秩序与寺庙文化。
philosophy.en: It was shaped mainly by Brahmanical-Hindu thought, with residual Jain and Buddhist influence, emphasizing royal ritual, dharma, and temple culture.

writingSystem.zh: 以早期卡纳达文、梵文书写传统并用，碑铭多见南婆罗米系统演变字体。
writingSystem.en: Early Kannada and Sanskrit written traditions coexisted, using scripts derived from southern Brahmi.

unit.en: silver dinara equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以旱作与灌溉农业为基础，并由寺庙中心、手工业和内陆贸易网络强化。德干高原的区域整合使其具备较稳固的农产税基和工艺生产能力。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on dry and irrigated agriculture, reinforced by temple centers, craft production, and inland trade networks. Regional integration across the Deccan plateau gave it a relatively solid agrarian tax base and artisanal capacity.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、寺庙经济、石工建筑、纺织、金属加工、畜牧
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, temple economy, stone construction, textiles, metalworking, livestock

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、粟类、棉布、胡椒与香料转运品、铁器、铜器、宝石、象牙
tradeGoods.en: Rice, millets, cotton cloth, re-exported pepper and spices, iron goods, copper goods, gemstones, ivory

name.en: Gold coinage and local silver-copper coinage

householdWealth.zh: 寺庙、地主和商人家庭财富集中，普通农户财富较低但在良好收成时可维持稳定生活。工匠依附城镇与宗教中心，收入受赞助与市场波动影响。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was concentrated among temples, landlords, and merchant families, while ordinary cultivators had limited assets but could maintain stability in good harvest years. Artisans tied to towns and religious centers saw incomes fluctuate with patronage and market demand.

unit.en: silver dinara equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以土地收益和地方封建关系为核心，辅以寺庙财富、商人行会和区域市场交换的复合经济。
economicSystem.en: A composite economy centered on land revenue and feudal-local relationships, supplemented by temple wealth, merchant guilds, and regional market exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库较为充裕，足以支持大型宗教工程与季节性战争，但若连续远征或遭遇歉收则会迅速承压。实物储备与贵金属并存。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was fairly healthy, sufficient to support large religious projects and seasonal warfare, but prolonged campaigns or poor harvests could strain it quickly. Reserves existed in both kind and precious metal.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持农税稳定、通过附庸体系分摊军费，并以宗教赞助巩固合法性与地方支持。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on stabilizing agrarian revenue, spreading military burdens through the feudatory system, and using religious patronage to reinforce legitimacy and local support.

description.zh: 作为主要守备和攻城力量，负责据点控制与战线维持。
description.en: Served as the main defensive and siege force, holding strongpoints and sustaining battle lines.

description.zh: 用于快速机动、突击与追击，精锐部分由国王近臣和强大封臣提供。
description.en: Used for rapid movement, shock action, and pursuit, with elite elements supplied by royal retainers and powerful feudatories.

description.zh: 在决战和威慑中具有象征与实战作用，常与步兵协同推进。
description.en: Played both symbolic and practical roles in set-piece battles and deterrence, often advancing with infantry support.

commanderInChief.zh: 超日王二世
commanderInChief.en: Vikramaditya II

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制兵器、城防、攻城器、战象运用和区域骑兵传统为核心。德干地形促使军队重视要塞控制与季节性调兵。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, fortifications, siege devices, war elephants, and regional cavalry practice. Deccan terrain encouraged emphasis on fortress control and seasonal campaigning.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南方帕拉瓦、地方封臣离心以及德干诸竞争王朝的边境冲突。王权一旦衰弱，附庸体系可能迅速瓦解。
threats.en: Main threats came from the Pallavas in the south, centrifugal feudatories, and frontier conflict with rival Deccan dynasties. If royal authority weakened, the subordinate system could unravel quickly.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以农村农业社群为主，聚居于可耕平原、河谷和灌溉区。城市多兼具行政、宗教与手工业功能，规模不如北印度大都会但区域影响力强。
populationDescription.en: The population was predominantly rural, concentrated in arable plains, river valleys, and irrigated zones. Towns combined administrative, religious, and artisanal functions and, while smaller than the great metropolises of northern India, were regionally influential.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与封臣、婆罗门与寺庙阶层、商人行会、工匠、农民、劳役者与边地部族。
socialClasses.en: Royal and feudatory elites, Brahmins and temple establishments, merchant guilds, artisans, peasants, laboring groups, and frontier tribes.

allies.zh: 与部分德干附庸、地方首领及因共同利益而依附王朝的宗教中心保持同盟关系。
allies.en: It maintained alliances with Deccan feudatories, local chiefs, and religious centers attached to the dynasty by shared interests.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手为帕拉瓦王朝及德干地区的潜在竞争者，局部还面临叛乱封臣挑战。
enemies.en: Its main enemies were the Pallava dynasty and other potential Deccan rivals, along with occasional rebellious feudatories.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在保持德干霸权、打击南方强敌并通过征服与赐地网络维持缓冲地带。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve Deccan hegemony, check powerful southern rivals, and maintain buffer zones through conquest and grant networks.

era.zh: 8世纪德干古典国家技术阶段
era.en: 8th-century Deccan classical state technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 石构神庙建筑、灌溉管理、区域碑铭行政、铁器武备和寺庙—王权经济组织。
keyInnovations.en: Stone temple architecture, irrigation management, inscription-based regional administration, iron armaments, and temple-royal economic organization.

strengths.zh: 拥有成熟的王权仪式、坚实农业税基、强大封臣军事网络和显著建筑文化成就。
strengths.en: It possessed mature royal ritual, a solid agrarian tax base, a strong feudatory military network, and notable architectural achievements.

weaknesses.zh: 中央集权有限，对封臣依赖较深，宗教赐地过多也会侵蚀直接财政基础。
weaknesses.en: Centralization was limited, dependence on feudatories was deep, and extensive religious grants could erode the direct fiscal base.

outlook.zh: 在超日王二世时期仍具强势，但中长期将面临德干新兴势力与内部离心的双重挑战。
outlook.en: Under Vikramaditya II it remained strong, but in the medium to long term it faced twin pressures from rising Deccan powers and internal centrifugal forces.

description.zh: 750年前后的遮娄其王朝是德干地区的重要印度王国，以封臣体系、农业税基和寺庙文化维系其强盛。
description.en: Around 750, the Chalukyas were a major Deccan Indian kingdom sustained by feudatory structures, agrarian revenue, and temple-centered culture.

governmentForm.zh: 由可汗统领、依靠部族联盟与军事追随关系维系的草原汗国。
governmentForm.en: A steppe khaganate led by a khagan and held together through tribal confederation and military followership.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以可汗家族、部族首领、骑战贵族、普通牧民与被支配的农耕居民构成。随着定居化加深，潘诺尼亚地区的斯拉夫与其他臣属人口在经济上愈发重要。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the khagan's family, tribal chiefs, mounted warrior elites, ordinary pastoralists, and subjected agricultural populations. As sedentarization deepened, Slavic and other dependent populations in Pannonia became increasingly important economically.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层为阿瓦尔可汗及其近亲、主要部族酋长和骑兵贵族。其权力建立在战利品分配、对附庸的支配和军事威望之上。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the Avar khagan, his close kin, leading tribal chieftains, and cavalry aristocrats. Their authority rested on the distribution of booty, dominance over subject peoples, and military prestige.

succession.zh: 继承通常在可汗家族与精英部族内部解决，并受实力和联盟支持影响。缺乏稳定成文规则，故权力更替常伴随内斗。
succession.en: Succession was generally settled within the khagan's family and elite tribes, influenced by strength and alliance backing. In the absence of stable written rules, transitions often involved internal conflict.

structure.zh: 阿瓦尔汗国的政体较为简约，可汗通过营帐朝廷和巡行首领会议发号施令。对定居臣属地区的控制主要依靠贡赋、驻军点和附庸首领，而非细密文官体系。
structure.en: The Avar polity was relatively simple: the khagan ruled through a tent court and itinerant councils of chiefs. Control over settled subject regions relied mainly on tribute, military posts, and subordinate leaders rather than a dense bureaucracy.

function.zh: 处理部族协调、赏赐分配、外交接待与军事动员。
function.en: Handled tribal coordination, distribution of rewards, diplomatic reception, and military mobilization.

function.zh: 向臣属聚落与附庸部族征收贡物、牲畜与人力，并监督边地关系。
function.en: Collected tribute, livestock, and labor from subordinate settlements and tribes, and supervised frontier relations.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理高度依赖部族首领与臣属社群长老。定居地区通常保留原有村社组织，只需按期纳贡并提供劳役或兵员。
localAdmin.en: Local governance depended heavily on tribal chiefs and elders of subject communities. Settled areas usually retained their existing village structures so long as they paid tribute and supplied labor or men.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以草原习惯法、可汗裁决和部族会议仲裁为主。对臣属农耕人口则往往采取按社群习俗处理、对叛服问题严厉镇压的方式。
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on steppe customary practice, judgments by the khagan, and arbitration in tribal councils. Subject agricultural populations were often left to their own customs, while rebellion and disobedience were punished harshly.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政并非系统性税收国家，而是依靠牲畜、谷物、手工品、战利品和附庸贡赋。对外掠夺衰退后，内地贡纳的重要性明显上升。
taxationSystem.en: The khaganate was not a systematic tax state but relied on livestock, grain, craft goods, booty, and tribute from subordinates. As external raiding declined, internal tribute became markedly more important.

religion.zh: 腾格里信仰
religion.en: Tengrism

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿瓦尔人在喀尔巴阡盆地传播了典型草原金属工艺、马具样式和复合弓骑战文化。其贵族墓葬与装饰品反映出欧亚草原与中欧文化的融合。
culturalAchievements.en: The Avars spread characteristic steppe metalwork, horse gear styles, and composite-bow cavalry culture across the Carpathian Basin. Elite burials and ornamentation reveal a fusion of Eurasian steppe and Central European elements.

languageFamily.zh: 可能为欧亚草原混合语群，行政与臣属环境中并存突厥语、斯拉夫语等多语现象
languageFamily.en: Likely a mixed Eurasian steppe linguistic milieu, with multilingual use including Turkic and Slavic in administrative and subject settings

philosophy.zh: 核心信仰带有腾格里式草原宗教特征，并混合祖先崇拜、萨满实践及与基督教邻邦接触产生的影响。
philosophy.en: Its core belief system had Tengrist steppe features, mixed with ancestor veneration, shamanic practices, and influences from contact with Christian neighbors.

writingSystem.zh: 无广泛制度化本族文字，主要依赖口传传统，并在外交中借用希腊文、拉丁文或周边书写系统。
writingSystem.en: There was no widely institutionalized native script; oral tradition predominated, while Greek, Latin, or neighboring scripts were borrowed for diplomacy.

unit.en: silver solidus equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以畜牧、附庸贡赋和有限农耕产出为基础，已不复早期对拜占庭掠夺与勒索的高收益。其物质基础较脆弱，受草场、战事和臣属忠诚度影响极大。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on pastoralism, tribute from subordinates, and limited agricultural output, no longer enjoying the high returns once gained from raiding and extorting Byzantium. Its material base was fragile and highly sensitive to pasture conditions, warfare, and the loyalty of subject populations.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、贡赋征收、金属手工、马具制作、有限农耕
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, tribute extraction, metal craft, horse-gear production, limited agriculture

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、皮革、奴隶、金属饰品、蜂蜜、蜡、谷物
tradeGoods.en: Horses, leather, slaves, metal ornaments, honey, wax, grain

name.zh: 拜占庭索利都斯与实物贡纳
name.en: Byzantine solidi and payment in kind

householdWealth.zh: 贵族财富主要体现为马群、牲畜、武器和贵金属器物，普通牧户资产有限且波动很大。臣属农民家庭以粮食与劳役维持生计。
householdWealth.en: Elite wealth was expressed mainly through horse herds, livestock, weapons, and precious metal objects, while ordinary pastoral households held limited and highly variable assets. Subject peasant families lived primarily by grain production and labor obligations.

unit.en: silver solidus equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以草原再分配、附庸贡赋和小规模边境交换构成的混合经济，市场化程度较低。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on steppe redistribution, subordinate tribute, and small-scale frontier exchange, with relatively low marketization.

treasuryDescription.zh: 汗庭储备有限，更多依赖临时贡物和贵族私人财富。持续财政赤字反映其衰退期对外掠夺减少而内部维系成本上升。
treasuryDescription.en: Court reserves were limited, with heavier reliance on ad hoc tribute and private elite wealth. The continuing deficit reflects a late phase in which external raiding had fallen while internal maintenance costs rose.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策侧重维持部族忠诚与征收附庸贡纳，而非建设固定税制；在衰退期更趋向加重内部抽取。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on preserving tribal loyalty and extracting subordinate tribute rather than building a fixed tax system; in decline it tended toward heavier internal exaction.

description.zh: 核心作战力量，擅长复合弓骑射、快速突袭与机动作战。
description.en: The core fighting arm, skilled in composite-bow mounted archery, raiding, and mobile warfare.

description.zh: 由臣属斯拉夫等社群提供，主要用于守营、围困和辅助作战。
description.en: Provided by subject communities such as Slavs, they were mainly used for camp defense, sieges, and auxiliary service.

description.zh: 负责保护可汗、维持营帐权威并在关键时刻突击。
description.en: Protected the khagan, upheld authority in the camp, and struck at critical moments.

commanderInChief.zh: 阿瓦尔可汗
commanderInChief.en: Avar Khagan

technology.zh: 军事技术以复合弓、马镫、骑战装备、鳞甲或札甲及营地防御为主。其优势在机动与骑射，但攻坚和长期补给能力相对有限。
technology.en: Military technology centered on the composite bow, stirrups, mounted war gear, scale or lamellar armor, and camp defense. Its advantages lay in mobility and mounted archery, but siegecraft and sustained logistics were more limited.

threats.zh: 主要威胁是法兰克王国扩张、臣属斯拉夫社群离心、内部部族分裂以及资源基础萎缩。
threats.en: Main threats were Frankish expansion, centrifugal tendencies among subject Slavic communities, internal tribal fragmentation, and a shrinking resource base.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀少且成分复杂，游牧阿瓦尔人只是统治少数，广泛臣属人口包括斯拉夫和其他喀尔巴阡盆地居民。定居聚落比真正城市更重要。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse and heterogeneous; the nomadic Avars were only a ruling minority, while broad subject populations included Slavs and other inhabitants of the Carpathian Basin. Settlements were more important than true cities.

socialClasses.zh: 可汗家族、部族贵族、战士、牧民、臣属农民、奴仆与俘虏。
socialClasses.en: Khaganal family, tribal nobility, warriors, pastoralists, subject peasants, servants, and captives.

allies.zh: 可与部分边境部族和机会性附庸结盟，但稳定盟友有限。
allies.en: It could ally with certain frontier tribes and opportunistic dependents, but had few stable allies.

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为法兰克人、反叛臣属社群及周边试图夺取潘诺尼亚控制权的势力。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were the Franks, rebellious subject communities, and neighboring forces seeking control of Pannonia.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持喀尔巴阡盆地支配、平衡周边势力并通过威慑或劫掠获取资源为主，但750年前后已明显转入守势。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at preserving domination over the Carpathian Basin, balancing neighbors, and gaining resources through intimidation or raiding, though by around 750 it had clearly shifted onto the defensive.

era.zh: 8世纪中叶欧亚草原晚期汗国技术阶段
era.en: Mid-8th-century late steppe khaganate technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 复合弓骑兵体系、马具和金属装饰工艺、壕环营地组织，以及草原军事再分配模式。
keyInnovations.en: The composite-bow cavalry system, horse gear and metal ornament craftsmanship, ringwork camp organization, and a steppe military redistribution model.

strengths.zh: 骑兵机动性强，草原军事传统深厚，对臣属社群仍具一定威慑力。
strengths.en: It retained highly mobile cavalry, deep steppe military traditions, and still exercised a degree of deterrence over subject communities.

weaknesses.zh: 财政基础薄弱、城市与文官体系不足、内部整合力下降，且过度依赖部族忠诚与贡赋。
weaknesses.en: Its fiscal base was weak, urban and bureaucratic structures were underdeveloped, internal cohesion was declining, and it depended excessively on tribal loyalty and tribute.

outlook.zh: 前景不佳；若遭遇外部强权持续打击和内部附庸脱离，汗国很可能在数十年内瓦解。
outlook.en: Its outlook was poor; under sustained pressure from external great powers and internal secession, the khaganate was likely to collapse within decades.

description.zh: 750年前后的阿瓦尔汗国是喀尔巴阡盆地中走向衰落的草原政权，仍有强骑兵传统，但财政和统合能力明显减弱。
description.en: Around 750, the Avar Khaganate was a declining steppe power in the Carpathian Basin, still formidable in cavalry warfare but visibly weakening in finance and cohesion.

governmentForm.zh: 以部落联盟为核心的游牧酋邦体制，由若干部族首领在共同军事与迁徙事务上协商决策。
governmentForm.en: A nomadic tribal confederation in which multiple clan chiefs coordinated decisions on war, migration, and alliance.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、家族与部落为基本组织单位，游牧营地构成日常生活核心。上层为战士贵族与首领家族，中层为自由牧民，下层包括依附者、俘虏与奴仆。宗教祭司与萨满在精神生活和部落仪式中具有重要影响。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, kindreds, and tribes, with mobile camps forming the core of daily life. The upper stratum consisted of warrior nobles and chiefly families, the middle of free pastoralists, and the lower of dependents, captives, and servants. Priests and shamans held notable influence in ritual and spiritual affairs.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是各部落首领、军事贵族和显赫氏族长老。其权威来自血缘、战功、牲畜财富与追随者数量。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was composed chiefly of tribal chiefs, military nobles, and elders of prominent clans. Their authority rested on lineage, martial success, livestock wealth, and the number of followers they could command.

succession.zh: 继承多遵循氏族内部的长幼资望与武力支持相结合的原则，并非严格固定。部落联盟层面的领导权常需通过各首领认可与实际实力来确认。
succession.en: Succession generally followed a combination of seniority within leading lineages and practical military backing rather than strict fixed inheritance. Leadership over the wider confederation depended on recognition by other chiefs and on effective personal power.

structure.zh: 部落联盟缺乏常设都城和完备文官机构，政务与军事高度合一。重大事务通常由首领会议、氏族长老和宗教人物共同参与商议。日常管理依靠部落内部的家族网络与随营首领执行。
structure.en: The confederation lacked a fixed capital and a developed bureaucratic apparatus, with governance and military command largely fused. Major matters were commonly discussed among chiefs, clan elders, and religious specialists. Day-to-day administration was carried out through kinship networks and camp-based leaders.

function.zh: 协调部落间迁徙、战争、盟约与争端裁决。
function.en: Coordinated migration, warfare, alliances, and intertribal dispute settlement.

function.zh: 统计牲畜、分配牧场并征集战时贡赋与军需。
function.en: Tracked herds, allocated grazing lands, and gathered wartime tribute and supplies.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以各部落、氏族和营地首领为核心，不设固定州县。司法、征集与迁徙安排多由基层长老和武装首领共同处理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on tribal, clan, and camp leaders rather than fixed provinces or districts. Justice, levy collection, and migration arrangements were handled jointly by elders and armed chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依靠习惯法、血亲赔偿和首领仲裁。盗窃牲畜、违抗盟约和营地内暴力行为会受到赔偿、驱逐或报复性处罚。
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on custom, kin-compensation, and chiefly arbitration. Livestock theft, breach of alliance, and violence within the camp could lead to compensation, expulsion, or retaliatory punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制不规则，主要表现为对附属群体的贡赋、战利品分配和战时征集牲畜。联盟首领对各部的汲取能力取决于威望与军事实力。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was irregular, consisting mainly of tribute from subordinate groups, shares of plunder, and wartime requisitions of livestock. The confederation leader's ability to extract resources depended on prestige and military strength.

religion.zh: 腾格里信仰
religion.en: Tengrism

culturalAchievements.zh: 马扎尔文化在骑射、口传史诗、家族谱系记忆和草原宗教仪式方面表现突出。其游牧生活方式塑造了高度机动的军事文化与灵活的联盟传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Magyar culture was notable for mounted archery, oral epic traditions, genealogical memory, and steppe ritual practices. Their nomadic way of life fostered a highly mobile martial culture and flexible confederative traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 乌拉尔语系乌戈尔语支
languageFamily.en: Uralic, Ugric branch

philosophy.zh: 腾格里信仰、祖先崇拜与草原习惯伦理
philosophy.en: Tengrism, ancestor veneration, and steppe customary ethics

writingSystem.zh: 无广泛固定书写系统，信息传递主要依赖口述传统与记忆
writingSystem.en: No widely standardized writing system; communication relied chiefly on oral tradition and memory

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模较小，以畜牧业和掠夺性收益为主，现金化程度很低。财富主要以马匹、牛羊、皮革和可移动财物衡量，而非城市地产或铸币储备。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was relatively small, based chiefly on pastoralism and predatory gains, with very low monetization. Wealth was measured primarily in horses, cattle, sheep, hides, and movable goods rather than urban property or coined reserves.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、马匹饲养、皮革加工、狩猎、掠夺贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, horse breeding, leatherworking, hunting, and raiding exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、皮毛、牲畜、奴隶、兽皮、骨角制品
tradeGoods.en: Horses, furs, livestock, slaves, hides, and bone-horn goods

unitName.en: head of livestock, silver weight equivalent

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异较大，首领和贵族拥有大规模马群与附从，自由牧民则以中小型牲畜群维生。贫弱家庭在灾年容易依附强宗族或沦为从属者。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply, with chiefs and nobles controlling large horse herds and dependents, while ordinary free pastoralists lived from modest livestock holdings. Poorer families could become dependent on stronger kin groups in bad years.

unit.en: silver dinar equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以游牧自给、部族贡赋和间歇性贸易为核心的草原经济
economicSystem.en: A steppe economy centered on pastoral subsistence, tribal tribute, and intermittent trade

treasuryDescription.zh: 部落联盟没有固定国库，储备多以贵金属、马匹、牲畜和可分配战利品形式分散保存于首领营帐。其流动性高，但在连续灾年或战败后极易缩水。
treasuryDescription.en: The confederation had no fixed state treasury; reserves were held in precious metal, horses, livestock, and distributable booty dispersed among chiefly camps. These assets were mobile, but could shrink quickly after bad seasons or military defeat.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持战士忠诚与联盟凝聚，优先用于馈赠、军需与盟约关系，而非长期公共建设。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining warrior loyalty and confederative cohesion, spending first on gifts, supplies, and alliances rather than long-term public works.

description.zh: 以复合弓和轻装骑兵为主，是部落联盟最核心的野战力量。
description.en: The core field force, centered on composite bows and light cavalry.

description.zh: 由精锐战士和首领随从构成，用于冲击、护卫与关键战斗。
description.en: Elite warriors and chiefly retainers used for shock action, protection, and decisive engagements.

description.zh: 承担守营、押运和近距离作战任务，战场作用次于骑兵。
description.en: Handled camp defense, escort duty, and close combat, secondary to the cavalry.

commanderInChief.zh: 部落联盟最高首领
commanderInChief.en: Supreme confederation chief

technology.zh: 军事技术以草原骑射传统为主，使用复合弓、长矛、短刀、皮甲与马具改良。缺乏成熟攻城器械和固定后勤体系，但机动侦察能力强。
technology.en: Military technology was rooted in steppe mounted warfare, using composite bows, spears, short blades, leather armor, and effective horse gear. The Magyars lacked advanced siege equipment and fixed logistics, but possessed strong mobility and scouting capacity.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近草原部族竞争、可萨势力影响以及迁徙路线上的资源冲突。内部方面，部落离散和首领争权也可能削弱联盟战斗力。
threats.en: Major threats came from rival steppe peoples, Khazar influence, and resource conflicts along migration routes. Internally, tribal fragmentation and chiefly rivalries could also weaken confederation strength.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分散，多随季节迁徙于草原与森林草原地带。定居城市人口极少，绝大多数生活在流动营地与临时聚落中。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse and dispersed, moving seasonally across steppe and forest-steppe zones. Urban settlement was minimal, with most people living in mobile camps and temporary encampments.

socialClasses.zh: 部落首领与战士贵族、自由牧民、依附者与俘虏
socialClasses.en: Tribal chiefs and warrior nobles, free pastoralists, dependents, and captives

allies.zh: 与部分草原部族保持临时盟约，并可能在强邻体系下维持臣属或协从关系。
allies.en: Maintained temporary alliances with some steppe tribes and may at times have operated in subordinate or client relations under stronger neighbors.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象多为竞争牧场的草原部落及试图控制其迁徙与贡赋的区域强权。
enemies.en: Enemies were typically rival steppe groups contesting pastureland and regional powers attempting to control their movement and tribute.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以生存、迁徙安全和部族利益最大化为导向，在臣服、结盟、婚姻和袭掠之间灵活切换。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was driven by survival, migration security, and tribal advantage, shifting pragmatically among submission, alliance, marriage ties, and raiding.

era.zh: 早期中世纪欧亚草原技术阶段
era.en: Early medieval Eurasian steppe technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 复合弓骑射、马具改良、远程侦察与高机动营地组织
keyInnovations.en: Composite-bow mounted warfare, improved horse gear, long-range scouting, and highly mobile camp organization

strengths.zh: 机动性强、骑射娴熟、社会适应草原环境，且首领可迅速集结战士。游牧经济在低人口密度地区具有较高弹性。
strengths.en: They were highly mobile, skilled in mounted archery, and well adapted to steppe conditions, while chiefs could mobilize warriors quickly. Their pastoral economy also offered resilience in low-density environments.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合有限，财政和行政能力薄弱，缺乏攻城与长期占领能力。联盟稳定性过度依赖首领威望和战利品分配。
weaknesses.en: Political integration was limited, and fiscal and administrative capacity remained weak, with little ability for siege warfare or long-term occupation. Confederation stability depended heavily on chiefly prestige and booty distribution.

outlook.zh: 若能在迁徙过程中形成更稳固的联盟核心，马扎尔人有潜力发展为区域性军事力量；若外压增强或内部分裂，则易被更强草原政权支配。
outlook.en: If they formed a more durable confederative core during migration, the Magyars had the potential to become a significant regional military force; if external pressure increased or internal divisions deepened, they could easily fall under stronger steppe powers.

description.zh: 约750年的马扎尔人是一个以骑射游牧为核心、尚处于松散联盟阶段的草原部族共同体。
description.en: Around 750, the Magyars were a steppe tribal community centered on mounted nomadism and still organized as a relatively loose confederation.

dynasty.zh: 阿努拉德普勒王国后期

governmentForm.zh: 以国王为核心的南亚佛教君主制，依托宫廷、地方贵族与寺院网络共同治理。
governmentForm.en: A South Asian Buddhist monarchy centered on the king, supported by the court, provincial elites, and monastic networks.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、贵族、僧团、农民和工匠为主要层次。僧伽罗佛教寺院不仅是宗教中心，也是土地拥有者、教育机构和社会组织节点。乡村人口占绝大多数，依赖灌溉农业与村社协作维持生产。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the royal house, nobles, the monastic order, peasants, and artisans. Buddhist monasteries were not only religious centers but also landholders, educational institutions, and key social nodes. The overwhelming majority lived in villages sustained by irrigation agriculture and communal cooperation.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由国王、王族、宫廷大臣、地方贵族和高级僧侣组成。王权需与寺院势力及地方豪族维持平衡，以确保税收、灌溉与军役体系运转。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the king, royal kin, court ministers, provincial nobles, and senior monks. Royal authority had to remain balanced with monastic institutions and local magnates to keep taxation, irrigation, and military service functioning.

succession.zh: 王位继承以王族血统为原则，但宫廷政变、王室旁支竞争和贵族支持常影响实际结果。佛教僧团虽不直接决定继承，却能通过合法性话语影响政局。
succession.en: Succession was ideally based on royal lineage, but palace coups, collateral dynastic rivalry, and noble backing often shaped the actual outcome. The Buddhist sangha did not directly determine succession, yet could influence legitimacy through religious sanction.

structure.zh: 国家以阿努拉德普勒王都为政治中心，王室通过宫廷官员、地方总督和寺院土地网络管理全国。灌溉工程、佛寺维护与王家土地是统治的重要支柱。相比游牧政体，其行政层级更稳定，但仍带有明显的人身依附与地方化特征。
structure.en: The kingdom was governed from Anuradhapura, where the royal court directed the realm through palace officials, provincial governors, and monastic land networks. Irrigation works, temple patronage, and crown lands formed essential pillars of rule. Compared with nomadic polities, its administrative hierarchy was more stable, though still marked by personal ties and strong localism.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保管王室记录并协调中央政务。
function.en: Drafted royal edicts, maintained records, and coordinated central administration.

function.zh: 征收田赋、管理谷仓、安排劳役与王室土地收入。
function.en: Collected land taxes, managed granaries, and oversaw labor dues and crown-land income.

function.zh: 维护水库、渠道、堤坝和道路等公共设施。
function.en: Maintained reservoirs, canals, embankments, and roads.

function.zh: 统筹王军、守卫都城并组织地方征召。
function.en: Oversaw the royal army, guarded the capital, and organized provincial levies.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依赖区域长官、村社首领与寺院地主共同运作。王权在核心灌溉区控制较强，在边缘地区则更依赖地方豪族和宗教机构协作。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through regional officers, village headmen, and monastic landholders. Royal control was stronger in the core irrigation zones, while peripheral areas depended more on cooperation with local magnates and religious institutions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合王令、习惯法与佛教伦理。土地争议、赋役问题和盗窃案件通常由地方官、长老或寺院权威参与裁断，重大案件上达王廷。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decree, customary law, and Buddhist moral norms. Land disputes, labor obligations, and theft cases were often judged by local officials, elders, or monastic authorities, while major cases went to the royal court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以田赋、贡赋、劳役和贸易税为主，部分收入来自王室直辖土地与港口关税。寺院免税地的存在减少了王室直接征收面，但增强了政治与宗教联盟。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation rested on land revenue, tribute, labor dues, and trade duties, with additional income from crown lands and port tolls. Tax-exempt monastic lands reduced the crown’s direct revenue base but strengthened political and religious alliances.

religion.zh: 上座部佛教
religion.en: Theravada Buddhism

culturalAchievements.zh: 阿努拉德普勒时期以大型佛塔、寺院建筑、石刻铭文和水利工程闻名。佛教教育、巴利学术与王家护持共同推动了岛内高水平的宗教文化发展。
culturalAchievements.en: The Anuradhapura age was renowned for monumental stupas, monastic architecture, stone inscriptions, and hydraulic works. Buddhist education, Pali scholarship, and royal patronage together fostered a high level of religious culture on the island.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系印度-雅利安语族（僧伽罗语）；宗教学术语言为巴利语
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Indo-Aryan branch (Sinhala); Pali as the main liturgical and scholarly language

philosophy.zh: 上座部佛教、王权护法观念与本土习俗传统
philosophy.en: Theravada Buddhism, the ideal of the king as protector of the faith, and local customary traditions

writingSystem.zh: 早期僧伽罗文与巴利文书写并行，铭文与宗教文献发达
writingSystem.en: Early Sinhala writing used alongside Pali, with developed inscriptions and religious literature

gdpDescription.zh: 僧伽罗王国经济以灌溉稻作为核心，并辅以园艺、手工业和海上贸易。相较许多同时代岛屿政权，其水利体系和寺院土地制度使其具备较强的农业剩余生产能力。
gdpDescription.en: The Simhala economy was centered on irrigated rice cultivation, supplemented by horticulture, craft production, and maritime trade. Compared with many contemporary island polities, its hydraulic system and monastic land regime gave it a relatively strong capacity for agricultural surplus.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、稻作、寺院土地经营、宝石采集、金属与陶器手工业、海港贸易
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, rice cultivation, monastic estate management, gem extraction, metal and pottery crafts, and port trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、宝石、珍珠、象、肉桂及香料、象牙、纺织品
tradeGoods.en: Rice, gems, pearls, elephants, cinnamon and spices, ivory, and textiles

householdWealth.zh: 核心灌溉区农户生活相对稳定，拥有水田、牛畜和村社保障。贵族与寺院掌握更大土地和劳役资源，而边缘地区与无地者财富明显较低。
householdWealth.en: Households in the core irrigation zones enjoyed relatively stable livelihoods based on paddy fields, cattle, and village support. Nobles and monasteries controlled larger estates and labor resources, while peripheral populations and the land-poor were markedly less wealthy.

unit.en: silver dinar equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室与寺院土地、村社农业和印度洋海贸相结合的农业君主制经济
economicSystem.en: An agrarian monarchical economy combining royal and monastic estates, village agriculture, and Indian Ocean trade

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由贵金属、谷物储备、王室器物和可征调物资共同构成。由于王国需持续维护水利与寺院网络，财政储备虽可观，但并不完全闲置。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metal, grain reserves, royal valuables, and requisitionable supplies. Because the kingdom had to maintain both hydraulic works and monastic patronage, these reserves were substantial but not fully disposable.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重视维持灌溉体系、都城秩序和佛教护持，通过农业剩余与港口贸易共同支撑王权。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized sustaining irrigation, order in the capital, and support for Buddhism, using both agrarian surplus and port trade to uphold royal authority.

description.zh: 为王国主力，负责守城、野战和维持核心地区秩序。
description.en: The kingdom’s main force, used for garrison duty, field operations, and maintaining order in the core regions.

description.zh: 用于震慑、冲击和王室护卫，是王权威仪的重要象征。
description.en: Used for intimidation, shock action, and royal protection, they were also a key symbol of kingship.

description.zh: 负责沿海巡防、港口保护和海上交通安全。
description.en: Protected coasts, guarded ports, and secured maritime communications.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王兼最高统帅
commanderInChief.en: The king as commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制兵器、弓矛、城防工事和战象运用为特色。海上力量主要限于近海巡逻与运输，缺乏大规模远洋海军作战能力。
technology.en: Military technology featured iron weapons, bows and spears, fortified defenses, and the use of war elephants. Naval strength was largely confined to coastal patrol and transport rather than large-scale blue-water warfare.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南印度诸政权的跨海干预、宫廷内斗和地方叛乱。若灌溉核心区受破坏，王国军政体系将同时承压。
threats.en: Major threats came from South Indian intervention across the sea, palace struggles, and local rebellions. If the core irrigation zones were disrupted, both the kingdom’s military and administrative systems would come under severe strain.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于北中部灌溉农业区与王都周边，乡村社会占绝对主体。都城与宗教中心吸纳了较高比例的工匠、僧侣和服务人口。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the north-central irrigation zones and around the royal capital, with village society forming the overwhelming majority. The capital and religious centers attracted a relatively high share of artisans, monks, and service populations.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族、僧团、高级官员、自由农民、工匠与商人、劳役人口与边缘部族
socialClasses.en: Royal kin and nobles, the monastic order, senior officials, free peasants, artisans and merchants, laboring populations, and marginal tribal groups

allies.zh: 与部分南印度商人集团、佛教僧团网络及印度洋贸易伙伴维持友好往来。
allies.en: Maintained amicable relations with some South Indian merchant groups, Buddhist monastic networks, and Indian Ocean trading partners.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌手主要为南印度泰米尔诸政权及岛内争位势力。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included South Indian Tamil polities and internal claimants to the throne.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策重视海上贸易安全、佛教声望和对南印度势力的防范，常通过贸易、婚姻和宗教联系稳固外部关系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized maritime trade security, Buddhist prestige, and vigilance toward South Indian powers, often using commerce, marriage ties, and religious links to stabilize external relations.

era.zh: 南亚早期中世纪灌溉国家技术阶段
era.en: Early medieval South Asian hydraulic-state technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 大型蓄水池与渠道系统、佛塔与石构建筑、金属工艺、巴利文献与铭文记录
keyInnovations.en: Large reservoirs and canal systems, stupa and stone construction, metalworking, and Pali literary-inscriptional recordkeeping

strengths.zh: 水利农业发达、王都与寺院体系成熟、海贸条件优越，并具有较强的宗教合法性。王国在核心地区的组织能力明显强于多数周边小邦。
strengths.en: It possessed advanced hydraulic agriculture, a mature capital-monastic system, advantageous maritime trade conditions, and strong religious legitimacy. In its core regions, the kingdom’s organizational capacity surpassed that of many neighboring smaller polities.

weaknesses.zh: 政治稳定易受宫廷斗争和南印度干预影响，且灌溉体系维护成本高。寺院免税地扩大虽有助于合法性，却会压缩王室可直接动员资源。
weaknesses.en: Political stability was vulnerable to court conflict and South Indian intervention, while the irrigation system required costly upkeep. The growth of tax-exempt monastic lands enhanced legitimacy but reduced the crown’s directly mobilizable resources.

outlook.zh: 若王权能维持灌溉系统和王室—寺院—地方豪族之间的平衡，僧伽罗王国仍可保持稳定与繁荣；若海贸受阻或内战加剧，则都城体制将逐渐承压。
outlook.en: If royal power could preserve the irrigation system and the balance among crown, monasteries, and local elites, the kingdom could remain stable and prosperous; if maritime trade faltered or civil conflict worsened, the capital-centered order would come under mounting strain.

description.zh: 约750年的僧伽罗王国是一个以阿努拉德普勒为中心、依靠佛教王权与灌溉农业支撑的成熟岛屿国家。
description.en: Around 750, Simhala was a mature island kingdom centered on Anuradhapura, sustained by Buddhist kingship and irrigated agriculture.

governmentForm.zh: 以小型游群和部落为单位的分散式长老协商治理，没有统一王权或常设国家机构。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized system of small bands and tribes governed by elder consensus, without a unified kingship or permanent state institutions.

socialStructure.zh: 社会规模普遍较小，以亲族关系和婚姻网络维系群体稳定。多数地区由采猎者与部分牧养群体构成，强调共享、互助与移动性。社会分层较弱，但经验丰富的猎手、长老和仪式专家拥有较高声望。
socialStructure.en: Communities were generally small, held together by kinship and marriage networks. Most groups consisted of foragers, with some pastoral communities in certain areas, valuing sharing, mutual aid, and mobility. Social stratification was limited, though experienced hunters, elders, and ritual specialists enjoyed higher prestige.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在严格意义上的统治阶级，权威主要来自年长者、能干猎手和调解者的个人声望。部分牧养群体的首领在牲畜分配和对外交涉中影响更大。
rulingClass.en: There was no formal ruling class; authority derived mainly from the personal prestige of elders, skilled hunters, and mediators. In some pastoral communities, headmen had greater influence over livestock distribution and external relations.

succession.zh: 领导地位通常不通过固定王位继承，而由亲族认可、年龄资望和实际能力决定。若一位首领失去支持，群体成员往往可以迁离另立营地。
succession.en: Leadership did not usually follow fixed dynastic succession, but depended on kin approval, seniority, and practical ability. If a leader lost support, group members could often simply move away and form or join another camp.

structure.zh: 科伊桑诸群体没有统一中央政府，决策在营地或部落层面通过讨论与共识形成。政治与日常生计、婚姻安排、狩猎协作及仪式活动彼此交织。行政功能极少，更多体现为社会协调而非强制统治。
structure.en: The Khoisan peoples had no unified central government; decisions were made at the camp or tribal level through discussion and consensus. Politics was deeply intertwined with subsistence, marriage arrangements, hunting cooperation, and ritual life. Administrative functions were minimal and focused more on social coordination than coercive rule.

function.zh: 协调迁徙、婚姻、争端调解和对外接触。
function.en: Coordinated movement, marriage arrangements, dispute mediation, and external contact.

function.zh: 安排狩猎合作、采集区域与关键水源使用规则。
function.en: Organized hunting cooperation, gathering zones, and rules for the use of critical water sources.

localAdmin.zh: 地方即各营地与游群本身，不存在高于部落的稳固行政区。资源稀缺地区对水源和季节性路线的协调尤为重要。
localAdmin.en: Local administration consisted of the camps and bands themselves, with no durable administrative unit above the tribe or band. In resource-scarce areas, coordination over water and seasonal routes was especially important.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯与共识，重视调解、赔偿和群体和解。严重冲突若无法化解，常以分营迁徙而非持续强制惩罚收场。
legalSystem.en: Law was grounded in custom and consensus, emphasizing mediation, compensation, and communal reconciliation. When severe disputes could not be settled, they often ended in fission and relocation rather than sustained coercive punishment.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税制，公共负担主要通过共享食物、互赠物品和共同劳动承担。个别牧养群体可能向附属家庭收取牲畜性贡献，但规模很小。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; communal burdens were met through food sharing, gift exchange, and collective labor. Some pastoral groups may have received small livestock contributions from dependent households, but on a very limited scale.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 科伊桑文化以精细的岩画传统、复杂的口述知识、追踪狩猎技术和仪式舞蹈著称。其对动物行为、植物资源和干旱环境的认知极为深厚。
culturalAchievements.en: Khoisan culture was distinguished by refined rock art traditions, complex oral knowledge, tracking-based hunting techniques, and ritual dance. Their understanding of animal behavior, plant resources, and arid environments was exceptionally deep.

languageFamily.zh: 科伊桑诸语言群，含多种带搭嘴音语言
languageFamily.en: Khoisan language groupings, including multiple click languages

philosophy.zh: 泛灵信仰、祖灵观念、与自然环境密切相连的仪式世界观
philosophy.en: Animism, ancestral spirit beliefs, and a ritual worldview closely tied to the natural environment

writingSystem.zh: 无本土书写系统，知识依靠口述、歌舞、岩画与仪式传承
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, song, dance, rock art, and ritual

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、采集和局部牧养为主，几乎不具备国家财政意义上的剩余。物质生产总量不高，但对生态环境的适应使其在低人口密度地区能够长期维持生计。
gdpDescription.en: The economy relied on hunting, gathering, and localized pastoralism, producing little surplus in the sense of state finance. Total material output was modest, but ecological adaptation allowed long-term subsistence in sparsely populated landscapes.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、局部牧养、皮革制作、骨木石器加工
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, localized pastoralism, hide preparation, and bone-wood-stone toolmaking

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、鸵鸟蛋壳珠、猎产品、牲畜、药用植物
tradeGoods.en: Hides, ostrich eggshell beads, hunted products, livestock, and medicinal plants

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富有限，主要体现在工具、装饰品、食物储备与亲族互助网络。少数牧养群体因拥有牲畜而更富裕，但整体贫富差距不大。
householdWealth.en: Most households possessed limited wealth, mainly in tools, ornaments, food stores, and kin support networks. Some pastoral groups were wealthier because they held livestock, but overall inequality remained modest.

unit.en: silver dinar equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以共享和互惠为核心的采猎经济，部分地区夹杂小规模牧养交换
economicSystem.en: A foraging economy centered on sharing and reciprocity, with small-scale pastoral exchange in some regions

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在固定国库，所谓储备主要是干肉、皮张、珠饰、工具和少量牲畜。财富高度分散并随营地移动，不具备集中财政动员能力。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no fixed treasury; reserves consisted mainly of dried food, hides, beads, tools, and small livestock holdings. Wealth was highly dispersed and moved with the camps, offering little capacity for centralized fiscal mobilization.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 其“财政”本质上是共享与互惠安排，目标在于保证群体生存而非积累国家财富。
fiscalPolicy.en: Its 'fiscal policy' was essentially a system of sharing and reciprocity aimed at collective survival rather than the accumulation of state wealth.

description.zh: 以弓箭、投矛和追踪技能为主，擅长小规模袭扰与自卫。
description.en: Used bows, throwing spears, and tracking skills, excelling in small-scale raiding and self-defense.

description.zh: 由成年成员临时组成，负责保护家属、水源和牲畜。
description.en: Temporarily formed from adult community members to protect families, water sources, and livestock.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各群长老和资深猎手分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; each band was led by its own elders and senior hunters

technology.zh: 军事技术接近狩猎技术，依赖小弓、毒箭、投掷武器和环境知识。缺乏金属甲胄、攻城器械和持续集结能力，但在追踪和生存方面极具优势。
technology.en: Military technology closely resembled hunting technology, relying on small bows, poison arrows, thrown weapons, and environmental knowledge. The Khoisan lacked metal armor, siege tools, and sustained mobilization capacity, but possessed major advantages in tracking and survival.

threats.zh: 威胁主要来自邻近牧养群体争夺水源、猎场和牲畜，以及更大规模外来人口的逐步扩张。环境波动与干旱对其生存压力往往大于正规战争。
threats.en: Threats came mainly from neighboring pastoral groups competing over water, hunting grounds, and livestock, as well as the gradual expansion of larger incoming populations. Environmental fluctuation and drought often posed a greater danger than formal warfare.

populationDescription.zh: 人口密度极低，广泛分布于南部非洲草原、干旱区与半干旱地带。绝大多数人居于小型流动营地，仅有少数季节性较稳定的聚居点。
populationDescription.en: Population density was extremely low, spread across southern African grasslands, arid zones, and semi-arid areas. Most people lived in small mobile camps, with only a few more seasonally stable settlements.

name.zh: 卡拉哈里北部季节营地

socialClasses.zh: 长老与仪式专家、成年猎手与牧人、普通家庭成员、依附亲属
socialClasses.en: Elders and ritual specialists, adult hunters and herders, ordinary family members, and dependent kin

allies.zh: 同语群或通婚群体之间常保持互助关系，并与邻近部族开展小规模交换。
allies.en: Mutual support often existed among related or intermarrying groups, alongside small-scale exchange with neighboring peoples.

enemies.zh: 敌对关系通常是局部性的，多围绕水源、牲畜、婚姻争端和狩猎地竞争展开。
enemies.en: Hostility was usually local, arising over water access, livestock, marriage disputes, and competition for hunting grounds.

foreignPolicy.zh: 没有统一对外政策，群体行为以避冲突、灵活迁徙、互赠结盟和局部报复为主。
foreignPolicy.en: There was no unified foreign policy; group behavior emphasized conflict avoidance, flexible movement, gift-based alliances, and localized retaliation.

era.zh: 南部非洲采猎社会早期中世纪技术阶段
era.en: Early medieval technological stage of southern African foraging societies

keyInnovations.zh: 毒箭狩猎、精细追踪、鸵鸟蛋壳珠制作、岩画与环境适应知识
keyInnovations.en: Poison-arrow hunting, advanced tracking, ostrich eggshell bead production, rock art, and environmental adaptation knowledge

strengths.zh: 对环境适应力极强，社会弹性高，内部分享机制有助于在资源不稳条件下生存。追踪、狩猎与地形认知使其在局部冲突中具有隐蔽与机动优势。
strengths.en: They were highly adapted to their environment, socially flexible, and supported by sharing systems that aided survival under unstable resources. Their tracking, hunting, and terrain knowledge gave them stealth and mobility advantages in local conflict.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、组织分散、缺乏集中防御和长期储备能力，面对大规模牧养或农耕扩张时处于劣势。无书写与无国家机构也限制了跨区域协调。
weaknesses.en: Their small population, dispersed organization, and lack of centralized defense or long-term reserves put them at a disadvantage against large pastoral or agricultural expansions. The absence of writing and state institutions also limited interregional coordination.

outlook.zh: 在生态相对稳定且外来压力有限时，科伊桑群体能够长期维持独特生活方式；但随着更强势牧养和农耕社会扩张，其生存空间将逐步收缩。
outlook.en: Under relatively stable ecological conditions and limited outside pressure, Khoisan groups could sustain their distinctive way of life for long periods; however, as stronger pastoral and agricultural societies expanded, their living space would gradually contract.

description.zh: 约750年的科伊桑诸群体是以采猎和局部牧养为基础、政治高度分散而生态适应力极强的南部非洲原住民社会。
description.en: Around 750, the Khoisan peoples were politically decentralized southern African indigenous societies based on foraging and limited pastoralism, with exceptional ecological adaptability.

governmentForm.zh: 以岛屿与村社为基础的酋邦和部族联合体，由地方酋长在礼仪威望与亲属网络支持下治理。
governmentForm.en: A network of island-based chiefdoms and tribal communities governed by local chiefs whose authority rested on ritual prestige and kinship ties.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、村落与岛屿群体为核心，内部通常分为酋长家族、祭仪长者、普通农渔民与依附者。较大的岛屿上已有较明显的等级差异，而小岛社群则更接近平衡的村社组织。跨岛婚姻、交换与仪式往来维系了区域认同。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on kin groups, villages, and island communities, often divided into chiefly families, ritual elders, common farming-fishing households, and dependents. Larger islands showed clearer social ranking, while smaller communities remained more egalitarian. Inter-island marriage, exchange, and ceremonial contact helped sustain regional identity.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由世袭酋长家族与掌握祭仪知识的长者组成。其权威来自祖先传统、馈赠能力以及对土地与劳动力的协调。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted chiefly of hereditary chiefly lineages and elders who controlled ritual knowledge. Their authority came from ancestral tradition, gift-giving capacity, and their ability to coordinate land and labor.

succession.zh: 继承多依血缘与母系或双系亲属规则决定，不同岛屿做法不尽相同。若继承人年幼或威望不足，长老会议可推举旁支亲属暂代。
succession.en: Succession was generally determined by kinship, often through matrilineal or bilateral rules, though practice varied by island. If an heir was too young or lacked prestige, a council of elders could elevate a collateral relative as caretaker.

structure.zh: 政治结构松散，通常由村社首领、岛屿酋长与长老共同议事。较大岛屿可能形成若干从属村社向核心酋长进贡的体系，但并无统一国家机器。
structure.en: The political structure was loose, with village headmen, island chiefs, and elders making decisions collectively. On larger islands, several subordinate communities might render tribute to a paramount chief, but there was no unified state apparatus.

function.zh: 协调岛内分配、仪式、纠纷调解与对外往来。
function.en: Coordinated distribution, ritual affairs, dispute settlement, and external relations.

function.zh: 管理木薯、鱼干、棉布及礼物储备，筹办集会与赈济。
function.en: Managed stores of cassava, dried fish, cotton cloth, and gift goods for feasts and relief.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由村社首领和家族长者执行。不同聚落在耕地分配、捕鱼区使用和婚姻纠纷上享有较大自治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out mainly by village leaders and lineage elders. Settlements retained broad autonomy over land allocation, fishing zones, and marriage disputes.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、祖先规范与公开调解为基础。严重冲突常以赔礼、交换、迁居或仪式和解处理。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, ancestral norms, and public mediation. Serious conflicts were often resolved through compensation, exchange, relocation, or ritual reconciliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 并无固定税制，主要通过贡礼、劳役与集体宴飨时的物资征集来实现资源集中。酋长对再分配的能力比征税权更重要。
taxationSystem.en: There was no regular tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, labor obligations, and contributions for communal feasts. A chief's redistributive power mattered more than formal taxation.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长独木舟航行、岛际交换与适应海岛环境的园圃农业。陶器、编织品、身体装饰和仪式舞蹈具有鲜明地区风格。
culturalAchievements.en: They excelled in canoe travel, inter-island exchange, and garden agriculture adapted to island environments. Pottery, woven goods, body ornament, and ceremonial dance displayed distinctive regional styles.

languageFamily.zh: 以阿拉瓦克语系为主，部分地区有加勒比语族成分
languageFamily.en: Primarily Arawakan languages, with Cariban elements in some areas

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵、祖灵崇敬与自然循环观念构成核心世界观。
philosophy.en: Animism, ancestor reverence, and a worldview centered on natural cycles formed the cultural core.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识主要通过口述传统、歌谣、图案与仪式记忆传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, designs, and ritual memory.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性木薯农业、渔业、采集和有限的岛际交换为主。市场化程度很低，但礼物交换网络在资源调剂中作用显著。
gdpDescription.en: The economy relied on subsistence cassava cultivation, fishing, foraging, and limited inter-island exchange. Monetization was negligible, but gift-exchange networks played an important role in balancing resources.

mainIndustries.zh: 木薯种植、渔业、独木舟制造、陶器、编织
mainIndustries.en: Cassava cultivation, fishing, canoe building, pottery, weaving

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、贝饰、陶器、鱼干、木薯饼、石斧、羽饰
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, shell ornaments, pottery, dried fish, cassava bread, stone axes, feather ornaments

name.zh: 无铸币，礼物与实物交换
name.en: No coinage, gift and barter exchange

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在独木舟、园圃、渔具、棉织品与亲属互助网络上。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was embodied mainly in canoes, garden plots, fishing gear, cotton textiles, and kin-based support networks.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、酋长再分配和跨岛礼物交换并存的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy combining household production, chiefly redistribution, and inter-island gift exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
